### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

When using `openclaw tui` to connect to the gateway, the assistant's replies are not shown in the terminal output, even when using the `--deliver` flag. The TUI shows "gateway connected | idle" but the actual responses from the AI are missing.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run `openclaw tui` or `openclaw tui --deliver` in terminal
2. Send a message to the assistant (e.g., "who are you")
3. The assistant processes the message (visible in other clients like webchat at http://127.0.0.1:18789/)
4. But the terminal does not display the assistant's reply

### Expected behavior

The assistant's reply should be displayed in the TUI terminal when `--deliver` is used.

### Actual behavior

but --deliver is still unused

### OpenClaw version

2026.03.02

### Operating system

macos 14.8

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

_No response_

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Azure OpenAI provider does not appear in the onboarding model/auth provider list even after switching to the mani/7feb branch that supposedly adds Azure support.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Clone the forked repository: https://github.com/skyhawk14/openclaw
2. Checkout the branch `mani/7feb`
3. Install dependencies using `pnpm install`
4. Run onboarding using `pnpm openclaw onboard`
5. Follow the onboarding wizard

Result: The provider list does not show Azure OpenAI.

### Expected behavior

The onboarding wizard should display "Azure OpenAI" as a selectable model/auth provider.

### Actual behavior

The onboarding wizard skips the provider selection step and proceeds directly to channel configuration.

Additionally, the system shows messages such as:
"No auth configured for provider anthropic"
even though Azure OpenAI support is expected to be available.

### OpenClaw version

Commit: f3db0b4 (mani/7feb branch)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 22.04 (AWS EC2 instance)

### Install method

Manual install from source || git clone || git checkout mani/7feb || pnpm install || pnpm openclaw onboard ||

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
During onboarding the wizard skips the provider selection and directly shows:

Model check
No auth configured for provider "anthropic"

The provider list does not include Azure OpenAI.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Users trying to configure Azure OpenAI provider

Severity: Medium–High

Frequency: 100% reproducible

Consequence: Azure OpenAI cannot be configured through onboarding even though the branch indicates Azure support was added.

### Additional information

The branch `mani/7feb` includes commits mentioning Azure OpenAI support and fixes.

Example commit:
45552b574 add azure openai support while onboarding

However the onboarding wizard does not expose Azure OpenAI as a selectable provider.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: Azure OpenAI provider not appearing during onboarding

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

OpenClaw v2026.3.2 sends parallel_tool_calls: true in API requests to all OpenAI-compatible providers, including models that don't support parallel tool calling (e.g. moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 on NVIDIA NIM). This causes a 400 error from the provider and breaks ALL tool execution for the agent.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure a custom provider using NVIDIA NIM with moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 (or any model that doesn't support parallel tool calls)
2. 2. Send any message that triggers tool use
3. 3. Agent attempts parallel tool calls
4. 4. Provider returns: 400 This model only supports single tool-calls at once!
5. 5. All tools become unavailable - exec, read, write, browser, etc.
6. 6. Agent reports "Tool not found" for any subsequent tool call attempts

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw should either:
1. Expose a parallelToolCalls: false config option per model/provider (currently returns Unrecognized key).
2. 2. Auto-detect the 400 response and retry with single tool calls.
3. 3. Not force parallel_tool_calls: true for custom openai-completions providers.

### Actual behavior

Provider returns 400 This model only supports single tool-calls at once! and all tool execution breaks.

Error Log:
embedded run agent end: runId=0244ece4 isError=true error=400 This model only supports single tool-calls at once!
lane task error: lane=session:agent:main:main durationMs=600123 error="FailoverError: LLM request timed out."

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

Linux (Ubuntu)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Attempted Workarounds (none work):
- "parallelToolCalls": false in model config -> Unrecognized key
- "requestParams": {"parallel_tool_calls": false} in provider -> Unrecognized key

Working Workaround:
Downgrade to 2026.2.25

Environment details:
- Node: 22.22.0
- Provider: NVIDIA NIM (openai-completions API)
- Model: moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All users using providers that don't support parallel tool calls (like Kimi on NVIDIA NIM).
Severity: High (breaks ALL tool execution, making the agent useless).
Frequency: 100% repro on affected models.
Consequence: Agent becomes unable to use any tools after the first error.

### Additional information

Last known good version: 2026.2.25
First known bad version: 2026.3.2

comments.body:
I put together a small failure-class regression harness for this issue:
https://github.com/aashmawy/openclaw-issue-37048-harness

What it models:
- parallel_tool_calls: true forced on a provider/model path that only supports single tool calls, producing the 400 class described here.
- A follow-on “tool not found” cascade path after that failure.

Notes:
- This is a synthetic/deterministic harness for reproducible CI checks (record/run/repro/shrink), not a fix.
- No API keys are required to run it.
- The goal is to make this regression class easy to reproduce and gate in automation.

## Root Cause Analysis

**Problem**: OpenClaw v2026.3.2 sends `parallel_tool_calls: true` to all OpenAI-compatible providers, breaking models that don't support it (e.g., Kimi on NVIDIA NIM).

**Where**: The parameter is likely set in `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core` (v0.55.3). No `parallel_tool_calls` references found in OpenClaw codebase.

**Impact**: 
- Severity: **High** - ALL tools become unavailable
- Scope: All models not supporting parallel tool calls
- Frequency: 100% repro

## Proposed Solutions

### Option 1: Add Config Override (Recommended)
Add `parallelToolCalls` to provider/model config:

```json
{
  "models": {
    "providers": {
      "nvidia-nim": {
        "parallelToolCalls": false
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**Files to modify**:
- `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` - Add schema
- `src/agents/models-config.ts` - Add resolution logic
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/model.ts` - Pass to runner

### Option 2: Auto-Detect 400 Errors
Detect `400 This model only supports single tool-calls at once!` and retry with single calls.

### Option 3: Smart Defaults
Maintain known non-supporting models list (Kimi, etc.).

## Questions for Maintainer

1. Does `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core` expose a way to control `parallel_tool_calls`?
2. Should this be per-provider, per-model, or both?
3. Should we implement auto-retry on 400 errors?

## Recommendation

Implement **Option 1 + Option 3**: Config override with smart defaults.

**Priority**: High | **Complexity**: Medium | **Risk**: Low (backward compatible)

Happy to implement if maintainer confirms the approach! 🚀

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: [Bug] v2026.3.2 sends parallel_tool_calls to models that don't support it (breaks all tools)

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

OpenClaw on Azure Ubuntu VM receives inbound messages (Web UI + Telegram), runs agent turns successfully, but produces no user-visible reply.

Runs finish with `status: ok` but output is empty:
- `result.payloads: []`
- assistant `content: []`
- usage fields all zero
- gateway/journal repeatedly logs `No reply from agent.`

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start with OpenClaw `2026.3.2` and configure openai api compatible custom provider (I am using `https://llm-api-jpe.dou.chat`) in config.
2. Start gateway:
```bash
openclaw gateway --port 18789
```
3. Create a fresh local session and run:
```bash
openclaw agent --to +15555550123 --message "Reply exactly BUG_REPORT_TEST" --json
```
(Any E.164 dummy target works to create a session key.)
4. If it asks routing/session selection, run once with explicit session from `sessions.json`:
```bash
cat ~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
openclaw agent --session-id <new_session_id> --message "Reply exactly BUG_REPORT_TEST" --json
```
5. Failure signature:
- `status: "ok"` but `result.payloads: []`
- usage zeros
- logs show `No reply from agent.`

This issue happens on newly created session as well; not tied to a specific existing session ID.

### Expected behavior

Inbound DM/webchat message should generate assistant payload and deliver reply.


### Actual behavior

Agent run completes but no outbound payload is produced; no reply delivered.


### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
#### 1) Gateway journal: agent RPC success + no reply
From `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway.service`:

2026-03-06T01:07:55.586Z [ws] ⇄ res ✓ agent 59ms runId=4fddd6f8-b843-489e-8733-8ddcd05a8b4f ...
2026-03-06T01:07:56.363+00:00 No reply from agent.
2026-03-06T01:07:56.661+00:00 No reply from agent.
2026-03-06T01:08:09.653+00:00 No reply from agent.
...
2026-03-06T02:11:32.253+00:00 No reply from agent.


#### 2) Session JSONL: assistant content is empty
From [42469e34-dd51-4fe2-9c20-348884387204.jsonl](/home/azureuser/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/42469e34-dd51-4fe2-9c20-348884387204.jsonl):

{"type":"message","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[Fri 2026-03-06 02:11 UTC] Reply exactly NO_TOOLS_SURFACE_OK"}]}}
{"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[],"api":"openai-completions","provider":"custom-llm-api-jpe-dou-chat","model":"deepseek/deepseek-v3.2","usage":{"input":0,"output":0,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"totalTokens":0,"cost":{"input":0,"output":0,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"total":0}},"stopReason":"stop"}}

{"type":"message","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[Fri 2026-03-06 02:21 UTC] Reply exactly BUG_REPORT_TEST"}]}}
{"type":"message","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[],"api":"openai-completions","provider":"custom-llm-api-jpe-dou-chat","model":"deepseek/deepseek-v3.2","usage":{"input":0,"output":0,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"totalTokens":0,"cost":{"input":0,"output":0,"cacheRead":0,"cacheWrite":0,"total":0}},"stopReason":"stop"}}


Same pattern appears in webchat session:
- [b3f3555e-5fef-45cf-bf75-44587e6d7bfa.jsonl](/home/azureuser/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/b3f3555e-5fef-45cf-bf75-44587e6d7bfa.jsonl)

#### 3) OpenClaw file log
From [openclaw-2026-03-06.log](/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-03-06.log):

... "No reply from agent." ... date":"2026-03-06T01:07:56.363Z" ...
... "No reply from agent." ... date":"2026-03-06T01:07:56.662Z" ...
... "No reply from agent." ... date":"2026-03-06T01:08:09.653Z" ...
... "No reply from agent." ... date":"2026-03-06T02:21:27.900Z" ...
```

### Impact and severity

- **Affected users/systems/channels**
  - Any OpenClaw deployment on `2026.3.2` using a custom `openai-completions` provider path can be affected.
  - Reproduced on both **Web UI direct chat** and **Telegram DM** channels.
  - Affects bot owners during initial setup/onboarding and normal daily use.

- **Severity**
  - **High / workflow-blocking**.
  - Core assistant function fails silently: turns complete but no reply is delivered.
  - Not a direct data-loss issue, but it behaves like a functional outage for messaging.

- **Frequency**
  - **Consistent (effectively always)** in the affected setup.
  - Reproduced across many consecutive runs, different prompts, and multiple sessions/channels.

- **Practical consequence**
  - Messages are received but users get no response, causing **missed conversations** and **failed onboarding** (appears “online” but unusable).
  - Generates repeated failed turns and troubleshooting overhead.
  - Potential extra model/runtime cost from retries and debugging attempts with no successful user output.

### What Was Tried

1. Verified gateway health/auth/tunnel
- Gateway status healthy, loopback RPC probe OK.

2. Reproduced across channels/sessions
- `agent:main:main` and Telegram session both fail the same way.

3. Model A/B in OpenClaw
- `deepseek/deepseek-v3.2` and `openai/gpt-4.1-mini` both still yielded empty payloads.

4. Tool/MCP-related mitigation attempt
- Set `commands.native=false` and `commands.nativeSkills=false`, retested.
- No change; still empty reply.
- Restored:
  - `commands.native=auto`
  - `commands.nativeSkills=auto`

5. Upstream control tests
- Direct calls to provider return normal text.
- Replay of captured OpenClaw payload directly to upstream returns non-empty text.

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

FAQ page contains a broken cross-document anchor link to a non-existent troubleshooting section about Xfinity/SSL errors.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/faq#i-cant-access-docsopenclawai-ssl-error-what-now
2. Click the "Troubleshooting" link in that section
3. Observe you land on /help/troubleshooting with no matching anchor

### Expected behavior

The link navigates to a relevant troubleshooting section with additional Xfinity SSL details.

### Actual behavior

The link targets /help/troubleshooting#docsopenclawai-shows-an-ssl-error-comcastxfinity, but that anchor does not exist in troubleshooting.md. 
The page has only these sections: "First 60 seconds", "Anthropic long context 429", "Plugin install fails with missing openclaw extensions", and "Decision tree".

The link targets /help/troubleshooting#docsopenclawai-shows-an-ssl-error-comcastxfinity, but that anchor does not exist in troubleshooting.md. 

The page has only these
- sections: "First 60 seconds", "Anthropic long context 429", "Plugin install fails with missing openclaw extensions", and "Decision tree". On GitHub's file viewer,
- clicking the link shows: "Error loading page — An unexpected error occured. Try reloading the page."

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3

### Operating system

macOS 15.4

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Source: docs/help/faq.md line 452
Broken link: [Troubleshooting](/help/troubleshooting#docsopenclawai-shows-an-ssl-error-comcastxfinity)

grep result confirming anchor does not exist in target file:
$ grep -i "ssl\|xfinity\|comcast" docs/help/troubleshooting.md
(no matches)

When clicking the link from the GitHub repo file view:
"Error loading page — An unexpected error occured. Try reloading the page."
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Any user experiencing Comcast/Xfinity SSL blocks who follows the FAQ link
Severity: Low (docs-only, no runtime impact)
Frequency: 100% — the anchor simply does not exist
Consequence: Users land on the troubleshooting page with no scroll to relevant content; may assume the info is missing

### Additional information

The FAQ section itself (line 448) already contains the full workaround (disable Xfinity Advanced Security or allowlist docs.openclaw.ai), so the cross-reference is redundant. 
Removing the broken link is the simplest fix. I'll submit a PR shortly.

comments.body: Fixed in PR #36981: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36981
Opened PR #37137 to fix the broken FAQ TOC anchor by linking to /help/troubleshooting without the non-existent fragment.\n\nCloses #36970

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

   ## Description                                                                         
   TUI connected to gateway but not receiving agent messages in real-time. Messages only  
 appear after restarting TUI.                                                             
                                                                                          
   ## Environment                                                                         
   - OpenClaw: 2026.3.3                                                                   
   - VPS: running gateway in loopback mode (127.0.0.1:18789)                              
   - TUI: openclaw-tui (gateway-client)                                                   
                                                                                          
   ## Reproduction                                                                        
   1. Connect TUI to gateway                                                              
   2. Send message to agent                                                               
   3. Agent responds (visible in session transcript)                                      
   4. Response not shown in TUI until restart                                             
                                                                                          
   ## Logs                                                                                
   Web heartbeat shows `messagesHandled: 0`:                                              
 ```                                                                                      
                                                                                          
 {"module":"web-heartbeat"}..."me ssagesHandled":0,"lastMessageAt" :null                  
                                                                                          
 ```                                                                                      
                                                                                          
   ## Expected vs Actual                                                                  
   - Expected: Real-time message display in TUI                                           
   - Actual: Messages only visible after TUI restart 

### Steps to reproduce

1. run openclaw tui
2. send a message
3. see no response
4. /restart

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When a config change triggers an automatic gateway restart on macOS (LaunchAgent), the spawnSync launchctl call can time out. The fallback in-process restart also times out ("shutdown timed out; exiting without full cleanup"). The gateway continues running in a degraded state where memory reindex commands (openclaw memory index) complete successfully but indexes don't actually recover — leaving agent memory indexes at zero for hours until the process eventually self-heals.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run OpenClaw gateway via macOS LaunchAgent (ai.openclaw.gateway)
2. Have multiple agents with large memory indexes (1000+ files across 5 agents)
3. Make a config change that requires gateway restart (e.g., ACP settings: acp.enabled, acp.defaultAgent, acp.stream)
4. Gateway detects config change, sends SIGUSR1, attempts spawnSync launchctl kickstart
5. Spawn times out → falls back to in-process restart → shutdown also times out

### Expected behavior

Gateway should cleanly restart. If the primary restart mechanism fails, the fallback should either: - Successfully complete the in-process restart, OR - Exit cleanly so LaunchAgent's KeepAlive restarts a fresh process

### Actual behavior

Gateway enters a degraded state where: - Telegram message routing continues working (agents can chat) - Memory indexer is stuck — openclaw memory index CLI reports success but indexes don't update - Agent memory indexes drop to 0/N or freeze at stale counts - Watchdog monitoring detects the issue but repeated reindex attempts don't fix it - Self-heals after ~12-19 hours (likely when LaunchAgent eventually restarts the process)

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS (Darwin 25.3.0, arm64, M1 Pro)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
2026-03-04T04:18:16.943Z [reload] config change requires gateway restart (acp)
2026-03-04T04:18:19.023Z [gateway] full process restart failed (spawnSync launchctl ETIMEDOUT); falling back to in-process restart
2026-03-04T04:18:20.010Z [reload] config change requires gateway restart (acp.defaultAgent)
2026-03-04T04:19:01.170Z [gateway] shutdown timed out; exiting without full cleanup
Timeline: - 04:18 — Failed restart. Gateway degraded. - 07:10 — First memory index gap detected (indexed frozen, total growing) - 11:07 — One agent's index drops to ZERO. Stays at zero despite reindex every 30 min. - 23:19 — Self-healed (~19 hours later)
```

### Impact and severity

**Affected:** Multi-agent deployments on macOS (LaunchAgent) with large memory indexes (1000+ files across 5+ agents)
**Severity:** High — one agent's memory index stuck at zero for 12 hours, blocking all memory-dependent functionality
**Frequency:** Triggered once during embedding provider migration (config change → failed restart). Likely reproducible whenever a config change requiring restart occurs during heavy indexing.
**Consequence:** Agent loses all memory search capability. External watchdog reindex cannot recover while gateway is in degraded state. Self-heals only after eventual clean process restart (~12-19 hours).

### Additional information

**Additional Environment Information:**
Gateway: LaunchAgent (ai.openclaw.gateway)
Memory provider: Ollama (nomic-embed-text, 768-dim)
Agents: 5 (main + 4 staff)
Total indexed files: ~1,300 across all agents

**Related Issues:**
#10600 — Desktop app triggers periodic SIGUSR1 restarts (same SIGUSR1 → restart path)
#24279 / #24301 — PID mismatch in restart health check (restart mechanism bugs)
#32048 — "shutdown timed out; exiting without full cleanup" (same error, different trigger)
#32277 — Memory indexer doesn't detect embedding dimension mismatch (related — we were mid-migration)
#11728 — runSafeReindex partial reindex after DB deletion (related reindex bug)
#3573 — openclaw status always reports memory as dirty (makes debugging harder)

### Workaround:
External memory watchdog (LaunchAgent, runs every 30 min) that detects index drops and runs openclaw memory index. Works for normal cases but cannot recover from the degraded gateway state described here. Adding logic to detect repeated failures and trigger a full process restart (via launchctl kickstart -k) would mitigate this.

### Suggested Fix:
1. The in-process restart fallback should force-exit the process (e.g., process.exit(1)) after shutdown timeout, rather than continuing in a degraded state. Let LaunchAgent/systemd handle the restart cleanly.
2. Consider adding a health check that detects "indexes stuck" state and triggers self-restart.

comments.body: @dsantoreis - thanks for the fast response to this!

Thanks for the detailed report, this is very actionable.

I can reproduce the risk pattern from your timeline: the config-triggered restart path fails (Usage: launchctl <subcommand> ... | help [subcommand]
Many subcommands take a target specifier that refers to a domain or service
within that domain. The available specifier forms are:

system/[service-name]
Targets the system-wide domain or service within. Root privileges are required
to make modifications.

user/<uid>/[service-name]
Targets the user domain or service within. A process running as the target user
may make modifications. Root may modify any user's domain. User domains do not
exist on iOS.

gui/<uid>/[service-name]
Targets the GUI domain or service within. Each GUI domain is associated with a
user domain, and a process running as the owner of that user domain may make
modifications. Root may modify any GUI domain. GUI domains do not exist on iOS.

session/<asid>/[service-name]
Targets a session domain or service within. A process running within the target
security audit session may make modifications. Root may modify any session
domain.

pid/<pid>/[service-name]
Targets a process domain or service within. Only the process which owns the
domain may modify it. Even root may not do so.

When using a legacy subcommand which manipulates a domain, the target domain is
inferred from the current execution context. When run as root (whether it is
via a root shell or sudo(1)), the target domain is assumed to be the
system-wide domain. When run from a normal user's shell, the target is assumed
to be the per-user domain for that current user.

Subcommands:
	bootstrap       Bootstraps a domain or a service into a domain.
	bootout         Tears down a domain or removes a service from a domain.
	enable          Enables an existing service.
	disable         Disables an existing service.
	kickstart       Forces an existing service to start.
	attach          Attach the system's debugger to a service.
	debug           Configures the next invocation of a service for debugging.
	kill            Sends a signal to the service instance.
	blame           Prints the reason a service is running.
	print           Prints a description of a domain or service.
	print-cache     Prints information about the service cache.
	print-disabled  Prints which services are disabled.
	print-token     Prints service identifier given an xpc event token.
	plist           Prints a property list embedded in a binary (targets the Info.plist by default).
	procinfo        Prints port information about a process.
	hostinfo        Prints port information about the host.
	resolveport     Resolves a port name from a process to an endpoint in launchd.
	limit           Reads or modifies launchd's resource limits.
	examine         Runs the specified analysis tool against launchd in a non-reentrant manner.
	config          Modifies persistent configuration parameters for launchd domains.
	dumpstate       Dumps launchd state to stdout.
	dump-xsc        Dumps launchd XPC string caches to stdout.
	dumpjpcategory  Dumps the jetsam properties category for all services.
	reboot          Initiates a system reboot of the specified type.
	bootshell       Brings the system up from single-user mode with a console shell.
	load            Recommended alternatives: bootstrap | enable. Bootstraps a service or directory of services.
	unload          Recommended alternatives: bootout | disable. Unloads a service or directory of services.
	remove          Unloads the specified service name.
	list            Lists information about services.
	start           Starts the specified service.
	stop            Stops the specified service if it is running.
	setenv          Sets the specified environment variables for all services within the domain.
	unsetenv        Unsets the specified environment variables for all services within the domain.
	getenv          Gets the value of an environment variable from within launchd.
	bsexec          Execute a program in another process' bootstrap context.
	asuser          Execute a program in the bootstrap context of a given user.
	reload-atf      Reloads the active trial factors.
	submit          Submit a basic job from the command line.
	managerpid      Prints the PID of the launchd controlling the session.
	manageruid      Prints the UID of the current launchd session.
	managername     Prints the name of the current launchd session.
	error           Prints a description of an error.
	variant         Prints the launchd variant.
	version         Prints the launchd version.
	help            Prints the usage for a given subcommand. timeout), the fallback restart times out, and the process keeps serving traffic in a partially degraded state where memory indexing reports success but does not actually recover.

Your suggested direction makes sense:
1. On fallback shutdown timeout, fail loud and exit so the supervisor restarts a clean process.
2. Add a health-based self-restart trigger for prolonged "index stuck" states.

I will work on a fix path that prioritizes fail-safe restart semantics on macOS LaunchAgent and adds coverage around this degraded-state window.

title: [Bug]: Config-change-triggered restart fails via launchctl (ETIMEDOUT), gateway enters degraded state, memory indexes stuck at zero for hours

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When configuring Telegram group restrictions, the `channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom` field appears to expect Telegram user IDs (sender IDs) rather than group/chat IDs, which contradicts the field name and current documentation.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`
2. Configure `groupAllowFrom: ["-1001234567890"]` (group ID)
3. Send message to group from authorized user
4. Observe error in logs

### Expected behavior

According to the field name `groupAllowFrom`, it should accept group/chat IDs to whitelist specific groups where the bot should respond.


### Actual behavior

Invalid allowFrom entry: "-1001234567890" - allowFrom/groupAllowFrom
authorization requires numeric Telegram sender IDs only.
Messages are rejected with `reason: "not-allowed"`.

**Current Workaround:**
Using `groupPolicy: "open"` combined with `allowFrom: ["USER_ID"]`:

```json
{
  "groupPolicy": "open",
  "allowFrom": ["745xxxxxxxx"]
}
```

This allows the bot to join any group but only respond to specific users. However, this triggers **3 CRITICAL** security audit warnings about elevated tools and runtime exposure.

**Security Implications:**
- `groupPolicy: "open"` allows any user to add the bot to any group
- Bot sees all messages in those groups (privacy concern)
- Only mitigated by `allowFrom` restricting responses
- Not suitable for production/security-sensitive environments

**Suggested Fixes:**
1. **Option A**: Fix `groupAllowFrom` to actually accept group IDs as the field name suggests
2. **Option B**: Rename field to `groupSenderAllowFrom` or similar to clarify it expects user IDs
3. **Option C**: Update documentation to clearly state both `allowFrom` and `groupAllowFrom` only accept user IDs

**Additional Context:**
The "Doctor" warnings suggest using `groupAllowFrom` for group authorization, but the error message indicates it requires "sender IDs only". This creates confusion about the intended use of this field.


### OpenClaw version

Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

Linux 6.17.0-14-generic (x64)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
# OpenClaw Config Validation Errors
   19:38:13 error Error: Config validation failed: channels.telegram.groups:

   Invalid input: expected record, received array

   19:39:31 error Error: Config validation failed: channels.telegram:
   Unrecognized key: "allowedGroups"

   # Telegram Bot Access Warning (Root Cause)
   19:44:23 warn telegram/bot-access {"subsystem":"telegram/bot-access"}
   Invalid allowFrom entry: "-100XXXXXXXXXXXX" - allowFrom/groupAllowFrom
   authorization requires numeric Telegram sender IDs only.
   If you had "@username" entries, re-run onboarding or replace them
 manually.

   # Messages Being Rejected
   19:44:23 info telegram-auto-reply {"module":"telegram-auto-reply"}
   {"chatId":-100XXXXXXXXXXXX,"titl e":"GROUP_NAME_REDACTED","reason
 ":"not-allowed"}
   skipping group message

   19:44:45 info telegram-auto-reply {"module":"telegram-auto-reply"}
   {"chatId":-100XXXXXXXXXXXX,"titl e":"GROUP_NAME_REDACTED","reason
 ":"not-allowed"}
   skipping group message

   # Config Updates (Hot Reload)
   20:23:52 info Updated channels.telegram.allowFrom. Restart the gateway to
  apply.
   20:23:53 info gateway/reload {"subsystem":"gateway/reload"}
   config change detected; evaluating reload (channels.telegram.allowFrom)
   20:23:53 info gateway/channels {"subsystem":"gateway/channels"}
   restarting telegram channel
   20:23:53 info gateway/reload {"subsystem":"gateway/reload"}
   config hot reload applied (channels.telegram.allowFrom)

   20:23:55 info Updated channels.telegram.groupAllowFrom . Restart the
 gateway to apply.
   20:23:56 info gateway/reload {"subsystem":"gateway/reload"}
   config change detected; evaluating reload
 (channels.telegram.groupAllowFro m)
   20:23:56 info gateway/channels {"subsystem":"gateway/channels"}
   restarting telegram channel

   20:50:26 info Updated channels.telegram.groupPolicy. Restart the gateway
 to apply.
   20:50:26 info gateway/reload {"subsystem":"gateway/reload"}
   config change detected; evaluating reload (channels.telegram.groupPolicy)

   20:50:29 info Updated channels.telegram.allowFrom. Restart the gateway to
  apply.

   # Security Audit Warnings (After "open" Workaround)
   CRITICAL Open groupPolicy with elevated tools enabled
   Found groupPolicy="open" at: - channels.telegram.groupPolicy
   With tools.elevated enabled, a prompt injection in those rooms can
   become a high-impact incident.
   Fix: Set groupPolicy="allowlist" and keep elevated allowlists extremely
 tight.

   CRITICAL Open groupPolicy with runtime/filesystem tools exposed
   Found groupPolicy="open" at: - channels.telegram.groupPolicy
   Risky tool exposure contexts: - agents.defaults (sandbox=off;
   runtime=[exec, process]; fs=[read, write])
   Fix: For open groups, prefer tools.profile="messaging",
   set tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true, and use agents.defaults.sandbox.mode="al
 l"

   CRITICAL Telegram security warning
   Telegram groups: groupPolicy="open" with no channels.telegram.groups
   allowlist; any group can add + ping (mention-gated).
   Set channels.telegram.groupPolicy="a llowlist" and configure allowlist.
```

### Impact and severity

Impact:

 ```
   Users cannot restrict the bot to specific Telegram groups using
 groupPolicy="allowlist".
   The groupAllowFrom field appears to accept only user IDs instead of group
  IDs,
   contrary to field naming and documentation. This forces users to use
 groupPolicy="open"
   with allowFrom workaround, which triggers 3 CRITICAL security audit
 warnings and
   exposes the bot to potential prompt injection risks in any group.
 ```

 Severity:

 ```
   Medium

   - Workaround exists (groupPolicy="open" + allowFrom)
   - Core functionality works (bot responds in groups)
   - Security implications: 3 CRITICAL warnings with workaround
   - User experience impact: High (confusing configuration behavior)
 ```

### Additional information

```
   Tested with OpenClaw 2026.3.2 on Linux. Both `allowFrom` and
 `groupAllowFrom`
   behave identically (expecting user IDs), which contradicts the field
 naming.
   The "Doctor" config validation warnings are misleading in this case.
 ```
title: [Bug]: Telegram groupAllowFrom expects user IDs instead of group IDs

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

# Bug: Gateway crashes with dual-account Telegram config under systemd


## Summary

NOTE: I saw the PR was merged for this issue and may be related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/36725


Adding a second Telegram account via the `channels.telegram.accounts` schema causes the gateway to crash ~4s after both providers start polling. Reverting to single-account flat config restores stability. No error message is logged — the process exits silently with code 1.

## systemd unit (security hardening)

```ini
[Service]
Type=simple
User=openclaw
ExecStart=/usr/bin/openclaw gateway --port 18789
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=45

NoNewPrivileges=yes
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=tmpfs
BindPaths=/home/openclaw
ReadWritePaths=/home/openclaw
PrivateTmp=yes
CapabilityBoundingSet=
RestrictSUIDSGID=yes
MemoryDenyWriteExecute=no

Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=OPENCLAW_CONFIG=/home/openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json
EnvironmentFile=-/home/openclaw/.openclaw/secrets.env
```

The single-bot config runs indefinitely with these same systemd security directives.

## What we tested

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| Single-bot flat config under systemd | Stable (runs for hours) |
| Dual-account config under systemd | Crash at ~4s after providers start |
| Dual-account manual run (`sudo -u openclaw openclaw gateway`) | Survived 20s (timeout limit of test) |
| Dual-account under systemd with env vars commented out | Still crashes |
| Dual-account under systemd with `--trace-uncaught --trace-warnings` | No output before crash |
| Dual-account under systemd with stderr redirected to file | Empty file — no error written |
| Multiple cooldown periods (45s, 90s, 10min+) between restarts | Still crashes |

## Likely root causes (in order of probability)

1. **Schema mismatch** — code path still reads `config.telegram.botToken` directly somewhere; gets `undefined` with the nested accounts shape, leading to a native-level crash
2. **Duplicate polling conflict** — two bots both trying to long-poll Telegram's `getUpdates` API; Telegram rejects the second session with 409 Conflict, and the error handling path triggers a V8 crash
3. **Event bus collision** — both accounts registering on the same internal message handlers without namespacing, causing a native-level memory corruption

## What would help diagnose

The actual V8 crash stack trace. The `tgkill(self, SIGSEGV)` pattern means V8 detected an internal error but the crash reason isn't surfaced. Options:

- `NODE_OPTIONS='--abort-on-uncaught-exception'` might produce a more useful core dump
- V8 flag `--print-all-exceptions` or similar to capture the pre-SIGSEGV state
- Any internal crash logging that openclaw writes before the parent masks the exit code


### Steps to reproduce

## Environment

- OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
- Node.js: v22.22.0
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (kernel 6.8.0-101-generic, x86_64)
- Running as: systemd service with security hardening

## Config that works (single-bot flat)

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "botToken": "TOKEN_A",
      "dmPolicy": "pairing",
      "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
      "streaming": "partial"
    }
  }
}
```

## Config that crashes (dual-account)

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "accounts": {
        "default": {
          "botToken": "TOKEN_A",
          "dmPolicy": "pairing",
          "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
          "streaming": "partial"
        },
        "brainstorm": {
          "botToken": "TOKEN_B",
          "dmPolicy": "pairing",
          "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
          "streaming": "partial"
        }
      },
      "defaultAccount": "default"
    }
  }
}
```

Both tokens are valid and resolve to different bots. The rest of the config (agents, plugins, etc.) is identical between working and crashing states.


### Expected behavior

Health openclaw service

### Actual behavior

Crash loop



### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

ubuntu24.02

### Install method

npm

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
## Crash behavior

The gateway starts up normally — doctor runs, port binds, health-monitor starts. Both telegram providers begin polling:


[telegram] [default] starting provider (@bot_a)
[telegram] [brainstorm] starting provider (@bot_b)
[telegram] autoSelectFamily=true (default-node22)
[telegram] dnsResultOrder=ipv4first (default-node22)


~4 seconds later, the process exits with code 1. **No error message is logged to stdout, stderr, or the gateway log file.** The exit appears completely silent from the application's perspective.

## strace findings

We traced the process with `strace -f -e trace=kill,tkill,tgkill,exit_group`. The crash sequence:


# Go-based child processes (browser-server) exit cleanly:
PID_A exit_group(0)
PID_B exit_group(0)

# Then the gateway deliberately segfaults itself (V8 crash handler):
GATEWAY_PID tgkill(GATEWAY_PID, GATEWAY_PID, SIGSEGV) = 0
THREAD_1 +++ killed by SIGSEGV (core dumped) +++
THREAD_2 +++ killed by SIGSEGV (core dumped) +++
... (all threads killed by SIGSEGV)

# Parent launcher catches SIGCHLD, exits with code 1:
PARENT_PID --- SIGCHLD {si_code=CLD_DUMPED, si_status=SIGSEGV} ---
PARENT_PID exit_group(1)


The `tgkill(self, self, SIGSEGV)` is V8's internal crash handler — it detects a native-level error and intentionally raises SIGSEGV. The parent process (openclaw launcher) masks this as exit code 1 in systemd logs.

No writes to stdout (fd 1) or stderr (fd 2) before the exit — the crash produces zero application-level output.
```

### Impact and severity

channels - blocks workflow - always - cannot use second telegram channel

### Additional information

_No response_

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: Gateway crashes with dual-account Telegram config under systemd

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

# Bug Report: Telegram Multi-Account - Non-Default Accounts Don't Process Messages [REGRESSION]

⚠️ **REGRESSION:** This issue appeared AFTER upgrading to OpenClaw 2026.3.2. Multi-account Telegram bots worked correctly in the previous version.

### Steps to reproduce

# Bug Report: Telegram Multi-Account - Non-Default Accounts Don't Process Messages [REGRESSION]

⚠️ **REGRESSION:** This issue appeared AFTER upgrading to OpenClaw 2026.3.2. Multi-account Telegram bots worked correctly in the previous version.

## Environment
- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.3.2 (stable channel) - **BROKEN**
- **Previous Version:** Unknown (worked correctly)
- **OS:** macOS 26.3 (arm64)
- **Node:** 22.22.0
- **Installation:** npm global (`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`)

## Problem Summary
When multiple Telegram bot accounts are configured, only the "default" account processes incoming messages. Secondary accounts (e.g., `jarvis2`, `anselmo4`) successfully connect to Telegram API and consume messages (sender sees blue checkmarks), but OpenClaw does not route them to the agent - no processing, no logs, no responses.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure multiple Telegram accounts in `openclaw.json`:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "enabled": true,
      "dmPolicy": "open",
      "allowFrom": ["*"],
      "accounts": {
        "default": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8566...4F4Q"
        },
        "jarvis2": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A",
          "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
          "streaming": "partial"
        },
        "anselmo4": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8494...vN7U"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

2. Restart gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`

3. Send `/ping` to the "default" bot → **Works ✅**

4. Send `/ping` to `jarvis2` or `anselmo4` → **No response ❌**

## Expected Behavior
All configured bot accounts should receive and process messages independently.

## Actual Behavior
- **Default account:** Receives messages, processes them, responds correctly
- **Secondary accounts (jarvis2, anselmo4):**
  - Bot shows as online (`openclaw status --deep` reports OK)
  - Telegram API `getUpdates` shows 0 pending messages (consumed)
  - User sees blue checkmarks (message delivered)
  - **Zero logs** in `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log` for incoming messages
  - **Zero processing** - no agent invocation
  - **Zero responses**

## Isolation Test Result
Disabled "default" and "anselmo4", leaving only "jarvis2" enabled:

```bash
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.default.enabled false
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.anselmo4.enabled false
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Result:** jarvis2 still does not process messages (same behavior).

This confirms the issue is **not** a conflict between multiple bots, but a routing bug specific to non-default accounts.

## Verification Steps Performed

### 1. Bot Token Validity ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getMe"
```
**Result:** `{"ok":true,"result":{"id":8374026938,"is_bot":true,"username":"Jarvis1234567_bot",...}}`

### 2. Webhook Status ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getWebhookInfo"
```
**Result:** `{"url":"","pending_update_count":0}` (correctly using long-polling)

### 3. Message Queue ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getUpdates?limit=5"
```
**Result:** `{"result":[]}` (messages consumed but not processed)

### 4. Gateway Status
```bash
openclaw status --deep
```

**Output:**
```
│ Telegram │ OK │ ok (@guido_assistente_bot:default:113ms, 
                    @Anselmo4bot:anselmo4:59ms, 
                    @Jarvis1234567_bot:jarvis2:89ms) │
```

All bots show as connected with good latency.

## Log Evidence

Gateway successfully starts all providers:

```json
{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[default] starting provider (@guido_assistente_bot)"

{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[jarvis2] starting provider (@Jarvis1234567_bot)"

{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[anselmo4] starting provider (@Anselmo4bot)"
```

**But:** When sending messages to jarvis2 or anselmo4, **zero** incoming message logs appear (unlike "default" which logs every message).

## Configuration Attempts

Tried explicitly setting `dmPolicy` and `allowFrom`:

```bash
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.jarvis2.dmPolicy "open"
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.jarvis2.allowFrom '["*"]'
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Result:** No change - still doesn't process messages.

## Workaround
Use only the "default" account. Secondary accounts cannot be used.

## Hypothesis
The routing logic in OpenClaw's Telegram channel provider may be hardcoded or defaulting to route all messages only to the account named "default", ignoring messages from other account keys.

## ⚠️ Regression Details

**CRITICAL:** This is a **regression bug** introduced in version 2026.3.2.

- **Before upgrade:** All Telegram bot accounts (default, jarvis2, anselmo4) processed messages correctly
- **After upgrade to 2026.3.2:** Only "default" account works; jarvis2 and anselmo4 stopped processing messages
- **No configuration changes:** Same `openclaw.json` configuration that worked before the upgrade

This suggests:
1. A breaking change was introduced in the Telegram channel provider in 2026.3.2
2. The multi-account routing logic was likely refactored and broke secondary accounts
3. The "default" account may have special hardcoded handling that other accounts lack

**Priority: HIGH** - This breaks existing working multi-bot setups after upgrade.

## Additional Context
- No errors in logs
- No security audit failures beyond expected warnings for `dmPolicy="open"`
- Gateway service healthy (PID 65287, state active)
- All 3 bot tokens are valid and active
- Issue persists across multiple gateway restarts
- Issue persists even when secondary account is the **only** enabled account

## Requested Fix
Enable proper message routing for all configured Telegram accounts, not just "default".

---

**Reported by:** Guido Motti  
**Date:** March 5, 2026  
**Gateway PID:** 65287  
**Log file:** `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-03-05.log`

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

# Bug Report: Telegram Multi-Account - Non-Default Accounts Don't Process Messages

## Environment
- **OpenClaw Version:** 2026.3.2 (stable channel)
- **OS:** macOS 26.3 (arm64)
- **Node:** 22.22.0
- **Installation:** npm global (`/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`)

## Problem Summary
When multiple Telegram bot accounts are configured, only the "default" account processes incoming messages. Secondary accounts (e.g., `jarvis2`, `anselmo4`) successfully connect to Telegram API and consume messages (sender sees blue checkmarks), but OpenClaw does not route them to the agent - no processing, no logs, no responses.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure multiple Telegram accounts in `openclaw.json`:

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "enabled": true,
      "dmPolicy": "open",
      "allowFrom": ["*"],
      "accounts": {
        "default": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8566...4F4Q"
        },
        "jarvis2": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A",
          "groupPolicy": "allowlist",
          "streaming": "partial"
        },
        "anselmo4": {
          "enabled": true,
          "dmPolicy": "open",
          "allowFrom": ["*"],
          "botToken": "8494...vN7U"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

2. Restart gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`

3. Send `/ping` to the "default" bot → **Works ✅**

4. Send `/ping` to `jarvis2` or `anselmo4` → **No response ❌**

## Expected Behavior
All configured bot accounts should receive and process messages independently.

## Actual Behavior
- **Default account:** Receives messages, processes them, responds correctly
- **Secondary accounts (jarvis2, anselmo4):**
  - Bot shows as online (`openclaw status --deep` reports OK)
  - Telegram API `getUpdates` shows 0 pending messages (consumed)
  - User sees blue checkmarks (message delivered)
  - **Zero logs** in `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log` for incoming messages
  - **Zero processing** - no agent invocation
  - **Zero responses**

## Isolation Test Result
Disabled "default" and "anselmo4", leaving only "jarvis2" enabled:

```bash
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.default.enabled false
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.anselmo4.enabled false
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Result:** jarvis2 still does not process messages (same behavior).

This confirms the issue is **not** a conflict between multiple bots, but a routing bug specific to non-default accounts.

## Verification Steps Performed

### 1. Bot Token Validity ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getMe"
```
**Result:** `{"ok":true,"result":{"id":8374026938,"is_bot":true,"username":"Jarvis1234567_bot",...}}`

### 2. Webhook Status ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getWebhookInfo"
```
**Result:** `{"url":"","pending_update_count":0}` (correctly using long-polling)

### 3. Message Queue ✅
```bash
curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot8374026938:AAHnDWP7yN4FIyaOILwJSepCBS7YpsMlG_A/getUpdates?limit=5"
```
**Result:** `{"result":[]}` (messages consumed but not processed)

### 4. Gateway Status
```bash
openclaw status --deep
```

**Output:**
```
│ Telegram │ OK │ ok (@guido_assistente_bot:default:113ms, 
                    @Anselmo4bot:anselmo4:59ms, 
                    @Jarvis1234567_bot:jarvis2:89ms) │
```

All bots show as connected with good latency.

## Log Evidence

Gateway successfully starts all providers:

```json
{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[default] starting provider (@guido_assistente_bot)"

{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[jarvis2] starting provider (@Jarvis1234567_bot)"

{"subsystem":"gateway/channels/telegram"}
"[anselmo4] starting provider (@Anselmo4bot)"
```

**But:** When sending messages to jarvis2 or anselmo4, **zero** incoming message logs appear (unlike "default" which logs every message).

## Configuration Attempts

Tried explicitly setting `dmPolicy` and `allowFrom`:

```bash
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.jarvis2.dmPolicy "open"
openclaw config set channels.telegram.accounts.jarvis2.allowFrom '["*"]'
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Result:** No change - still doesn't process messages.

## Workaround
Use only the "default" account. Secondary accounts cannot be used.

## Hypothesis
The routing logic in OpenClaw's Telegram channel provider may be hardcoded or defaulting to route all messages only to the account named "default", ignoring messages from other account keys.

## Additional Context
- No errors in logs
- No security audit failures beyond expected warnings for `dmPolicy="open"`
- Gateway service healthy (PID 65287, state active)
- All 3 bot tokens are valid and active
- Issue persists across multiple gateway restarts
- Issue persists even when secondary account is the **only** enabled account

## Requested Fix
Enable proper message routing for all configured Telegram accounts, not just "default".

---

**Reported by:** Guido Motti  
**Date:** March 5, 2026  
**Gateway PID:** 65287  
**Log file:** `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-03-05.log`

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

After upgrading to 2026.3.2, session.dmScope was effectively reset to main, causing cross-channel reply leakage (WebChat/TUI replies being delivered to an iMessage contact). 

### Steps to reproduce

 1. Start from a setup where channel isolation is expected (session.dmScope = per-channel-peer) and iMessage is enabled.                                                          
 2. Upgrade OpenClaw to 2026.3.2.                                                                                                                                                 
 3. Send a message from WebChat/TUI (sender: gateway-client / openclaw-tui).                                                                                                      
 4. Observe that assistant reply is also sent to an iMessage contact (visible in imsg history as [sent] entries to that contact).                                                 
 5. Check config/state and routing behavior (openclaw status, session/channel behavior).

### Expected behavior

- Replies stay in the originating channel/session only.                                                                                                                          
 - WebChat/TUI reply should not be delivered to iMessage unless explicitly routed.                                                                                                
 - Upgrade should not change session isolation behavior unexpectedly.

### Actual behavior

Actual behavior                                                                                                                                                                  
 - Replies generated in WebChat/TUI were also sent to an iMessage contact.                                                                                                        
 - This created cross-channel message leakage and wrong-recipient delivery.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.3 (arm64)

### Install method

npm / Homebrew global CLI install (local gateway service)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Evidence observed via iMessage history (imsg history --chat-id <id> --limit <n>), showing assistant messages sent to iMessage contact at the same times as WebChat/TUI replies.  
Also reproducible context from runtime status and channel/session config checks (openclaw status, openclaw gateway status).
```

### Impact and severity

 Impact and severity                                                                                                                                                              
 - Affected users/systems/channels: Multi-channel setups using WebChat/TUI + iMessage.                                                                                            
 - Severity: Critical / data risk (cross-channel leakage to unintended recipient).                                                                                                
 - Frequency: Deterministic in observed environment after update until mitigation.                                                                                                
 - Consequence: Privacy risk, unintended outbound messages, trust/safety issue, potential sensitive info disclosure.

### Additional information

 Additional information                                                                                                                                                           
 - Last known good behavior: before update to 2026.3.2 (session isolation worked as expected).                                                                                    
 - First known bad: immediately after updating to 2026.3.2 (around 20:27 local time).                                                                                             
 - Temporary workaround that stopped leakage:                                                                                                                                     
     1. Set session.dmScope to per-channel-peer                                                                                                                                   
     2. Disable iMessage channel (channels.imessage.enabled=false)                                                                                                               
 - Request: please verify migration/update logic around session.dmScope and enforce strict cross-channel isolation guardrails by default.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

### Bug type

### Summary

I was successfully using OpenClaw using the default model and i just successfully set up its ability to create cron jobs without me and Microsoft Teams integration. Then i told it to switch to a cheaper model and it told me it successfully switched to Gemini 3 Flash. After that, everything was broken.

Now, on every single request i try, both in the Web UI as well as in Microsoft Teams, i always get this error message:

```
HTTP 400: error: Invalid JSON payload received. Unknown name "patternProperties" at 'tools[0].function_declarations[3].parameters.properties[4].value': Cannot find field.
```

I cannot interact with the AI in any way. Also, the model seems to be stuck, it always shows in the UI as "Nexos GPT 5 2" and whenever i try to change it and save it, then reload the page, it is always back at GPT 5 2. I am basically completely stuck now with an unusable system. Any help appreciated in fixing this and getting it to run again.

### Steps to reproduce

I do not think i can reproduce it.
I was using the default model (which i assume was GPT 5 2), then the AI told me it switched to Gemini 3 Flash.
Since then, every single request fails.

### Expected behavior

### Actual behavior

comments.body:
## Root Cause
Tool schemas were not consistently normalized for Gemini models when routed through OpenRouter proxy identifiers. The Gemini cleanup path relied on `modelProvider` containing `google`/`gemini`, but OpenRouter Gemini model entries often set `provider=openrouter` with `modelId` like `google/gemini-3-flash-preview`, so `patternProperties` and other JSON Schema keywords were passed through and rejected by Gemini function declarations.

## Fix
Updated Gemini detection in `src/agents/pi-tools.schema.ts` so `cleanSchemaForGemini()` runs when provider is Gemini directly or when provider is `openrouter` with `modelId` prefixed by `google/`. This strips `patternProperties` and other unsupported schema keywords before tool/function declaration conversion. Added regression coverage in `src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts` to verify the proxy Gemini path cleans unsupported fields and non-Gemini provider paths do not.

## Verification
Please verify by switching to Gemini 3 Flash and sending any request through PI; request should now succeed without `patternProperties` in Gemini tools payload. Existing non-Gemini proxy and local Gemini flows should remain unchanged behavior-wise.

I was able to unbrick the system by manually digging through files and changing `agent:main:main` `modelOverride` in `agents/main/sessions.json` to a uuid of another model from `models.json`. I can reproduce the issue by switching back and forth between Gemini Flash and other models by changing `modelOverride`.

Thank you for trying to fix it, i will avoid switching to Gemini 3 Flash again for the near future.

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

There is a critical discrepancy between the openclaw agents list CLI command and the internal agents_list tool used by sub-agents. While the CLI correctly lists all 12 configured agents, the agents_list tool (and subsequently sessions_spawn when an agentId is provided) only recognizes "main".
This prevents spawning specific personas (e.g., "maria") because the system fails the validation check, claiming the agent ID is invalid or unauthorized, even though it is fully configured in openclaw.json.


### Steps to reproduce

Steps to Reproduce
1. Configure Multiple Agents
Add agents to ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with proper agentDir and workspace settings.

2. Create Agent Files
For each agent, create:

~/.openclaw/agents/{name}/agent/SOUL.md
~/.openclaw/agents/{name}/agent/auth.json
~/.openclaw/agents/{name}/agent/auth-profiles.json
~/.openclaw/agents/{name}/agent/models.json
~/.openclaw/agents/{name}/sessions/ directory
3. Verify via CLI
openclaw agents list
Shows all agents correctly ✓

4. Call agents_list Tool
Expected: All agents returned
Actual (BUG): Only main returned

5. Call sessions_spawn with agentId
{"task": "...", "agentId": "maria", "runtime": "subagent"}
Expected: Maria spawns
Actual (BUG): Error: agentId is not allowed for sessions_spawn (allowed: none)

### Expected behavior

The agents_list tool should return all configured agents visible to the CLI, and sessions_spawn should successfully initialize the requested agentId.

### Actual behavior

agents_list tool returns only ["main"].
sessions_spawn fails with an error indicating the agentId is not allowed or found.
Workaround: Spawning without an agentId works (defaults to "main"), but the sub-agent loses the specific persona/tools of the intended agent.
Evidence: CLI correctly identifies 15 agents (IDs ranging from -1 to 18), but agents_list tool results remain restricted to the caller ID only.
Environment Note: The issue persists across diverse model providers (Moonshot, Ollama, OpenRouter), confirming it is a logic error in OpenClaw's internal agent-runtime and not a model-specific hallucination.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.2

### Install method

npm

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Evidence: CLI correctly identifies 15 agents (IDs ranging from -1 to 18), but agents_list tool results remain restricted to the caller ID only.
Environment Note: The issue persists across diverse model providers (Moonshot, Ollama, OpenRouter), confirming it is a logic error in OpenClaw's internal agent-runtime and not a model-specific hallucination.

Technical Findings
The issue appears to be an "internal filtering" bug introduced in the recent security hardening updates. The tool-layer registry is not syncing with the global agent config unless allowAgents is explicitly defined, which breaks the documented "out-of-the-box" behavior where local agents should be discoverable.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected any sub agents being spawned

### Additional information

_No response_
