body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Gateway crashes with uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'setSession') in Node's TLS when undici reconnects after socket close under concurrent subagent load. Six identical crashes across 3 days; zero in prior 14 days before concurrent agents were active.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure 2+ sandboxed subagent profiles with Docker sandboxing (sandbox.mode: "non-main", docker.network: "bridge")
2. Spawn 5 concurrent subagent sessions making outbound HTTPS requests (LLM API calls)
3. Wait for network disruption or socket timeout on any TLS connection (occurs naturally on WSL2; DNS failures like getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN observed preceding crashes)
4. Gateway crashes when undici's reconnect path calls tls.connect() with a cached TLS session

### Expected behavior

Gateway handles socket close gracefully — reconnects or surfaces error to the requesting session without crashing.

### Actual behavior

Uncaught TypeError kills the process:

TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'setSession')
    at TLSSocket.setSession (node:_tls_wrap:1132:16)
    at Object.connect (node:_tls_wrap:1826:13)
    at Client.connect (undici/lib/core/connect.js:70:20)
    at connect (undici/lib/dispatcher/client.js:452:21)
    at _resume (undici/lib/dispatcher/client.js:627:7)
    at resume (undici/lib/dispatcher/client.js:561:3)
    at Client.<computed> (undici/lib/dispatcher/client.js:285:31)
    at TLSSocket.onHttpSocketClose (undici/lib/dispatcher/client-h1.js:942:18)

OpenClaw's uncaughtException handler calls process.exit(1). If network is still unstable at restart, Discord gateway fetch fails → crash loop.

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2

### Operating system

Operating system: Ubuntu (WSL2), kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2, Windows 11

### Install method

Install method: npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Crash timestamps (all PST):
• Feb 28, 14:08 — Heavy API calls (memory indexing)
• Mar 1, 21:42 — Doc review session
• Mar 4, 23:42 — Batch spawn, 5 research agents
• Mar 5, 00:07 — Batch spawn, 5 research agents
• Mar 5, 00:28 — Batch spawn, DNS failure 38s prior
• Mar 5, 00:54 — Batch spawn, VPN OFF

DNS failure preceding crash:
[tools] web_search failed: getaddrinfo EAI_AGAIN api.search.brave.com

(00:28:02 → TLS crash at 00:28:40)

Call chain: onHttpSocketClose → kResume() → _resume → connect(client) → connector calls tls.connect() with cached session from WeakRef cache → setSession(options.session) → this._handle is null → crash.

Standalone stress test (same undici 7.22.0 / Node 22.22.0 / WSL2): 150 setSession calls with cached sessions, 128 forced socket destructions — zero null _handle occurrences. Does not reproduce with programmatic socket.destroy().
```

### Impact and severity

• Affected: All sessions (gateway process dies, all channels disconnected)
• Severity: High — total gateway crash
• Frequency: Intermittent — 6 times in 6 days with concurrent agents, 0 in 14 days without
• Consequence: Kills active agent work, loses in-flight responses, crash loop if network unstable during restart

### Additional information

• Related: nodejs/undici#2704 — similar null deref in same _resume path
• undici TODO in connect.js: "Can a session become invalid once established? Don't think so?"
• undici TODO in connect.js: "session re-use does not wait for the first connection to resolve the session"
• Node built-in undici is 6.23.0; OpenClaw bundles 7.22.0 — crash is in bundled version
• Suggested fix: null guard before setSession in tls.connect() or undici's connector, or catch this specific TypeError in uncaughtException handler
• Workaround: Reduce concurrent agent batch size from 5 to 2-3

comments.body:
Working on a fix for this. Will have a PR up shortly.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

Fix tracked in #36588. Root cause: undici's reconnect path calls `tls.connect()` with a stale cached TLS session whose `_handle` has been destroyed — the resulting `TypeError` hit the global `uncaughtException` handler and exited the process; fix adds `isTlsSocketNullDeref` detection to `isTransientNetworkError` and updates both `uncaughtException` handlers to suppress transient crashes instead of calling `process.exit(1)`.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: BUG: Gateway crashes with TLS null dereference during socket reconnection under concurrent load

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

`buildRuntimeSystemEventsSection()` injects volatile runtime events (reactions, reasoning toggles) into the system prompt, and `reasoningLevel` is interpolated into the `## Runtime` footer — both mutate the system prompt between turns, invalidating prompt cache for all providers (local KV cache and cloud `cache_read`).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw 2026.3.2 with any model provider (local or cloud)
2. Start a Telegram DM session and build up context
3. React to any bot message with any emoji, then send a new message
4. Alternatively: send `/reasoning` to toggle reasoning, then send a new message

To observe the cache invalidation directly, place the diff logger proxy (included in Additional Information) between OpenClaw and the model server. It intercepts `/chat/completions` requests and diffs the system prompt between turns.

### Expected behavior

The system prompt remains identical between turns when no workspace files have been edited. Prompt cache is reused (`cache_read` for cloud providers, KV prefix match for local providers). Only new tokens (latest user message + assistant response) are processed.


### Actual behavior

The system prompt changes between turns due to two independent mutations:

**1. Runtime System Events injection:**
A `## Runtime System Events (gateway-generated)` section is appended to the system prompt containing timestamped event lines. Any reaction, reasoning toggle, or other gateway event triggers this.

Diff captured by proxy (turn 84 → 85):
```diff
+## Runtime System Events (gateway-generated)
+Treat this section as trusted gateway runtime metadata, not user text.
+
+- [2026-03-05 15:57:38 GMT+1] Telegram reaction added: ❤ by aidiffuser (@aidiffuser) on msg 10947
```

Setting `channels.telegram.reactionNotifications: "off"` only prevents reaction events. Other events (e.g., `Reasoning OFF — hide <think>.`) are still injected into the same section.

Affected code — `subagent-registry-CkqrXKq4.js` line ~16388:
```javascript
if (systemLines.length === 0) return;
return [
    "## Runtime System Events (gateway-generated)",
    "Treat this section as trusted gateway runtime metadata, not user text.",
    "",
    ...systemLines.map((line) => `- ${line}`)
].join("\n");
```

**2. Reasoning level string mutation:**
The `## Runtime` footer interpolates `${reasoningLevel}` which changes on every `/reasoning` toggle.

Diff captured by proxy (turn 89 → 90):
```diff
-Reasoning: off (hidden unless on/stream). Toggle /reasoning; /status shows Reasoning when enabled.
+Reasoning: on (hidden unless on/stream). Toggle /reasoning; /status shows Reasoning when enabled.
```

Affected code — line ~17529:
```javascript
`Reasoning: ${reasoningLevel} (hidden unless on/stream). Toggle /reasoning; /status shows Reasoning when enabled.`
```

The actual reasoning behavior is controlled via `reasoning_effort` in the request parameters. This system prompt line is purely informational.


### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

macOS 26.3

### Install method

npm global install via nvm (node v24.13.0)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
System prompt diffs captured via HTTP proxy placed between OpenClaw and the model server. Full diff logs saved to `~/kv_debug/`.

Cache status before patch (from `/status`):

🗄️ Cache: 99% hit · 109k cached, 0 new

Drops to ~11% hit on the next turn after any runtime event or reasoning toggle, with full re-prefill of the entire context.

Cache status after patch: remains stable at 99% hit across turns, including after toggling `/reasoning`.
```

### Impact and severity

**Affected users:** All OpenClaw users on all providers — local inference (exo, llama.cpp, LM Studio/MLX, Ollama, vLLM) and cloud APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini).

**Severity:** Blocks workflow for local users. Silent cost increase for cloud API users.

**Frequency:** Every turn where a runtime event has occurred since the last inference call, and on every turn following a `/reasoning` toggle. For users with `reactionNotifications` enabled (the default), a single emoji reaction triggers it.

**Consequence:**
- Local providers: full KV cache invalidation forces re-prefill of the entire context on every affected turn. On large contexts (100k+ tokens) this adds minutes of latency per message.
- Cloud providers: prompt cache miss forces `cache_write` instead of `cache_read`, increasing input token cost per turn proportional to the system prompt size. See #19989 and #21999 for related user reports.

### Additional information

### Minimal patch (workaround)

We are running the following two patches in production. Apply to all 5 affected bundle files:

**1. Early-return from runtime events builder:**
```bash
sed -i.bak 's/if (systemLines.length === 0) return;/return; \/\/ PATCHED: skip runtime events for KV cache stability/' "$FILE"
```

**2. Replace volatile reasoning interpolation with static string:**
```bash
sed -i '' 's/`Reasoning: ${reasoningLevel} (hidden unless on\/stream)/`Reasoning: stable (hidden unless on\/stream)/' "$FILE"
```

**Affected files:**
- `dist/subagent-registry-CkqrXKq4.js`
- `dist/pi-embedded-DgYXShcG.js`
- `dist/pi-embedded-CtM2Mrrj.js`
- `dist/reply-DhtejUNZ.js`
- `dist/plugin-sdk/reply-DFFRlayb.js`

**Trade-off:** This removes runtime event delivery and reasoning state from the model's awareness entirely. The model no longer sees reactions or knows which reasoning mode is active via the system prompt. Reasoning behavior is unaffected since it's controlled via request parameters.

These patches are overwritten on `openclaw` updates.

### Suggested upstream fix

Move volatile state out of the system prompt and into conversation-level messages at the end of the messages array. This preserves cache stability (prefix stays immutable) while still delivering runtime information to the model.

For the reasoning line: make it static or remove it — reasoning behavior is already controlled via `reasoning_effort` in request parameters.

A config option (`agents.defaults.runtimeSystemEvents: "off"`) would be a welcome escape hatch for users who don't need runtime events at all.

### Diff logger proxy

The proxy used to diagnose this issue intercepts `/chat/completions` requests, extracts the system prompt, and diffs it against the previous turn. Deploy between OpenClaw and the model server. Logs to `~/kv_debug/`.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""diff_logger_proxy.py — KV cache debug proxy"""

import argparse, difflib, json, socket, sys, threading
from datetime import datetime
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
from pathlib import Path

LOG_DIR = Path.home() / "kv_debug"
LOG_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
UPSTREAM_HOST = "localhost"
UPSTREAM_PORT = 1235
turn_counter = 0
turn_lock = threading.Lock()
last_system_prompt = None

def extract_system_text(payload):
    def flatten(content):
        if isinstance(content, str): return content
        if isinstance(content, list):
            return "\n".join(
                b.get("text") or b.get("content") or ""
                if isinstance(b, dict) else b
                for b in content if b)
        return ""
    if "system" in payload:
        t = flatten(payload["system"])
        if t: return t
    for msg in payload.get("messages", []):
        if msg.get("role") in ("system", "developer"):
            t = flatten(msg.get("content", ""))
            if t: return t
    return ""

def save_and_diff(system_text):
    global turn_counter, last_system_prompt
    with turn_lock:
        turn_counter += 1; tc = turn_counter
    ts = datetime.now().strftime("%H%M%S")
    path = LOG_DIR / f"turn_{tc:03d}_{ts}_system.txt"
    path.write_text(system_text, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"\n{'='*70}\nTURN {tc} — {datetime.now().isoformat()} — {len(system_text)} chars\n{'='*70}")
    if last_system_prompt is None:
        print("  [turn 1] Baseline captured.")
    elif system_text == last_system_prompt:
        print("  ✅ SYSTEM PROMPT IDENTICAL — cache prefix should be stable.")
    else:
        print("  ❌ SYSTEM PROMPT DIFFERS — cache will be busted!")
        diff = list(difflib.unified_diff(
            last_system_prompt.splitlines(keepends=True),
            system_text.splitlines(keepends=True),
            fromfile=f"turn_{tc-1:03d}", tofile=f"turn_{tc:03d}", n=3))
        diff_path = LOG_DIR / f"diff_{tc-1:03d}_vs_{tc:03d}.diff"
        diff_path.write_text("".join(diff), encoding="utf-8")
        for line in diff[:80]: sys.stdout.write("  " + line)
    last_system_prompt = system_text; sys.stdout.flush()

def pipe_chunks(src, dst, chunk_size=65536):
    try:
        while True:
            data = src.recv(chunk_size)
            if not data: break
            dst.sendall(data)
    except (ConnectionResetError, BrokenPipeError, OSError): pass

class ProxyHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    def log_message(self, *a): pass
    def do_POST(self):
        body = self.rfile.read(int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0)))
        if self.path.rstrip("/").endswith("/chat/completions"):
            try:
                payload = json.loads(body)
                st = extract_system_text(payload)
                if st: save_and_diff(st)
            except Exception as e: print(f"[warn] {e}")
        skip = {"host","content-length","transfer-encoding","connection","proxy-connection"}
        hdrs = [f"POST {self.path} HTTP/1.1",
                f"Host: {UPSTREAM_HOST}:{UPSTREAM_PORT}",
                f"Content-Length: {len(body)}", "Connection: close"]
        for k,v in self.headers.items():
            if k.lower() not in skip: hdrs.append(f"{k}: {v}")
        raw = ("\r\n".join(hdrs) + "\r\n\r\n").encode() + body
        try: up = socket.create_connection((UPSTREAM_HOST, UPSTREAM_PORT), timeout=600)
        except Exception as e: return self._err(502, str(e))
        try:
            up.sendall(raw); buf = b""
            while b"\r\n\r\n" not in buf:
                c = up.recv(4096)
                if not c: break
                buf += c
            hend = buf.find(b"\r\n\r\n"); rh = buf[:hend]; bs = buf[hend+4:]
            code = int(rh.split(b"\r\n")[0].decode().split()[1])
            self.send_response(code)
            for l in rh.split(b"\r\n")[1:]:
                if b":" in l:
                    k,_,v = l.partition(b":")
                    if k.decode().strip().lower() not in ("connection","keep-alive"):
                        self.send_header(k.decode().strip(), v.decode().strip())
            self.send_header("Connection","close"); self.end_headers()
            if bs: self.wfile.write(bs); self.wfile.flush()
            pipe_chunks(up, self.connection)
        except (BrokenPipeError, ConnectionResetError): pass
        finally: up.close()
    def _err(self, code, msg):
        b = json.dumps({"error":msg}).encode()
        self.send_response(code)
        self.send_header("Content-Type","application/json")
        self.send_header("Content-Length",str(len(b))); self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(b)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("--listen-port", type=int, default=1236)
    p.add_argument("--upstream-port", type=int, default=UPSTREAM_PORT)
    a = p.parse_args(); UPSTREAM_PORT = a.upstream_port
    print(f"Proxy: :{a.listen_port} → :{UPSTREAM_PORT}  Logs: {LOG_DIR}")
    s = HTTPServer(("127.0.0.1", a.listen_port), ProxyHandler)
    s.socket.setsockopt(socket.SOL_SOCKET, socket.SO_REUSEADDR, 1)
    s.serve_forever()
```

### Related issues

- #20430 — Per-message `message_id` in system prompt invalidates KV cache every turn (partially fixed by PR #20597)
- #21785 — PR #20597 incomplete — `message_id` still injected in 3 unpatched bundle files
- #19892 — 2026.2.15 breaks prompt cache for local model providers
- #21999 — 150k+ token system prompt investigation and optimization plan
- #13854 — Local agent `models.json` silently overrides central config (related discovery during debugging)

comments.body: Submitted a fix for the reasoning level interpolation in PR #36555. Replaces dynamic `${reasoningLevel}` with a static label so the system prompt stays identical across `/reasoning` toggles. Reasoning behavior is unaffected since it's controlled via `reasoning_effort` in request parameters.

Thanks @Sid-Qin — this covers cache buster # 2 (reasoning line). Note that cache buster # 1 (runtime events injection via `buildRuntimeSystemEventsSection()`) is the broader issue and still open. That one fires on any gateway event — reactions, reasoning toggles, etc. — and injects timestamped lines into the system prompt. The reasoning line fix alone won't prevent cache invalidation when runtime events are present.

body:
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Summary

Multi-step tool calls using the google-gemini-cli provider (native OAuth) consistently hang for exactly 60 seconds between steps, likely due to silent HTTP 429 rate limits and hardcoded retries. In contrast, using a self-hosted API proxy (cliproxyapi) with the exact same Google account executes the same workflow seamlessly in seconds.

### Steps to reproduce

1.Configure OpenClaw to use the default google-gemini-cli provider (which uses native web OAuth).

2.Submit a prompt that strictly requires a multi-step tool-calling chain. For example: "Read openclaw config and .bashrc, then search Wikipedia in the browser and send me a screenshot, do this in one go."

3.Monitor the debug logs. Observe the time gap between embedded run prompt start and embedded run tool start.

4.Notice the severe ~60s hangs during the LLM's thinking phase.

5.Change the provider to a custom API proxy (cliproxyapi) using the EXACT SAME underlying Google account.

6.Re-run the exact same prompt and observe that the hangs disappear entirely.

### Expected behavior

The agent should process consecutive tool calls without arbitrary 60-second freezes. If the OAuth web endpoint is actively rate-limiting the requests (HTTP 429) due to the agent's rapid tool-calling loop and large context, the google-gemini-cli adapter should either:

1.Explicitly expose the 429 Too Many Requests or "Rate Limit" warning in the [info] or [debug] logs.

2.Fail gracefully rather than entering a silent, hardcoded exponential backoff (sleep 60s) that makes the user think the OpenClaw process has frozen or deadlocked.

### Actual behavior

When using the OAuth provider, the local tool execution is instantaneous (0s), but the LLM network turnaround silently stalls for exactly ~60s or ~44s between steps.

Run 1: google-gemini-cli (OAuth) - Total Time: ~183s

Step 1 (Read file): LLM ~7s -> Tool ~0s

Step 2 (Read file): LLM ~44s (Hang) -> Tool ~0s

Step 3 (Browser search): LLM ~60s (Hang) -> Tool ~1s

Step 4 (Browser screenshot): LLM ~4s -> Tool ~0s

Step 5 (Final reply): LLM ~64s (Hang)

Run 2: Self-hosted Proxy (cliproxyapi with SAME account) - Total Time: ~29s

Step 1 (Read file): LLM ~5s -> Tool ~0s

Step 2 (Read file): LLM ~3s -> Tool ~0s

Step 3 (Browser search): LLM ~5s -> Tool ~0s

Step 4 (Browser screenshot): LLM ~5s -> Tool ~0s

Step 5 (Final reply): LLM ~11s

The identical account performs 6x faster purely by changing the endpoint/adapter, proving the OAuth adapter is silently swallowing rate limits and hanging the agent's event loop.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: 🚨 CRITICAL CONTEXT: I want to heavily emphasize that both methods are using the EXACT SAME underlying Google account. The cliproxyapi is self-hosted and is simply wrapping my own account's credentials. The performance difference is NOT due to different account tiers (e.g., free vs. paid). It is strictly an issue with how the google-gemini-cli OAuth implementation interacts with Google's Web endpoints.

A consistent “~60s pause between steps” with the `google-gemini-cli` OAuth path strongly suggests the adapter is hitting a rate limit (HTTP 429) and sleeping/retrying without surfacing it clearly.

### Practical workaround
If you need tool-chains to stay responsive, switch away from the OAuth adapter to an API-based Gemini endpoint (your proxy or an API-key based provider). You already validated this: same account, different endpoint, 6× faster.

### Make it diagnosable (so you/maintainers can prove it’s 429)
1) Turn on debug logs and reproduce once.
2) Capture the exact timestamps around one “LLM step start → 60s gap → next step” window.

If you can paste the gateway log snippet for that window, maintainers can decide whether to:
- surface 429 explicitly in logs, and/or
- shorten the hardcoded backoff, and/or
- expose retry/backoff settings in config.

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Resources Hub | CoClaw](https://coclaw.com/resources)
- [Updating and Migrating OpenClaw Safely (No Surprises)](https://coclaw.com/guides/updating-and-migration)
@newcodebook Thanks for the quick response! I'm going to run a clean, fresh test right now and will provide you with the latest and most accurate data shortly.

body:
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Summary

When running openclaw agent --local with a prompt that invokes the Browser tool, the agent produces the final answer successfully, but the CLI process never exits and remains stuck (appears to keep running indefinitely). If the message does not use the Browser tool (e.g., a simple “hello”), the command exits normally.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run:
`openclaw agent --local --session-id "hello" --message "Open the browser, navigate to https://www.baidu.com, and tell me what you see. If the browser is not working, say so directly."`
2. Observe: The agent prints a complete final answer. After that, the process does not terminate and the shell prompt never returns (must Ctrl+C).
3. Control case (exits normally):
`openclaw agent --local --session-id "hello" --message "Hello"`


### Expected behavior

After printing the final answer, openclaw agent should terminate and return control to the shell (exit code 0), regardless of whether Browser was used.

### Actual behavior

With Browser usage in the message, the command finishes answering but never exits and stays running indefinitely.

### OpenClaw version

2026.2.6-3

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This looks like the embedded `--local` run leaving a browser/tool service handle open, so the Node event loop never drains even though the final answer printed.

### Workarounds
1) Prefer gateway mode (no `--local`) when your prompt uses Browser:

```bash
openclaw gateway start
openclaw agent --session-id hello --message "..."   # omit --local
```

2) If you must use `--local`, set a finite timeout so the process doesn’t hang indefinitely:

```bash
openclaw agent --local --timeout 120 --session-id hello --message "..."
```

If you can paste a minimal repro transcript (with `--verbose on`) it’ll help confirm which browser/runtime subscriber keeps the process alive.

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Browser Automation Timeouts: Fix 'browser control service timeout'](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-browser-automation-timeouts)
- [Troubleshooting - OpenClaw Solutions](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting)

title: [Bug]: openclaw agent hangs after completing response when Browser tool is used (CLI never exits)

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

The following problem has already been reported in [#31676](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/31676) and [#31677](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/31677). But since both have been closed (one probably by mistake, because 31676 has been closed as redundant because of #31677 and 31677 has been closed as redundant because of #31676) and nothing has been done to fix the problem in the last two releases 2026.3.2 and 2026.3.3, I am trying to focus some attention to the problem again.

Thanks for checking! 

To sum it up:
I can confirm what was written in both issues #31676 and #31677 and here's my personal journey with theSQLite routing bug:

I tried setting up `@mem0/openclaw-mem0` (v0.1.2) in OSS mode on OpenClaw 2026.3.3 (git-based install, Ubuntu 24, Node v22.22.0) with Qdrant as vector store and Ollama for embeddings. The infrastructure works fine — Qdrant health check passes, embedding model loads, plugin initializes and registers correctly:

```
openclaw-mem0: initialized (mode: open-source)
openclaw-mem0: registered (mode: open-source)
```

However, both `recall` and `capture` immediately fail with:

```
openclaw-mem0: capture failed: Error: Could not locate the bindings file.
→ .../jiti@2.6.1/node_modules/jiti/build/node_sqlite3.node
```

The root cause is the known jiti module resolution regression introduced in 2026.3.1 — jiti resolves the sqlite3 native binding path relative to itself instead of the actual sqlite3 package. This affects both the built-in memory system and the Mem0 plugin.

The Mem0 plugin internally defaults to a SQLite-based history store (`mem0ai/oss` → `SQLiteManager`), and there is currently no config option to disable or replace it. The `disableHistory` flag proposed in [[mem0ai/mem0#4172](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4172)](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4172) has not been merged into the plugin yet.

**What I tried (none of which worked):**
- `npm install better-sqlite3` in the plugin extension directory
- `pnpm rebuild better-sqlite3` in the OpenClaw root
- Updating OpenClaw from 2026.3.2 to 2026.3.3
- Setting `oss.disableHistory: true` in plugin config (option not implemented in v0.1.2)

**Current workaround:** Disabled the Mem0 plugin and reverted to `memory-core` until either:
1. OpenClaw fixes the jiti/sqlite3 binding resolution ([[openclaw/openclaw#31677](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/31677)](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/31677))
2. Mem0 merges the `disableHistory` / `historyStore` config option ([[mem0ai/mem0#4172](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4172)](https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4172))

The entire Mem0 OSS setup (Qdrant, Ollama embeddings, plugin config) is ready and just waiting for either fix. Would be great to see this unblocked — the plugin concept is exactly what OpenClaw's memory system needs.


### Steps to reproduce

Please see #31677

### Expected behavior

Please see #31677

### Actual behavior

Please see #31677

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

pnpm dev github

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Opened a fix PR for this: #37054

The change updates plugin loading so `jiti` handles the sqlite native stack as native modules (`sqlite3`, `better-sqlite3`, `bindings`) instead of resolving bindings under `jiti` paths.

I also added regression coverage for loader config + loader-path sqlite resolution, and cleaned up env handling in tests to avoid cross-test leakage.

title: [Bug]: Mem0 OSS Plugin unusable on OpenClaw 2026.3.x due to jiti/sqlite3 binding regression

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When configuring kimi-coding provider with a custom baseUrl, OpenClaw   ignores it and requests the wrong URL, causing all API calls to return 404.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure kimi-coding provider in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json:                
 ```json                                                                        
   {                                                                            
     "models": {                                                                
       "mode": "merge",                                                         
       "providers": {                                                           
         "kimi-coding": {                                                       
           "baseUrl": "https://api.kimi.com/coding",                            
           "apiKey": "sk-kimi-...",                                             
           "api": "anthropic-messages",                                         
           "models": [{"id": "kimi-for-coding", "name": "Kimi for Coding"}]     
         }                                                                      
       }                                                                        
     },                                                                         
     "agents": {                                                                
       "defaults": {                                                            
         "model": {"primary": "kimi-coding/kimi-for-coding"}                    
       }                                                                        
     }                                                                          
   }                                                                            
 ```                                                                            
                                                                                
 2. Restart OpenClaw gateway: openclaw gateway restart                          
 3. Send any message to the agent                                               
 4. Check logs: tail -f /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-*.log  

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw should request https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1/messages and return successful responses from Kimi API.    

### Actual behavior

OpenClaw returns 404 error. Log shows: "error=404 没找到对象"                
                                                                                
   Investigation confirms OpenClaw is requesting                                
 https://api.kimi.com/v1/messages (missing /coding prefix) instead of the       
 configured baseUrl.                                                            
                                                                                
   Direct curl test with correct URL returns 200, proving the API key and       
 endpoint work correctly.

2026.3.2

Linux 6.17.0-14-generic

```

_No response_

Root cause in model-selection-*.js:                                            
                                                                                
 ```javascript                                                                  
   if (kimiCodingKey) providers["kimi-coding"] = {                              
       ...buildKimiCodingProvider(),  // Uses hardcoded baseUrl, ignores user   
 config                                                                         
       apiKey: kimiCodingKey                                                    
   };

comments.body:
## Root Cause Analysis

After deep investigation (prompted by PR #36381 code review), I've identified the **real root cause** of this bug.

### The Bug is NOT in `resolveImplicitProviders()`

My initial PR #36381 fixed the wrong location. While `resolveImplicitProviders()` could be improved, the real bug happens later in the data flow.

### The Real Bug: `mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets()`

**Location:** `src/agents/models-config.ts` lines 165-166

**Problem:**
```typescript
function mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets(params) {
  // ...
  const preserved: Record<string, unknown> = {};
  if (typeof existing.apiKey === "string" && existing.apiKey) {
    preserved.apiKey = existing.apiKey;  // ✅ OK: preserve secret
  }
  if (typeof existing.baseUrl === "string" && existing.baseUrl) {
    preserved.baseUrl = existing.baseUrl;  // ❌ BUG: preserve OLD baseUrl from models.json
  }
  mergedProviders[key] = { ...newEntry, ...preserved };
  // This overwrites user's NEW baseUrl with OLD value from models.json!
}
```

### Complete Data Flow

```
1. User updates openclaw.json:
   { providers: { "kimi-coding": { baseUrl: "https://custom-proxy.com", ... } } }
   ↓
2. resolveImplicitProviders() + mergeProviders():
   provider.baseUrl = "https://custom-proxy.com"  ✅ Correct
   ↓
3. resolveProvidersForMode() reads old models.json:
   old models.json has: baseUrl = "https://api.kimi.com/coding/"
   ↓
4. mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets(new, old):
   preserved.baseUrl = "https://api.kimi.com/coding/"  ❌ Uses OLD value
   final result: baseUrl = "https://api.kimi.com/coding/"  ❌ User config ignored!
```

### Why This Happens

The function `mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets()` is designed to preserve **secrets** (like `apiKey`) from `models.json` so they don't need to be re-entered. However, it incorrectly treats `baseUrl` as a "secret" and preserves the old value, **preventing users from updating their configuration**.

### The Correct Fix

**Remove lines 165-166** from `src/agents/models-config.ts`:

```diff
  const preserved: Record<string, unknown> = {};
  if (typeof existing.apiKey === "string" && existing.apiKey) {
    preserved.apiKey = existing.apiKey;
  }
- if (typeof existing.baseUrl === "string" && existing.baseUrl) {
-   preserved.baseUrl = existing.baseUrl;
- }
  mergedProviders[key] = { ...newEntry, ...preserved };
```

**Why:** `baseUrl` is **user configuration**, not a secret. Users should be able to update it in `openclaw.json`, and the new value should take effect after `openclaw gateway restart`.

### Test Evidence

Created test script that proves the bug:

```bash
$ node test-merge-existing-secrets.mjs
❌ FAIL: User's new baseUrl was overwritten by old models.json value
❌ This is the REAL bug in Issue #36353!
```

### Next Steps

I will submit a new PR with:
1. Fix for `mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets()` (remove baseUrl preservation)
2. End-to-end test that verifies the fix
3. Documentation update explaining the behavior

### Workaround (Until Fixed)

Users can manually edit `~/.openclaw/agents/agent-X/models.json` and update the `baseUrl` there, then restart the gateway.

---

CC @greptile-apps[bot] - Thanks for the code review on PR #36381 that led me to dig deeper and find the real root cause!

title: [Bug]: Kimi Coding provider ignores user-configured baseUrl, causing 404 errors

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

I've observed that after updating OpenClaw globally using `npm install -g openclaw@latest`, the OpenClaw Gateway service (as reported in the control UI's "Instances" page) continues to display an older version number, even though `openclaw doctor` confirms the CLI tools are updated. This requires a manual edit to `C:\Users\paul\.openclaw\gateway.cmd` to correct the displayed version.

### Steps to reproduce

1.  Note the current OpenClaw Gateway version displayed in the OpenClaw control UI (e.g., `2026.2.26`).
2.  Run `npm install -g openclaw@latest` in a terminal to update OpenClaw.
3.  (Optional but confirms update) Run `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` and observe that the CLI reports the new version (e.g., `2026.3.2`).
4.  Restart the OpenClaw gateway (e.g., `openclaw gateway restart`).
5.  Check the OpenClaw control UI's "Instances" page. The gateway still reports the old version (e.g., `2026.2.26`).
6.  Manually edit `C:\Users\paul\.openclaw\gateway.cmd` and locate the `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION` line (or add it if missing) and update it to the correct, new version (e.g., `set OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION=2026.3.2`).
7.  Restart the OpenClaw gateway again.
8.  The OpenClaw control UI now correctly reports the updated gateway version.

**Observed `gateway.cmd` line (after initial `npm` update, before manual edit):**

### Expected behavior

The `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION` environment variable in `gateway.cmd` should be automatically updated or managed by the `openclaw update` command or `npm install -g openclaw@latest` process, ensuring the gateway reports the correct version without manual intervention.


### Actual behavior

The openclaw.cmd file is unchanged

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Windows 11

### Install method

npm

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Windows users?

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
This is the Windows-service wrapper (`gateway.cmd`) still carrying the old `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION`, even though the global npm package updated.

### Fix (no manual edits)
1) Regenerate the gateway service wrapper scripts:

```bash
openclaw gateway install --force
```

2) Restart the gateway.

If you still see mismatched versions, run a deep doctor scan to detect multiple service installs / stale paths:

```bash
openclaw doctor --deep
```

Recommended reading:
- [Install OpenClaw on Windows - Windows 10/11 & WSL2 Setup Guide](https://coclaw.com/getting-started/windows)
- [Updating and Migrating OpenClaw Safely (No Surprises)](https://coclaw.com/guides/updating-and-migration)

body: ### Bug type Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash) ### Summary Got a error right after install the app. Here's `gateway.err.log` ``` 2026-03-05T14:32:06.771Z [gateway] security audit: device access upgrade requested reason=scope-upgrade device=06de572f10cf4c16637d6442a62c7fb754073134041357b887c5d57d41e975c9 ip=unknown-ip auth=token roleFrom=operator roleTo=operator scopesFrom=operator.read scopesTo=operator.admin client=gateway-client conn=ed8c4fa1-ddd3-49c3-92cb-fd8e767ec946 2026-03-05T14:32:10.805Z [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=af7f9733-0d8b-417e-8659-55d76e9581a2 isError=true error=HTTP 401: Invalid Authentication 2026-03-05T14:32:12.936Z [ws] unauthorized conn=4fbcdf5c-6ae5-41e5-950e-8c8f2cb6ece6 remote=127.0.0.1 client=openclaw-tui ui v2026.3.2 reason=token_mismatch ``` ### Steps to reproduce 1. Download version 2026.3.2 2. Setup with the guide 3. Open tui and messaging the agent ### Expected behavior Agent respond ### Actual behavior Got 401 error ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 ### Operating system macOS 26.3 ### Install method npm ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body:
Your logs show the key clue:

- `unauthorized ... reason=token_mismatch` (openclaw-tui)

That usually means the TUI/client is using a different gateway token than the running gateway expects.

### Fix
1) Regenerate + apply a fresh gateway token (and let OpenClaw repair local client auth):

```bash
openclaw doctor --repair --generate-gateway-token
```

2) Restart the gateway, then reconnect the TUI.

If you suspect you have multiple installs/config paths, also run:

```bash
openclaw doctor --deep
```

**Why this works:** it removes the “old token cached somewhere” class of problems and ensures the gateway + local clients agree on the same auth token.

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Control UI Auth & Pairing: Fix 'unauthorized', 1008, and Remote Dashboard Access](https://coclaw.com/guides/control-ui-auth-and-pairing)
- [New User Checklist: Secure Setup, Backups, and First Week Wins](https://coclaw.com/guides/new-user-checklist)

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Environment:

OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
OS: macOS 26.3 (arm64)
Node: 25.7.0
Channel: Telegram bot DM (polling mode), channel status OK
Config:
tools.media.audio.enabled = true
skills.entries.openai-whisper-api.apiKey configured
Gateway healthy/reachable (openclaw gateway status RPC probe OK)
Expected behavior:

When I send a Telegram voice note, OpenClaw should run inbound audio transcription (STT) and provide transcript context to the agent before response.
Actual behavior:

Telegram voice update is received.
Agent run starts/completes normally.
Bot sends a response.
But no transcription occurs; runtime behaves as if only raw media is present (no transcript available).
Debug evidence (from openclaw --log-level debug gateway + filtered logs):

2026-03-05T13:38:45.653Z debug gateway/channels/telegram/raw-update ... "voice": {"duration":2,"mime_type":"audio/ogg",...}
2026-03-05T13:38:47.127Z debug agent/embedded ... embedded run start ... messageChannel=telegram
2026-03-05T13:38:47.169Z debug agent/embedded ... [context-diag] pre-prompt ...
2026-03-05T13:38:53.778Z debug agent/embedded ... embedded run agent end: isError=false
2026-03-05T13:38:54.375Z info gateway/channels/telegram ... telegram sendMessage ok ...
What’s missing:

No media/audio download/transcription/STT log lines between raw Telegram voice update and agent prompt.
Additional notes:

Gateway has periodic Telegram stale-socket restarts in polling mode (health-monitor: restarting (reason: stale-socket)), but channel remains operational.
Text messages work fine.
Voice notes consistently do not produce transcripts.
Would appreciate guidance on whether this is:

a regression in 2026.3.2,
missing config key(s) for this build, or
expected behavior change for Telegram voice handling.

### Steps to reproduce

1) Fresh start gateway on OpenClaw 2026.3.2:
openclaw gateway start

2) Confirm Telegram channel is connected:
openclaw channels status --probe

3) Ensure audio media is enabled:
openclaw config get tools.media
(shows: {"audio":{"enabled":true}})

4) In Telegram DM with the configured bot, send a 1–2 second voice note.

5) Run gateway with debug logging (or inspect logs):
openclaw --log-level debug gateway
(or openclaw logs --follow --max-bytes 80000)

Observed:
- Telegram raw update includes a `voice` payload.
- Agent run starts and ends normally.
- Bot sends a reply.
- No STT/transcription/media-processing step appears; transcript is unavailable.

### Expected behavior

When I send a Telegram voice note in DM, OpenClaw should process the inbound audio through the media/STT pipeline, produce transcript text, and include that transcript in agent context so the assistant can respond to what was said.

### Actual behavior

Telegram voice updates are received and the agent run executes/responds, but no STT/transcription step occurs (no transcript is available in context). The assistant replies as if only raw audio media was received. Logs show voice update + run start/end + sendMessage ok, with no media/STT/transcript processing lines in between.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.3 (arm64)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
2026-03-05T13:38:45.653Z ... telegram/raw-update ... "voice": {...}
2026-03-05T13:38:47.127Z ... embedded run start ... messageChannel=telegram
2026-03-05T13:38:53.778Z ... embedded run agent end: isError=false
2026-03-05T13:38:54.375Z ... telegram sendMessage ok ...

“No media/STT/transcript processing logs appear between voice update and response.”
```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Additional reproducible evidence (debug mode)

I ran the gateway in debug and sent a 3s Telegram voice note in DM.

Observed sequence:
1. Telegram raw update includes a `voice` object (`audio/ogg`, file_id present)
2. Session enqueues and agent run starts
3. Agent run completes successfully
4. Bot sends a normal text reply
5. No media/STT/transcript processing logs appear between voice update and agent prompt

Relevant log excerpt:
- `[telegram] update: {... "voice": {...}}`
- `[agent/embedded] embedded run start ... messageChannel=telegram`
- `[agent/embedded] [context-diag] pre-prompt ...`

Bot reply confirms missing transcript path:
“this Telegram runtime isn’t exposing an audio-transcription tool... can’t decode your .ogg directly...”

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Using Perplexity search engine via OpenRouter it throws an error since 2026.3.3 update.

The API Key is working since it's the same used for the main model.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure Perplexity as search engine with OpenRouter

  "tools": {
    "web": {
      "search": {
        "enabled": true,
        "provider": "perplexity",
        "perplexity": {
          "baseUrl": "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
          "apiKey": "sk-or-v1-XXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
          "model": "perplexity/sonar"
        }
      }
    }
  }

2. Ask the bot to search something on the internet

### Expected behavior

Search is fulfilled and result returned

### Actual behavior

Error.

{
  "status": "error",
  "tool": "web_search",
  "error": "Perplexity Search API error (401): {\"error\":{\"message\":\"Invalid API key provided. You can find your API key at https://www.perplexity.ai/settings/api.\",\"type\":\"invalid_api_key\",\"code\":401}}"
}

openclaw-gateway-1  | 2026-03-05T13:18:39.314+00:00 [tools] web_search failed: Perplexity Search API error (401): {"error":{"message":"Invalid API key provided. You can find your API key at https://www.perplexity.ai/settings/api.","type":"invalid_api_key","code":401}}


### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24

### Install method

docker

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
## Root Cause
- Perplexity requests through the OpenRouter path were sending auth details in a format not accepted by Perplexity compatibility requirements.
- The request/credential path did not provide the Perplexity-specific fallback/compatibility header behavior, causing 401 responses.

## Fix
- Normalized the OpenRouter Perplexity auth handling to use the compatibility header format expected by that provider while preserving existing non-Perplexity behavior.
- Added tests for header/auth handling and default provider behavior to guard against regressions.

## Verification
- Confirmed the Perplexity-specific branch now emits the expected auth header format and compatibility header.
- Confirmed OpenRouter non-Perplexity behavior remains unchanged.
- Ran the focused unit/integration tests updated in this change.

## Root Cause
Perplexity web-search auth was failing when users provided `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` or Bearer-prefixed keys, because key resolution and request routing were tied to Perplexity-specific input handling and the direct Perplexity endpoint alone.

## Fix
- Normalize API key inputs for Perplexity/OpenRouter (including stripping `Bearer `).
- Auto-detect OpenRouter mode from `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` and route to OpenRouter-compatible Perplexity chat/completions flow.
- Add OpenRouter compatibility headers and default model/endpoint selection that is key-aware.
- Keep direct Perplexity behavior for existing `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY` usage and update tests around key resolution, endpoint selection, and headers.

## Verification
- Unit tests were added/updated to cover Bearer normalization, OpenRouter key detection, OpenRouter endpoint routing, and `x-api-key`/Authorization header usage.
- Manual behavior now matches: OpenRouter keys no longer return 401 for Perplexity web search and legacy direct Perplexity keys remain functional.

Submitted a fix in PR #36275. The root cause is that `PerplexityConfig` only declared `apiKey` — the `baseUrl` and `model` fields the user sets in config were silently discarded. The fix adds both fields (following the existing `KimiConfig` pattern) and threads them through to `runPerplexitySearchApi`, which now constructs the endpoint as `{baseUrl}/search` instead of using the hardcoded `https://api.perplexity.ai/search`.

Note: if the configured `baseUrl` points to an endpoint that doesn't implement the Perplexity Search API `/search` format (e.g. OpenRouter's chat completions API), the user will get a clear HTTP error from that endpoint instead of the misleading 401 from the wrong server.

Submitted a fix in #36251. The root cause is that `web_search` hardcodes the native Perplexity Search API endpoint (`/search`), which is not available through OpenRouter — it only exposes `chat/completions`.

My PR adds a transparent compatibility layer:

- Auto-detects OpenRouter mode from `sk-or-` key prefix, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env var, or a custom `baseUrl` pointing to a non-Perplexity host
- Routes to `chat/completions` with the correct request format and auth headers when OpenRouter mode is active
- Embeds search filter parameters (country, domain, language, recency, date range) as natural-language hints in the user message, since `chat/completions` doesn't support structured filter fields — this avoids silently dropping user-configured filters
- Correctly selects `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` over `PERPLEXITY_API_KEY` when `baseUrl` targets OpenRouter, preventing 401 errors in mixed-key environments
- Native Perplexity API behavior is completely unchanged

15 new tests covering key resolution, filter embedding, and the full OpenRouter request/response flow.

title: [Bug]:  Perplexity Search API error (401) with OpenRouter

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

`chat.inject` returns:                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   `failed to write transcript: transcript file not found`                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   when the resolved `transcriptPath` exists in session metadata but the transcript file itself does **not** exist on disk (ENOENT). Manually creating (touching) the transcript 
 file makes the error go away immediately.                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   This breaks transcript persistence and makes `chat.history` / `sessions_history` return empty. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw with ACP backend `acpx` and run an ACP oneshot/run session (nonInteractive).                                                                            
   2. Observe the session entry has a `transcriptPath` like:                                                                                                                     
      `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`                                                                                                                  
      but the file does **not** exist (ENOENT).                                                                                                                                  
   3. Call gateway RPC `chat.inject` with params:                                                                                                                                
      `{ "sessionKey": "<sessionKey>", "message": "hello" }`                                                                                                                     
   4. Observe error:                                                                                                                                                             
      `failed to write transcript: transcript file not found`                                                                                                                    
   5. Workaround: `touch` the transcript file at `transcriptPath`, then retry `chat.inject`; it succeeds and messages are persisted.

### Expected behavior

If `transcriptPath` is resolved but the file is missing, `chat.inject` should create the transcript file (mkdirp + create/touch) and then append the injected message, instead of failing. 

### Actual behavior

Source inspection (OpenClaw 2026.3.2) shows `chat.inject` calls the transcript append helper with `createIfMissing: false`, so a missing transcript file becomes a hard error. 

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS arm64

### Install method

brew (openclaw-cli) + LaunchAgent gateway  

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
File: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`                                                                                                                                    
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   - `appendAssistantTranscriptMessag e(...)` returns `"transcript file not found"` if `createIfMissing` is false and the transcript file does not exist.                        
   - RPC method `"chat.inject"` calls `appendAssistantTranscriptMessag e` with:                                                                                                  
     `createIfMissing: false`  
 ### Proposed fix                                                                                                                                                              
                                                                                                                                                                                 
   In `chat.inject`, pass `createIfMissing: true` (or ensure transcript file is created at session creation / first write). Missing transcript file should not hard-fail         
 injection.
```

### Impact and severity

 - Affected: ACP oneshot/run sessions where transcript is not pre-created                                                                                                      
   - Severity: High (breaks persistence/observability; history APIs unusable)                                                                                                    
   - Frequency: 100% when transcript file is missing                                                                                                                             
   - Consequence: chat history remains empty; tooling depending on transcript breaks 

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #36171. Changed `createIfMissing: false` to `createIfMissing: true` in the `chat.inject` handler so that missing transcript files are auto-created via the existing `ensureTranscriptFile` helper — consistent with how the abort and auto-reply code paths already handle this.

Fixed in PR #36194.

`chat.inject` was passing `createIfMissing: false` to `appendAssistantTranscriptMessage`, so a missing transcript file caused a hard error. Changed to `createIfMissing: true` — matching what the websocket streaming path and reply delivery path already do — so a missing file is created on first inject instead of failing.

title: [Bug]:  chat.inject fails when transcript file is missing (createIfMissing=false)

body: ### Bug type Regression (worked before, now fails) ### Summary 报错如下400 Item 'fc_0c525c4a4ce4502f0069a8f7c07dc88190b6f50347f7cefd23' of type 'function_call' was provided without its required 'reasoning' item: 'rs_0c525c4a4ce4502f0069a8f7ad168881908544e83a9e64b642'. 相关配置 ### Steps to reproduce 首先配置cloudfare代理openai的api "models": { "mode": "merge", "providers": { "my-proxy": { "baseUrl": "https://gateway.ai.cloudflare.com/v1/*/open-ai/openai", "apiKey": "*", "api": "openai-responses", "models": [ { "id": "openai/gpt-5.2-pro", "name": "My Model" } ] } } },然后上传图片之后就会包上文提到的错 ### Expected behavior 麻烦告诉我如何修复我的openclaw ### Actual behavior == ### OpenClaw version 3.2 ### Operating system win11 ### Install method _No response_ ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body:
## Root Cause
The stream/proxy path assumed `reasoning` was always present on OpenAI `function_call` items, and the parser path would not preserve a valid function-call payload when it was missing.

## Fix
Updated the OpenAI ws stream/proxy transformation to route missing `reasoning` function-call items through a compatible proxy representation and adjusted `pi` embedded helper handling to keep function-call fields consistent. Added tests covering the affected paths so this edge case is now guarded.

## Verification
Executed the updated tests for the touched areas and validated the PR behavior by checking that transformed payloads still carry expected tool-call metadata in both normal and `pi` embedded streams.

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When sessions_spawn targets a Google/Gemini model via either alias ("gemini") or full provider ID ("google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview"), the session returns modelApplied: true but the sub-agent actually runs on the configured fallback provider (Anthropic/Sonnet). The model override is silently ignored with zero error or warning.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw with both anthropic and google authentication profiles
2. Spawn a sub-agent: sessions_spawn({ task: "Identify yourself", model: "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview", mode: "run" })
3. Observe: modelApplied: true is returned in the response
4. Check actual output: sub-agent identifies itself as Anthropic/Claude (e.g., "I'm Claude...")
5. Repeat with alias: sessions_spawn({ ..., model: "gemini", ... })
6. Result: Same silent fallback to Anthropic

### Expected behavior

Sub-agent is created and runs on the Google Gemini model specified. If the Google provider is unavailable or unconfigured, return an error with modelApplied: false and a clear message explaining why the override failed.

### Actual behavior

Sub-agent is created with modelApplied: true in the response, but the sub-agent actually runs on Anthropic/Claude (Sonnet or configured default).

The model override is silently ignored — no error, no warning, no log at any level. modelApplied: true is actively misleading.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3, commit 809f951

### Operating system

macOS 12.7.4 / Darwin 21.6.0 (x64)

### Install method

npm / development

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
**Test Matrix: Model Parameter vs Actual Provider**

| Model Parameter | Intended Provider | Actual Provider | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gemini` (alias) | Google | Anthropic | ❌ Silent fallback to Sonnet |
| `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` (full ID) | Google | Anthropic | ❌ Silent fallback to Sonnet |
| `grok` (alias) | xAI | xAI | ✅ Correct provider |
| `xai/grok-4-1-fast` (full ID) | xAI | xAI | ✅ Correct provider |

**Evidence:** Provider switching works correctly for xAI (both alias and full ID). Same code path, same auth profiles configured, but Google routes to fallback silently while xAI works as expected.

**Cost impact:** Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite = $1.50/MTok output. Sonnet 4.6 = $15/MTok output. Silent fallback = 10x cost increase with no visibility.

**Metadata misleading:** Session response includes `modelApplied: true` even though the actual model is different.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Multi-model pipelines attempting to use Google Gemini models (including Guardian screening workflows, cost-optimised deployments, and model diversity experiments).

Severity: HIGH (breaks model selection and cost control).

Frequency: 100% reproducible — occurs every time sessions_spawn targets a Google model.

Consequence: 
- Silent 10x cost increase (Gemini $1.50/MTok → Sonnet $15/MTok)
- modelApplied metadata misleading — no visibility into actual routing failure
- Model diversity experiments broken — user expects Gemini, gets Sonnet
- Guardian pipelines lose provider flexibility

### Additional information

Workaround: Explicitly using Anthropic models works correctly. Users can work around this by avoiding Google models for now.

Related: This routing bug has a significant cost impact on Guardian workflows — our testing showed we've been accidentally running on Sonnet (10x cost) while believing we were using Gemini. The fix/subagent-announce-delivery branch is already being worked on; this may be related to provider routing changes in that PR.

Tested with: anthropic:default and google:default auth profiles both configured and live.

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #36145. Extends the Gemini 3.1 forward-compat to also cover the `google` provider (not just `google-gemini-cli`). Also adds bare-ID normalization for `gemini-3.1-pro`, `gemini-3.1-flash`, and `gemini-3.1-flash-lite`.

Submitted a fix in #36154.

- PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36154
- Title: Fix sessions_spawn Google/Gemini model routing
- Branch: `codex/fix-36134-gemini-spawn`

## Root Cause
- sessions_spawn did not infer Google provider for bare Gemini model names; it defaulted to Anthropic provider

## Fix
- Added inferProviderFromModelHint() to detect gemini-* patterns and map to google provider; also added normalizeSubagentSpawnModelSelection() to normalize model selection with provider inference; set modelApplied: false on model patch failures

## Verification

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite (google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview) works correctly when set as the primary model (API calls succeed, tokens are counted normally). However, when the same model is used in the fallbacks array, it fails with:
FailoverError: Unknown model: google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
The same issue also affects google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview — it shows "model not found" when used as a fallback.

### Steps to reproduce

Add google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview or google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview to the fallbacks array in agent config
Trigger a failover (e.g., primary model hits rate limit)
Observe the error in diagnostic logs

### Expected behavior

Gemini 3.1 series models should be recognized and routed correctly in both primary and fallback contexts.

### Actual behavior

Models work as primary but are rejected as "Unknown model" during failover. It seems the fallback routing logic references an internal model registry that hasn't been updated for the 3.1 series (released March 3, 2026).
Relevant Config
json"model": {
  "primary": "google/gemini-3-flash-preview",
  "fallbacks": [
    "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
    "google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview",
    "anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5"
  ]
}

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

OS: Rocky Linux (Proxmox VM)

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: This is addressed in PR #36145 — extends Gemini 3.1 forward-compat to the `google` provider, so `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` and `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` are resolved correctly instead of triggering `Unknown model` errors.

Fixed in PR #36188.

The `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview` model (and other `gemini-3.1-flash-*` variants) wasn't in the built-in `google` provider model catalog in pi-ai yet. Added a forward-compat fallback that clones `gemini-3-flash-preview` as a template when resolving any `gemini-3.1-flash-*` model for the `google` provider — the same pattern already used for `google-gemini-cli`.

Note: `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` is already in the current catalog so it should resolve correctly.

Submitted a fix in #36176.

The root cause is that `resolveGoogleGeminiCli31ForwardCompatModel` only matched the `google-gemini-cli` provider. Models under the plain `google` provider were rejected during failover.

Changes:
- Introduced a `GOOGLE_PROVIDER_IDS` set to accept both `google-gemini-cli` and `google`
- Pass the actual `normalizedProvider` to `cloneFirstTemplateModel` instead of the hardcoded `"google-gemini-cli"`
- Renamed the function to `resolveGoogle31ForwardCompatModel` and updated comments to reflect multi-provider support
- Added tests covering `google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `google/gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, and unknown model rejection for the `google` provider

comments.body:
I'm analyzing this bug. The issue seems to be in how `deliveryContext` is handled when there's a channel conflict between TUI and webchat.

The `mergeDeliveryContext` function in `src/utils/delivery-context.ts` has logic to handle channel conflicts, but when `--deliver` is used with TUI, something is overwriting the channel to "webchat" instead of preserving "tui".

The key insight from the issue: `session's deliveryContext shows "channel": "webchat"` - this suggests the inbound message routing is overwriting the TUI channel.

I can try to create a PR fix if needed. Is there a specific file or function that should be checked first?

body: ### Bug type Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash) ### Summary Page prompt: origin not allowed (open the Control UI from the gateway host or allow it in gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins) 页面提示：origin not allowed (open the Control UI from the gateway host or allow it in gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins) ### Steps to reproduce ### 1.openclaw.json片段： ### 1.openclaw.json code fragment: ` "gateway": { "port": 18789, "mode": "local", "bind": "lan", "controlUi": { "allowedOrigins": [ "http://localhost:18789", "http://127.0.0.1:18789", ], "allowInsecureAuth": true }, "auth": { "mode": "token", "token": "xxxxxx" }, "tailscale": { "mode": "off", "resetOnExit": false },` ### 2.局域网中其他设备访问"http://192.168.x.x:18789" ，页面可以打开，但是出现图中标红提示： ### 2. When other devices on the local area network access 'http://192.168.x.x:18789', the page can be opened, but a red-marked prompt as shown in the figure appears: ### Expected behavior ### 能和主机UI界面一样正常对话和操作： ### Can it converse and operate normally just like the host UI interface: ### Actual behavior ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 ### Operating system Windows11 ### Install method _No response_ ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body:
This is the Control UI/WebChat **origin allowlist** doing its job.

When you open `http://192.168.x.x:18789`, your browser Origin becomes `http://192.168.x.x:18789` — but your `gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins` only includes `localhost` / `127.0.0.1`, so the websocket is rejected.

### Fix
1) Add the exact LAN origin(s) you will access from (scheme + host + port), and make sure your JSON has **no trailing commas**:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "controlUi": {
      "allowedOrigins": [
        "http://localhost:18789",
        "http://127.0.0.1:18789",
        "http://192.168.x.x:18789"
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

2) Restart the gateway (e.g. `openclaw gateway restart`, or restart your service).

Notes:
- If you access via a hostname (e.g. `http://my-pc:18789`), add that origin too.
- Avoid `"*"` unless you accept the security tradeoff (it effectively disables the allowlist).

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Control UI Auth & Pairing: Fix 'unauthorized', 1008, and Remote Dashboard Access](https://coclaw.com/guides/control-ui-auth-and-pairing)
- [OpenClaw Config Generator | CoClaw](https://coclaw.com/openclaw-config-generator)

comments.body:
Temporary solution (ubuntu/linux mint):

```
export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000
export DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS=unix:path=/run/user/1000/bus

mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user

cat > ~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=OpenClaw Gateway (bootstrap placeholder)

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/true
RemainAfterExit=yes

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
EOF

systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable --now openclaw-gateway.service
systemctl --user is-enabled openclaw-gateway.service

openclaw gateway install
```

```
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway.service
```

comments.body:
This is the same root cause as #34182. We have two PRs addressing this:

- **#34213** — clears `lastChannel`/`lastTo` on `/new` so new sessions don't inherit stale delivery context
- **#34204** — prevents webchat replies from leaking to stale session channels by suppressing session fallback for webchat origins

Both are CI-green and awaiting review.

Submitted a fix in #36164.

- PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36164
- Title: fix(gateway): keep mode=ui agent runs on webchat origin
- Branch: `codex/fix-agent-ui-origin-webchat`

- routeReply mirrored all replies to webchat/Control UI regardless of actual delivery channel, causing duplicates for iMessage

## Fix
- Added normalizedChannel !== 'imessage' condition to disable mirroring for iMessage replies; iMessage replies now only go to iMessage channel

## Verification
- Chat via iMessage with webchat open; verify reply only appears in iMessage, not in webchat

LLM error api_error: 上游返回了 19 个事件但未产生有效输出，请重试
Simple queries (without tools/system prompts) work fine, but any complex query that triggers tool use or requires extended reasoning fails consistently.

**Environment**
OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2
Node.js: v22.22.0
OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS (x86_64)
Hosting: Vultr VPS

json
{
  "models": {
    "mode": "merge",
    "providers": {
      "nexus": {
        "baseUrl": "https://example-relay.cc/api",
        "apiKey": "sk-xxx",

            "id": "claude-opus-4-6",
            "name": "Claude Opus 4.6",
            "contextWindow": 180000,

          }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}

### Steps to reproduce

Configure OpenClaw with a third-party Anthropic API relay that has extended thinking enabled by default
The relay returns "model": "claude-opus-4-6-thinking" in responses (appends -thinking suffix)
Send a simple message like "今天几号？" → ✅ Works fine
Send a complex message that triggers tool use, e.g. "今天科技圈最爆炸的是什么？" → ❌ Fails

### Expected behavior

OpenClaw should either:

Correctly parse SSE event streams that contain thinking content blocks alongside 
text
 content blocks
Or gracefully handle the case where the relay returns thinking events, extracting the actual text output

### Actual behavior

OpenClaw receives the streamed response (e.g., 17-19 SSE events), but fails to extract any valid text output from them. The error message indicates events were received but no usable content was found:

embedded run agent end: runId=xxx isError=true error=LLM error api_error: 上游返回了 17 个事件但未产生有效输出，请重试

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

In a Telegram group with 2 bots, a supervisor and a worker, /new (not /new@...) as well as /reset in any topic is paseed to General and automatically mentions (using @) the supervisor bot, causing it to reset instead of the worker bot 

### Steps to reproduce

Repro:
In a Telegram forum supergroup with 2 bots, open a non-General topic (e.g. threadId=158).
Send /new (or /reset) inside that topic.

### Expected behavior

Only the session for the worker bot in …:topic:158 resets.

### Actual behavior

General topic (threadId=1) supervisor bot session resets instead.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
New finding: Tested both qualified and unqualified forms in topic 158:

• /new@YerushalmiClawBot → correctly resets Bob's topic 158 session ✅
• /new@JobeYerushalmiBot → correctly resets Jobe's topic 158 session ✅
• /new (no @botname) → resets General (topic 1) regardless of which topic it's sent from ❌

So the bug is isolated to unqualified slash commands in forum topics. Qualified commands with @botname work correctly. Likely Telegram strips or ignores message_thread_id when routing an unqualified command, or OpenClaw falls back to the default/first session key without thread context.

Log result: /new is not logged at any level (INFO or DEBUG). The gateway receives and processes it silently — no inbound command entry, no threadId, no session key logged. The only downstream evidence was the session-memory hook firing for topic:1. There is no log trace of the actual command routing to diagnose.

Suggest: add logging to the slash command handler to capture chatId, message_thread_id, and resolved sessionKey when a command is received. That would make future diagnosis possible.

Based on your tests, the safest workaround right now is: **always use qualified commands in forum topics**.

### Workaround (works today)
In topic 158 (or any non-General topic), run:
- `/new@<worker_bot_username>` or `/reset@<worker_bot_username>`

Avoid unqualified `/new` / `/reset` in forum topics when multiple bots are present.

**Why:** Telegram forum topics can route unqualified slash commands without preserving `message_thread_id`/topic context, so OpenClaw falls back to the default/general session key (topic 1). Qualified commands keep the bot identity and thread context intact.

If you want to help confirm the root cause, paste one inbound update payload (redacted) for `/new` vs `/new@botname` from the gateway logs (or enable debug logging around Telegram updates) so we can see whether `message_thread_id` is missing in the unqualified case.

Recommended reading:
- [Telegram + OpenClaw: Setup, Group Controls, and Common Pitfalls](https://coclaw.com/guides/telegram-setup)
- [Troubleshooting - OpenClaw Solutions](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting)

Environment

OS: Linux under WSL
Gateway: local systemd service, loopback bind (127.0.0.1:18789)
Dashboard URL includes token fragment (#token=...)

Repro steps

Restart gateway:

Open dashboard URL:

Observe disconnect error:

What I already tried

Guest window + different browsers
Disabled extensions

~/.openclaw/devices/paired.json
~/.openclaw/devices/pending.json
Re-paired and approved pending requests via openclaw devices approve ...
Confirmed gateway token matches between:
