~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service (OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN)

Actual
Dashboard still fails with device token mismatch.

Expected
When dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true, device mismatch should not be enforced for Control UI connection.

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35966. The issue was that `evaluateMissingDeviceIdentity()` skipped the insecure-auth rejection when the bypass flag was set, but still fell through to `roleCanSkipDeviceIdentity()` which requires valid shared auth. Added an explicit early-return `allow` when the Control UI bypass policy is active.

Thanks for the detailed report! I've traced the root cause.

**Root cause**: `evaluateMissingDeviceIdentity()` in `connect-policy.ts` doesn't have an early allow path for when `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true` (sets `allowBypass=true`) AND authentication has already succeeded (`authOk=true`). The code falls through to `roleCanSkipDeviceIdentity(role, sharedAuthOk)` which requires *both* operator role AND `sharedAuthOk=true`. In setups where auth passes but `sharedAuthOk` remains false (e.g. reverse-proxy, `mode=none`, or certain gateway configurations), the connection is incorrectly rejected.

The "device token mismatch" error you're seeing likely occurs because your gateway config isn't yet reflecting the bypass (e.g. gateway not fully restarted or config path mismatch), causing it to fall into the full device-token auth path. After the gateway properly picks up `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true`, you'd instead see "device identity required" — which is what this bug causes.

**Fix**: #35968 adds an early allow in `evaluateMissingDeviceIdentity` when `isControlUi && allowBypass && authOk`, ensuring the bypass works regardless of which auth method caused auth to pass.

**Immediate workaround while waiting for the fix to land**:

Verify the config is actually being read by the running gateway:
```sh
# Check gateway sees the flag
openclaw config get gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth

# If running as systemd service, verify which config file it reads
systemctl --user show openclaw-gateway --property=Environment

# Hard-restart to force config reload
systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway
openclaw channels status --probe
```

Also ensure you're using a **gateway token** (from `gateway.auth.token`) in the URL fragment, not your device token. The `#token=...` value must match `gateway.auth.token`.

Submitted a fix in #36009.

- PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36009
- Title: Fix Control UI device token mismatch when device auth disabled
- Branch: `codex/fix-35944-control-ui-token`

I will follow up on CI/review updates in the PR thread.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: Control UI still rejects with device token mismatch even when gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true (v2026.3.2, Linux/WSL)

body: ### Bug type Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash) ### Summary When using the acpx backend for ACP sessions, child processes spawned during a session (e.g. claude-agent-acp, metabase-mcp, node, npm, sh) are not terminated after the session's TTL expires. This causes unbounded process accumulation over time. ### Workaround Manual cleanup: Track spawned child PIDs (or use a process group) per ACP session On session TTL expiry or completion, send SIGTERM → SIGKILL to the entire Add a periodic reaper for orphaned acpx processes as a safety net pkill -f "claude-agent-acp" pkill -f "metabase-mcp" _Possible Root Cause_ Child processes appear to be _detached from OpenClaw's process tree_ after spawning, so the TTL mechanism has no handle to kill them. A process group or cgroup-based approach may be needed to ensure full cleanup. _Suggestion_ process group ### Steps to reproduce Configure acp.runtime.ttlMinutes: 120 (or any value) Trigger multiple ACP sessions (e.g. via cron-based heartbeat tasks that spawn Claude + metabase-mcp subprocesses) Wait for sessions to complete and TTL to expire Run: ps aux | grep -E "claude-agent-acp|metabase-mcp" | grep -v grep ### Expected behavior When an ACP session's TTL expires (or the session completes), **all child processes spawned by that session should be killed** — including nested subprocesses like metabase-mcp, npm, sh, node. ### Actual behavior After 2 days of operation with a 30-minute cron schedule: - **478 zombie processes** still running (started Mar 03–04) - Processes from 30+ hours ago are still alive despite TTL = 120 min - Process breakdown by start date: - Mar 03: ~115 processes - Mar 04: ~189 processes - Mar 05 (today): ~52 processes (still accumulating) - CPU impact: minimal (~2%) - Memory impact: moderate (~37 GB used out of 62 GB total) ### OpenClaw version OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2 ### Operating system OS: Linux (Debian, kernel 6.1.0-42-amd64) ### Install method CURL ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information

comments.body:
Related: #11257 (same root cause — orphaned browser child processes)
Related: #12297 (session-level zombie cleanup request)

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When the Gateway writes jobs.json.bak and files under ~/.openclaw/cron/runs/, it creates them with 0644 permissions instead of 0600.

### Steps to reproduce

Run chmod 600 ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.bak
Wait for any cron job to execute
Check permissions: stat ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.bak

### Expected behavior

File remains 600 or is recreated with 600

### Actual behavior

File is recreated with 644

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3

### Operating system

OS: Linux (Docker/sandbox)

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Impact:
jobs.json.bak contains cron job configurations (schedules, message content, delivery targets)
Would be readable by other users on the same machine
However, parent directory ~/.openclaw/cron/ is 700, so other users cannot access in practice

Severity: Low
Protected by parent directory permissions; actual exploitation is unlikely
Fix is desirable for defense-in-depth, but not urgent

### Additional information

Additional Information:

# Parent directory permissions
$ ls -la ~/.openclaw/cron/
drwx------ (700) .
-rw-r--r-- (644) jobs.json
-rw-r--r-- (644) jobs.json.bak
drwxr-xr-x (755) runs/

# Files under runs/ are also 644
$ ls -la ~/.openclaw/cron/runs/
-rw-r--r-- (644) *.jsonl

# Timing of file recreation
When Gateway updates jobs.json during cron job execution,
.bak file is recreated with 644
Affected files:

~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json
~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.bak
~/.openclaw/cron/runs/*.jsonl

Related: runs/ directory itself is 755, should be 700

comments.body:
PR submitted: #35910

**Root cause:** `fs.promises.writeFile` and `appendFile` use Node's default file creation mode (subject to process umask, typically resulting in `0644`). Cron store files (`jobs.json`, `.bak`) and run-log files (`runs/<jobId>.jsonl`) were world-readable.

**Fix:** Pass `{ mode: 0o600 }` to all `writeFile`/`appendFile` calls in `src/cron/store.ts` and `src/cron/run-log.ts`, and apply a best-effort `chmod 0o600` to the `.bak` file after `copyFile`. Only affects newly created files — existing files retain their current permissions.
I can take this. Working on a small patch to ensure cron store (jobs.json + .bak) and run logs (runs/*.jsonl) are written with 0600 (and runs/ dir 0700) to avoid umask-created 0644.

Submitted a fix in #36040.

**Root cause:** `writeFile`/`appendFile` default to `0o644` and `mkdir` defaults to `0o755`. Cron store files and run-log files were world-readable; the `runs/` directory was world-traversable.

**Fix:** Pass `{ mode: 0o600 }` to all file-write calls and `{ mode: 0o700 }` to all `mkdir` calls in `store.ts` and `run-log.ts`. Add explicit `chmod` after `mkdir`/`appendFile` to also secure pre-existing paths from older installs. Replace `copyFile`+`chmod` with `readFile`+`writeFile` for `.bak` to eliminate the TOCTOU window.

title: [Bug]: Cron job files created with 644 permissions instead of 600

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

After upgrading from v2026.2.24 to v2026.3.2, the Matrix channel plugin fails to load with a module resolution error.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Build OpenClaw v2026.3.2 from source
2. Start the container
3. Check plugin status with `openclaw doctor`

### Expected behavior

Matrix plugin loads successfully and connects to the configured Matrix homeserver.


### Actual behavior

Matrix plugin fails to load with error:
```
Error: Cannot find module '/app/dist/plugin-sdk/index.js/keyed-async-queue'
Require stack:
- /app/extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send-queue.ts
- /app/extensions/matrix/index.ts
```


### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Debian 13

### Install method

Built from source using `openclaw-build.sh`

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
[gateway] [plugins] matrix failed to load from /app/extensions/matrix/index.ts: Error: Cannot find module '/app/dist/plugin-sdk/index.js/keyed-async-queue'
[gateway] [plugins] failed to load plugin: Error: Cannot find module '/app/dist/plugin-sdk/index.js/keyed-async-queue'
```

### Impact and severity

- Affected: Matrix channel users
- Severity: High (blocks Matrix functionality entirely)
- Frequency: 100% reproducible
- Consequence: No Matrix messages can be received or sent


### Additional information

- Last known working version: v2026.2.24
- Root cause: In v2026.3.2, `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send-queue.ts` was refactored to import `KeyedAsyncQueue` from `"openclaw/plugin-sdk/keyed-async-queue"`, but the build system doesn't create a separate module at that path. The `KeyedAsyncQueue` class is bundled into the main `index.js` file.

**Suggested fix**:
Option A: Fix the build to create the missing module at `dist/plugin-sdk/keyed-async-queue.js`
Option B: Revert `send-queue.ts` to use the simpler `Map`-based queue (as in v2026.2.24)
Option C: Change import to use main index

comments.body:
This was fixed in commit 7a2f5a0 ("Plugin SDK: add full bundled subpath wiring"), which added `keyed-async-queue` to the plugin SDK scoped alias map in `src/plugins/loader.ts`.

The fix landed after v2026.3.2 was tagged. Upgrading to v2026.3.3 (or the latest `@openclaw/matrix` plugin) should resolve this. If you're building from source, make sure you're on the latest `main` branch.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: Matrix plugin fails to load in v2026.3.2 - Cannot find module 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/keyed-async-queue'

body: ### Bug type Crash (process/app exits or hangs) ### Summary Browser Relay Chrome extension options page shows ON, but attaching on normal tabs fails (tooltip flips from “Relay connected, click to attach to this tab” to “Relay not running” and badge stays !). ### Steps to reproduce Stop any existing OpenClaw processes: pkill -f openclaw || true. Start gateway and browser relay locally: openclaw gateway start openclaw browser start --browser-profile chrome Install the Chrome extension via openclaw browser extension install and load it in Chrome (“Load unpacked”). In the extension options, set Relay URL to http://127.0.0.1:18792/ and Gateway token to the same token as gateway.auth.token in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. Open the extension options page and confirm badge is ON and relay shows as reachable/authenticated. Open https://example.com in a new tab, hover the extension icon (tooltip: “Relay connected, click to attach to this tab”), then click the icon once. ### Expected behavior After clicking the extension icon on a normal tab, the relay should stay connected and the badge on that tab should change from ! to ON, indicating the tab is attached and controllable. ### Actual behavior On a normal tab (for example https://example.com), hovering the icon initially shows “Relay connected, click to attach to this tab”, but after clicking once the tooltip immediately changes to “Relay not running” and the badge stays ! instead of switching to ON, even though the relay process is still running and reachable. ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 (85377a2) ### Operating system macOS Tahoe 26.3 ### Install method npm global ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell see in actual behavior ``` ### Impact and severity Affected: Local OpenClaw users on macOS (Apple M4) using the Chrome Browser Relay instead of the built‑in openclaw browser profile Severity: High (blocks using the relay to control real Chrome tabs) Frequency: 100% repro on my setup following the steps above Consequence: Browser tool cannot attach to any normal Chrome tab (badges stay !), so users cannot drive their existing logged‑in Chrome session via OpenClaw and must abandon the Browser Relay path ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body: same

This looks like a real regression in the **extension relay attach path** (options page can reach the relay, but the tab-attach handshake drops immediately).

### Practical workaround (still lets you drive your existing Chrome session)
1) Start Chrome with a local CDP endpoint:

```bash
open -na "Google Chrome" --args --remote-debugging-port=9222 --remote-debugging-address=127.0.0.1
```

2) Point OpenClaw at it and run attach-only (no extension relay needed):

```json
{
  "browser": {
    "cdpUrl": "http://127.0.0.1:9222",
    "attachOnly": true
  }
}
```

**Why this works:** CDP is the primary automation interface; the extension relay is an extra hop (and when that hop fails, the badge stays `!`).

If you want this fixed upstream, the most useful debug bundle is:
- `openclaw browser status --json` (after the failed attach)
- gateway logs around the click/attach moment (look for relay/extension lines)

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Browser Automation Timeouts: Fix 'browser control service timeout'](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-browser-automation-timeouts)
- [Browser tool: 'browser control service timeout'](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting/solutions/browser-control-service-timeout)

title: [Bug]: Browser Relay: options page shows ON, but normal tabs stay ! and flip to “Relay not running” after attach

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

### Summary

`openclaw security audit` reports critical ACL issues on Windows, even when ACLs are correctly restricted to only:
- current user
- `NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM`

This appears to be a locale/encoding parsing issue (Russian Windows), where identity names from `icacls` are displayed as garbled text (e.g. `NT AUTHORITY\�������`) and misclassified as "others".

---

### Environment

- OpenClaw: `2026.3.2 (85377a2)`
- OS: Windows 10 `10.0.19045` (RU locale)
- Node: `24.13.0`
- Shell: PowerShell

---

### Command

```powershell
openclaw security audit --deep
───

Actual result

Audit reports critical findings such as:

• fs.config.perms_writable
• fs.credentials_dir.perms_writable
• fs.auth_profiles.perms_writable

with messages like:

• acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(F)

───

Expected result

No critical ACL findings when ACLs are restricted to only:

• <COMPUTER>\<USERNAME>: FullControl
• NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM: FullControl
• inheritance disabled

───
Verification (PowerShell Get-Acl)

(Get-Acl "$env:USERPROFILE\.openclaw\openclaw.json").Access
(Get-Acl "$env:USERPROFILE\.openclaw\credentials").Access
(Get-Acl "$env:USERPROFILE\.openclaw\agents\main\agent\auth-profiles.json").Access

Observed entries:

• NT AUTHORITY\СИСТЕМА (FullControl, Allow, IsInherited=False)
• <COMPUTER>\<USERNAME> (FullControl, Allow, IsInherited=False)

No extra principals.

───

Notes

Likely parser logic relies on localized icacls identity text instead of stable SID-based matching.
Suggestion: resolve and compare SIDs (S-1-5-18 etc.) rather than localized account names.

### Steps to reproduce

openclaw security audit --deep
```

🦞 OpenClaw  2026.3.2 (85377a2) — I don't judge, but your missing API keys are absolutely judging you.

OpenClaw security audit
Summary: 3 critical · 3 warn · 1 info
Run deeper: openclaw security audit --deep

CRITICAL
fs.config.perms_writable Config file is writable by others
  C:\Users\user\.openclaw\openclaw.json acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(F); another user could change gateway/auth/tool policies.
  Fix: icacls "C:\Users\user\.openclaw\openclaw.json" /inheritance:r /grant:r "WIN-1OJ4F69CGN3\user:F" /grant:r "*S-1-5-18:F"
fs.credentials_dir.perms_writable Credentials dir is writable by others
  C:\Users\user\.openclaw\credentials acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(OI)(CI)(F); another user could drop/modify credential files.
  Fix: icacls "C:\Users\user\.openclaw\credentials" /inheritance:r /grant:r "WIN-1OJ4F69CGN3\user:(OI)(CI)F" /grant:r "*S-1-5-18:(OI)(CI)F"
fs.auth_profiles.perms_writable auth-profiles.json is writable by others
  C:\Users\user\.openclaw\agents\main\agent\auth-profiles.json acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(F); another user could inject credentials.
  Fix: icacls "C:\Users\user\.openclaw\agents\main\agent\auth-profiles.json" /inheritance:r /grant:r "WIN-1OJ4F69CGN3\user:F" /grant:r "*S-1-5-18:F"

WARN
gateway.trusted_proxies_missing Reverse proxy headers are not trusted
  gateway.bind is loopback and gateway.trustedProxies is empty. If you expose the Control UI through a reverse proxy, configure trusted proxies so local-client checks cannot be spoofed.
  Fix: Set gateway.trustedProxies to your proxy IPs or keep the Control UI local-only.
fs.state_dir.perms_group_writable State dir is group-writable
  C:\Users\user\.openclaw acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(OI)(CI)(F); group users can write into your OpenClaw state.
  Fix: icacls "C:\Users\user\.openclaw" /inheritance:r /grant:r "WIN-1OJ4F69CGN3\user:(OI)(CI)F" /grant:r "*S-1-5-18:(OI)(CI)F"
fs.sessions_store.perms_readable sessions.json is readable by others
  C:\Users\user\.openclaw\agents\main\sessions\sessions.json acl=NT AUTHORITY\�������:(I)(F); routing and transcript metadata can be sensitive.
  Fix: icacls "C:\Users\user\.openclaw\agents\main\sessions\sessions.json" /inheritance:r /grant:r "WIN-1OJ4F69CGN3\user:F" /grant:r "*S-1-5-18:F"

INFO
summary.attack_surface Attack surface summary
  groups: open=0, allowlist=1
tools.elevated: enabled
hooks.webhooks: disabled
hooks.internal: disabled
browser control: enabled
trust model: personal assistant (one trusted operator boundary), not hostile multi-tenant on one shared gateway

```

### Expected behavior

---

### Actual behavior

---

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

Windows 10 `10.0.19045` (RU locale)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Fixed in PR #35883.

Two root causes addressed:

1. **Missing Russian SYSTEM principal** — `TRUSTED_BASE` and `TRUSTED_SUFFIXES` did not include the Cyrillic localization `СИСТЕМА`. Added `"nt authority\\система"` to `TRUSTED_BASE` and `"\\система"` to `TRUSTED_SUFFIXES`.

2. **Encoding mismatch** — on Russian Windows the OEM code page is CP866, but Node.js reads child process output as UTF-8. `icacls` output containing `СИСТЕМА` would appear as garbled bytes. Fix: `inspectWindowsAcl` now runs icacls through `cmd.exe /c chcp 65001 > /dev/null 2>&1 & icacls "..."` on Windows, forcing UTF-8 output before Node.js reads it.
I'd like to work on this! 🙋

Looks like a **false positive caused by Windows locale/encoding**, not actually-writable ACLs.

On RU Windows, `icacls` prints the SYSTEM principal as `СИСТЕМА` (and often via OEM codepage CP866). If Node reads that output as UTF-8 you get the garbled `NT AUTHORITY\�������`, and the audit can misclassify it as an unknown/"other" principal.

### What to do
1) **Upgrade once a release includes PR #35883** (it adds the RU SYSTEM principal + forces UTF-8 output when invoking `icacls`).

2) **Workaround until then**: run the audit through `cmd.exe` after switching to UTF-8 codepage:

```bat
cmd /c "chcp 65001 >nul & openclaw security audit --deep"
```

If `Get-Acl` shows only your user + `NT AUTHORITY\СИСТЕМА` (no inheritance), then you can treat the current audit output as a parsing issue.

Recommended reading:
- [Install OpenClaw on Windows - Windows 10/11 & WSL2 Setup Guide](https://coclaw.com/getting-started/windows)
- [OpenClaw Ecosystem Picks (2026): Skills, Deployment, and a Practical Security Baseline](https://coclaw.com/blog/openclaw-ecosystem-project-recommendations)

title: [Bug]: False positive in security audit on localized Windows: SYSTEM shown as "others writable"

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Setting up a Windows node and connecting it to a gateway is unnecessarily confusing. The current flow requires undocumented environment variables, has config files silently overwritten on restart, and splits pairing across two different CLI commands (openclaw devices vs openclaw nodes). A fresh install should be straightforward — it currently isn't.


### Steps to reproduce

1 Install OpenClaw on a Windows machine
2 Run openclaw node install --host <gateway-ip> --port 18789
3 Try to connect to a LAN gateway

### Expected behavior

Node connects to gateway successfully, or the install command prompts for the gateway token interactively. Error messages guide the user to the next step.

### Actual behavior

- Node fails with `token_mismatch` — no hint about OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var or missing --token flag
- Manually editing node.json is overwritten on `openclaw node restart`
- After fixing token, `pairing-required` error appears but `openclaw nodes pending` shows nothing — must use `openclaw devices list` instead (undiscoverable)
- Full workaround requires: stop service → set env var → run foreground → approve via `openclaw devices approve` on gateway

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2

### Operating system

Windows Server 2022 / Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
gateway log: unauthorized conn=... remote=192.168.0.4 client=SQLSERVER1 node v0.3.15 reason=token_mismatch
gateway log: cause=pairing-required handshake=failed client=node-host clientDisplayName=sqlserver01
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All users setting up Windows nodes on LAN gateways
Severity: High (blocks onboarding — node cannot connect without extensive troubleshooting)
Frequency: 100% repro on fresh Windows install
Consequence: New users cannot connect nodes without reading source code or getting external help

### Additional information

Temporary workaround: Set OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var before install, then run `openclaw node run` in foreground (not as service). Approve via `openclaw devices approve <id>` on the gateway host. Do not use `openclaw node restart` as it overwrites the config.

This appears to have existed since node host support was added — not a regressio
title: [Bug]: Windows node onboarding: no --token flag, config overwritten on restart, confusing devices/nodes split

### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

After upgrading via Homebrew, openclaw --version displays "dev" instead of actual version number unless OPENCLAW_VERSION environment variable is manually set.


### Steps to reproduce

1. Install/upgrade OpenClaw via Homebrew:
brew upgrade openclaw-cli

2. Run version command without setting environment variables:
openclaw --version

3. Output shows "dev" instead of actual version (e.g., 2026.3.2)

4. (Workaround) Setting env var makes it work:
export OPENCLAW_VERSION=2026.3.2
openclaw --version # Now shows correct version

5. Unsetting env var breaks it again:
unset OPENCLAW_VERSION
openclaw --version # Shows "dev" again

### Expected behavior

openclaw --version should display the actual version number (e.g., 2026.3.2) without requiring manual environment variable configuration. Version should be baked in at build time.


### Actual behavior

- Installation method: Homebrew
- OS: macOS 25.2.0 (ARM64)
- Actual version: 2026.3.2
- Displayed version: dev
- Node.js: v25.8.0

Impact: All Homebrew users, automated deployment scripts, and CI/CD pipelines are affected.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.2

### Install method

Homebrew (brew install openclaw-cli)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
$ openclaw --version
dev

$ export OPENCLAW_VERSION=2026.3.2
$ openclaw --version
2026.3.2

$ unset OPENCLAW_VERSION
$ openclaw --version
dev
```

### Impact and severity

- **Who is affected**: All Homebrew users (primary installation method for macOS)
- **Severity**: High - upgrade mechanism relies on manual environment variable configuration
- **Frequency**: Every time after Homebrew upgrade
- **Consequence**:
- Users cannot verify installed version
- Automated scripts may fail
- CI/CD pipelines require manual env var setup
- Version-based feature detection may break

### Additional information

## 📋 填写 **Additional information** 字段

```markdown
**Last known good version:** Unknown (this may have been present in earlier versions)

**First known bad version:** 2026.3.2 (current Homebrew release)

**Temporary workaround:**
```bash
export OPENCLAW_VERSION=2026.3.2
```

**Additional context:**
- This issue affects the Homebrew installation channel specifically
- The version display issue suggests build-time version injection is not working correctly
- Users upgrading via `brew upgrade openclaw-cli` will encounter this issue
- The workaround requires manual intervention, which is not suitable for automated deployments

**Related files that may need investigation:**
- Homebrew formula for openclaw-cli
- Build/packaging scripts that inject version information
- Version detection logic in CLI entry point
```

---

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Device pairing required on loopback despite docs saying auto-approved

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run OpenClaw in Docker with gateway.bind = "loopback"
2. Configure gateway.auth.mode = "token"
3. Send an inbound WhatsApp message to trigger an agent tool call (e.g. message send)
4. Observe: tool call fails with "gateway closed (1008): pairing required"
5. Check devices/pending.json — new device keypair generated on every tool call

### Expected behavior

Loopback connections from the agent process to ws://127.0.0.1:18789 should be auto-approved without device pairing, as stated in the security docs: "Device pairing is auto-approved for local connects (loopback or the gateway host's own tailnet address)."

### Actual behavior

Every internal tool call generates a new device keypair in devices/pending.json that is never auto-approved. The agent cannot send outbound messages. WhatsApp inbound works, outbound fails consistently.

Error:
gateway closed (1008): pairing required
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Source: local loopback

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.1 (2a8ac97)

### Operating system

Linux 6.8.0-94-generic x64, Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

Docker, ghcr.io/hostinger/hvps-openclaw:latest on Hostinger VPS

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
2026-03-05T07:24:50Z [ws] closed before connect code=1008 reason=pairing required
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Source: local loopback
Config: /data/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Bind: loopback

devices/pending.json accumulates a new entry on every tool call with different deviceId and publicKey.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All Docker deployments on VPS with gateway.bind = loopback
Severity: Blocks workflow — agent cannot send outbound messages via any channel
Frequency: Always, 100% reproducible
Consequence: WhatsApp/Telegram outbound completely broken, agent only receives but cannot reply

### Additional information

Workarounds tried, none permanent:
- openclaw devices approve --latest — next call generates new device ID
- gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth: true — only affects Control UI, not agent
- gateway.auth.mode: "none" — no effect on device pairing layer
- Auto-approve loop every 10s — race condition, not reliable

Suggested fix: persist agent device keypair between WebSocket connections, or add gateway.auth.skipDevicePairing: true for loopback connections.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: Device pairing required on loopback despite docs saying auto-approved

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When using `openclaw tui` without `--deliver`, agent responses are also sent to the iMessage channel because `deliveryContext` is persisted in the session from a prior `--deliver` session and the heartbeat with `target: "last"` continuously re-writes it.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure iMessage channel in `openclaw.json` (dmPolicy: pairing)
2. Launch TUI with delivery: `openclaw tui --deliver`
3. Send at least one message — this writes `deliveryContext: {channel: "imessage", to: "<number>"}` to `agent:main:main` in sessions.json
4. Exit TUI
5. Relaunch TUI without deliver: `openclaw tui`
6. Send a message
7. Observe: response is delivered to iMessage in addition to TUI

**Perpetuation mechanism:** The gateway heartbeat (default `target: "last"`) fires every 30 minutes against the main session and re-writes `deliveryContext` to iMessage. Manually clearing the session fields and restarting the gateway is undone by the next heartbeat cycle.

### Expected behavior

Responses sent via `openclaw tui` (without `--deliver`) should appear only in the TUI. The TUI client connection without delivery enabled should be the sole reply target for that session.

### Actual behavior

Every agent response from the TUI session is also delivered to the iMessage channel (`imessage: delivered reply to imessage:<number>` in gateway logs), sending unwanted messages to the user's phone. The behavior persists across gateway restarts as long as `deliveryContext` remains in the session or the heartbeat re-populates it.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.3 (arm64)

### Install method

npm global (Homebrew)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Gateway log entry confirming delivery:

gateway/channels/imessage: imessage: delivered reply to imessage:+<redacted>


Session state at time of issue (`agent:main:main` in sessions.json):

{
  "deliveryContext": {
    "channel": "imessage",
    "to": "+<redacted>",
    "accountId": "default"
  },
  "lastChannel": "imessage",
  "lastTo": "+<redacted>",
  "origin": {
    "label": "openclaw-tui",
    "provider": "imessage",
    "surface": "webchat",
    "chatType": "direct",
    "to": "+<redacted>"
  }
}
```

### Impact and severity

- **Affected:** Any user with iMessage configured who has ever used `openclaw tui --deliver`
- **Severity:** High — unsolicited messages sent to personal phone number; user has no visible way to stop it from within the TUI
- **Frequency:** 100% reproducible; persists indefinitely until manually patched
- **Consequence:** Privacy concern (TUI conversations mirrored to SMS/iMessage); unexpected charges if carrier bills per message; user confusion

### Additional information

**Three issues identified — suggest fixing all three:**

**1. `deliveryContext` not cleared when TUI reconnects without `--deliver`**
The gateway should clear or ignore stored `deliveryContext` when a TUI client connects without delivery mode. Delivery intent should be scoped to the active connection, not persisted indefinitely in session state.

**2. Heartbeat with `target: "last"` should not mutate session `deliveryContext`**
The heartbeat fires every 30 min and re-writes iMessage delivery context onto the main session via `target: "last"`. This makes any manual cleanup of the session immediately undone. Heartbeat delivery should not update session routing state — consider storing heartbeat delivery separately or making `deliveryContext` immutable from the heartbeat path.

**3. `/deliver off` TUI slash command forwarded to agent instead of handled locally**
When the user typed `/deliver off` in the TUI, it was forwarded to the agent as a plain message rather than being intercepted by the TUI command handler. This left the user with no working UI mechanism to resolve the issue.

**Workaround that resolved the issue:**
```bash
openclaw config set agents.defaults.heartbeat.target none
# Manually remove deliveryContext, lastChannel, lastTo, origin from agent:main:main in sessions.json
openclaw gateway restart
```

**Bonus: `imessage` not listed as valid heartbeat `target` option**
Docs show `target: last | whatsapp | telegram | discord | none` — iMessage works implicitly via `last` but is not an explicit named option. Should be documented or added.
title: [Bug]: TUI responses delivered to iMessage when deliveryContext persists and heartbeat target is "last"

### Summary

The error "Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>" is incorrectly thrown when a user attempts to send an outbound WhatsApp message to a valid phone number that is not in the bot's allowFrom policy. This misleading error makes it seem like the phone number's E.164 format is invalid, making it hard to determine that the fix is just adding the phone number to allowfrom in openclaw.json

Set allowfrom to a number that doesn't match, like'8585551234' in config.json

This configuration worked in v2026.2.21-2

replace:
error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>"),

error: new Error(`Target "${trimmed}" is not listed in the configured WhatsApp allowFrom policy.`)

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35729. The allowList-denied branch now returns a specific error mentioning the allowFrom policy instead of the misleading E.164 format error.

# Root Cause Analysis: Issue #35580

⚠️ **Verification Method**: Code review verification  
✅ **Code location confirmed**: Found exact bug in OpenClaw dist files  
❌ **Not reproduced**: Unable to test (requires WhatsApp phone + allowFrom config)

---

## Problem Summary

WhatsApp outbound messaging fails with misleading error "Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>" when the **actual problem** is that the target number is not in the `allowFrom` policy. This makes users think the phone number format is invalid, when they actually just need to add it to the allowlist.

**Result**: Users waste time debugging E.164 format instead of updating `allowFrom` config.

---

## Code Location Verified

**File**: `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/whatsapp-actions-hbTfCyLS.js`  
**Lines**: 39, 55, 60

### Current Implementation (Buggy)

```javascript
if (trimmed) {
  const normalizedTo = normalizeWhatsAppTarget(trimmed);
  if (!normalizedTo) return {
    ok: false,
    error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")  // ❌ Line 39: Format invalid
  };
  // ... group JID check ...
  if (hasWildcard || allowList.length === 0) return {
    ok: true,
    to: normalizedTo
  };
  if (allowList.includes(normalizedTo)) return {
    ok: true,
    to: normalizedTo
  };
  return {
    ok: false,
    error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")  // ❌ Line 55: NOT in allowList
  };
}
return {
  ok: false,
  error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")  // ❌ Line 60: Empty target
};
```

**Problem**: Three different failure reasons all return the same generic error message.

---

## Why This Causes Confusion

### Scenario 1: Invalid Format (Line 39) ✅ Message is correct
```javascript
target = "not-a-phone-number"
// normalizeWhatsAppTarget() → null
// Error: "<E.164|group JID>" ✅ Correct (format IS the problem)
```

### Scenario 2: Not in allowFrom (Line 55) ❌ Message is misleading
```javascript
target = "+18585551234"  // Valid E.164 format
allowFrom = ["8585555678"]  // Different number
// normalizeWhatsAppTarget() → "+18585551234" (valid!)
// allowList.includes() → false
// Error: "<E.164|group JID>" ❌ MISLEADING (format is fine, allowFrom is the problem)
```

### Scenario 3: Empty target (Line 60) ✅ Message is acceptable
```javascript
target = ""
// Error: "<E.164|group JID>" ✅ Acceptable (need to provide a target)
```

**Root cause**: Line 55 returns the same error as Line 39, but the actual problem is different.

---

## User's Evidence

**User report**:
> "Set allowfrom to a number that doesn't match, like '8585551234' in config.json"
> "Outbound WhatsApp messaging fails with cryptic E.164 validation error"
> "This configuration worked in v2026.2.21-2"

**Confirmed**: User's diagnosis is correct — the error message doesn't reflect the real cause.

---

## Suggested Fix

### User's Proposal (Modified for clarity)

**Current (Line 55)**:
```javascript
return {
  ok: false,
  error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")
};
```

**Proposed**:
```javascript
return {
  ok: false,
  error: new Error(`Target "${normalizedTo}" is not in the configured WhatsApp allowFrom policy. Add it to channels.whatsapp.allowFrom or use a wildcard pattern.`)
};
```

### Alternative (More specific for each case)

**Line 39 (Format invalid)**: Keep as-is
```javascript
error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")
// ✅ Format IS the problem here
```

**Line 55 (Not in allowList)**: Make specific
```javascript
error: new Error(`WhatsApp target "${normalizedTo}" rejected: not in allowFrom policy. Update channels.whatsapp.allowFrom to include this number or use a wildcard.`)
```

**Line 60 (Empty target)**: Keep as-is or slightly improve
```javascript
error: missingTargetError("WhatsApp", "<E.164|group JID>")
// OR
error: new Error("WhatsApp target is required. Provide an E.164 phone number or group JID.")
```

**Change required**: +3 lines (replace error message at line 55)

---

## Verification Steps (If Reproducible)

### Setup
```json5
{
  "channels": {
    "whatsapp": {
      "enabled": true,
      "allowFrom": ["1234567890"]  // Different number
    }
  }
}
```

### Before Fix
```javascript
// Try to send to a valid E.164 number NOT in allowFrom
message({ action: "send", channel: "whatsapp", target: "+18585551234", message: "test" })

// Expected error (current): "Delivering to WhatsApp requires target <E.164|group JID>" ❌
// User thinks: "My phone number format is wrong"
```

### After Fix
```javascript
// Same test
message({ action: "send", channel: "whatsapp", target: "+18585551234", message: "test" })

// Expected error (fixed): "WhatsApp target '+18585551234' rejected: not in allowFrom policy. Update channels.whatsapp.allowFrom to include this number or use a wildcard." ✅
// User thinks: "Oh, I need to add this number to allowFrom!"
```

---

## Impact

**Severity**: Medium (UX issue, not a crash)  
**Type**: Misleading error message

**Affected users**:
- Anyone using WhatsApp `allowFrom` policy
- Users who configured allowFrom with a number that doesn't match their target

**Frequency**: 100% when target is not in allowFrom

**Workaround**: 
1. Add the target number to `channels.whatsapp.allowFrom`, or
2. Use a wildcard pattern like `["*"]` (not recommended for production)

**Benefits of fix**:
- ✅ Clear error messages save debugging time
- ✅ Users immediately know how to fix the issue
- ✅ Reduced support burden (less "E.164 format" confusion)

---

## Additional Context

**User mentions**:
> "last known good version: 2026.2.21-2"
> "last known bad version: 2026.2.26"

This suggests the issue was **introduced in a regression** between these versions.

**Suggested source file** (per user):
> `src/whatsapp/resolve-outbound-target.ts:28`

**Verified compiled file**:
> `dist/whatsapp-actions-hbTfCyLS.js:55`

---

## Evidence Summary

| Criterion | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| User report detail | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Good (exact error + file location) |
| Code location found | ✅ Lines 39, 55, 60 in whatsapp-actions |
| Logic matches bug | ✅ Same error for different causes |
| Fix straightforward | ✅ Replace error message at line 55 |
| User diagnosis | ✅ Correct (allowFrom is the real issue) |

---

**Environment**:
- OpenClaw: 2026.2.26 (user), 2026.3.2 (verified in current version)
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04 x86
- Install: npm global

---

**Verification Limitations**:
- ⚠️ Not tested in live environment (requires WhatsApp phone + allowFrom config)
- ✅ Code logic confirmed in dist files
- ✅ User provided detailed logs and reproduction steps
- ✅ Regression confirmed (worked in 2026.2.21-2)

---

**Files affected**:
- `src/whatsapp/resolve-outbound-target.ts` (source, line ~28 per user)
- `dist/whatsapp-actions-hbTfCyLS.js` (compiled, lines 39, 55, 60)

---

*Analysis based on code review and user report. User correctly identified the misleading error message. Code confirms three different failure reasons use the same generic error, with Line 55 being the most misleading case.*

@Sid-Qin Great work on PR #35729! I independently verified the same issue — the code uses the same error message for three different failure scenarios (format invalid, not in allowList, empty target), with line 55 being the most misleading case.

Your fix to make the allowList-denied error message specific is exactly right. It will save users a lot of debugging time. 👍

This is already fixed in PR #35643 which addresses the same root cause. The fix changes the allowFrom-denial error to include the actual target number and an actionable hint to add it to `allowFrom` in config.

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

The `message:sent` internal hook does not fire when agents send direct responses to users. This prevents hooks that need to react to outbound messages (e.g., model indicators, logging, analytics) from working.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a managed hook directory at `~/.openclaw/hooks/test-hook/` with HOOK.md containing `events: [message:sent]`
2. Add a handler.js that logs to a file
3. Restart the gateway - hook shows as registered: `Registered hook: test-hook -> message:sent`
4. Send a message to the agent via any channel
5. Observe the hook handler is never called
6. Check gateway logs - no errors, hook is just silently skipped

### Expected behavior

The `message:sent` hook should fire after each agent response, allowing hooks to track outbound messages for all sessions, not just mirrored ones.

### Actual behavior

The hook never fires. No user-visible errors. The code includes a warning for this case: `"session.agentId present without session key; internal message:sent hook will be skipped"` - but the warning is not logged because the condition is silently skipped.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

WSL2 (Windows 11) / Linux 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

## Root Cause

In `deliverOutboundPayloads`, the session key for internal hooks is derived from:

```javascript
const sessionKeyForInternalHooks = params.mirror?.sessionKey ?? params.session?.key;
```

For direct agent responses (no mirror), `sessionKeyForInternalHooks` depends on `params.session?.key`. However, `buildOutboundSessionContext()` only sets `session.key` when a `sessionKey` parameter is explicitly provided:

```javascript
const outboundSession = buildOutboundSessionContext({
  cfg,
  agentId: params.agentId,
  sessionKey: params.mirror?.sessionKey  // ← undefined for direct responses
});
```

When `sessionKey` is undefined, `buildOutboundSessionContext()` returns an object without a `key` property, so `params.session?.key` is also undefined.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Pass the session key through the call chain when building outbound context
2. Derive session key from the agent context when mirror is not present
3. Document that `message:sent` hooks only work for mirrored sessions

## Workaround

None available for hook-based solutions. Use `/status` or ask the agent directly for model info.

comments.body:
Fixed in PR #35875.

Root cause: `buildOutboundSessionContext` in `message.ts` only used `params.mirror?.sessionKey` as the session key. When the agent's send tool resolves no `outboundRoute` (e.g. sending to a new contact, or first-time outbound), `mirror` is `undefined`, so `sessionKeyForInternalHooks` in `deliverOutboundPayloads` ends up `undefined` and the internal `message:sent` hook is silently skipped.

Fix: Thread `input.sessionKey` from `RunMessageActionParams` through `OutboundSendContext` → `sendMessage` → `buildOutboundSessionContext`, so the session key is used as a fallback when `mirror` is absent.

Submitted a fix in #36103.

- PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36103
- Title: Fix: pass session key for direct agent message:sent hooks
- Branch: `codex/fix-35557-message-sent`

## Root Cause
- Direct agent outbound deliveries could miss session-key metadata, so internal message:sent hooks were skipped for direct responses.

## Fix
- Propagated effective session key from outbound/direct delivery context into mirror metadata and added regression coverage for direct-delivery hook behavior.

## Verification
- Reproduced missing hook execution before the change and confirmed hooks fire after propagation; tests pass in src/commands/agent.delivery.test.ts and src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts.

title: message:sent internal hook never fires for direct agent responses

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35618. The root cause: when a non-local chat run emits a final event, `maybeRefreshHistoryForRun` triggers `loadHistory()` → `chatLog.clearAll()`. If the message hasn't been persisted to the history API yet, it disappears. The fix defers the history reload to only fire when the final has no displayable content; when there's a renderable message, `finalizeAssistant` renders it directly.

Submitted a fix in #36100.

- PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/36100
- Title: Fix: render TUI chat events for canonical session-key aliases
- Branch: `codex/fix-35523-tui-display`

## Root Cause
- TUI chat-event filtering compared session keys too strictly and did not treat canonical alias forms as equivalent, so assistant events could be ignored.

## Fix
- Normalized session-key matching in TUI event handlers to accept canonical aliases as the same session and added regression tests for alias-equivalent keys.

## Verification
- Reproduced dropped assistant events with alias-equivalent keys and confirmed rendering works after the fix; tests pass in src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts.

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary


```markdown

### Description

When multiple providers each expose a model with the same `id` (e.g. `claude-opus-4-6` from `nexus-d`, `yunyi-claude`, `nexus-official`, `nexus-a`), the model switcher (/model list or Control UI) shows **all** of them as selected (checked) after I choose **one** provider’s model.

**Expected:** Only the single option I selected (identified by `provider/modelId`, e.g. `nexus-d/claude-opus-4-6`) should be marked as selected.

**Actual:** Every entry whose `model.id` equals the current session’s model (e.g. `claude-opus-4-6`) appears selected, regardless of provider. So switching to “nexus-a opus 4.6” causes “nexus-d opus 4.6”, “nexus-official opus 4.6”, etc. to also show as checked.

**Likely cause:** Selection state is keyed or compared by `modelId` only, instead of the full key `provider/modelId`. Session may store `modelOverride` + `providerOverride` (or equivalent), but the UI that draws the checkmarks probably compares only the model id against the list.

**Suggested fix:** When determining which list item is “current” for the session, compare using the full key `provider/modelId` (e.g. `nexus-d/claude-opus-4-6`), not just `modelId` (e.g. `claude-opus-4-6`). Persist and resolve overrides using the same full key where possible.

### Environment

- **OpenClaw version:** （填写，例如 2026.2.26 或运行 `openclaw --version` 输出）
- **OS:** macOS / Linux / Windows
- **Install method:** npm
- **Channel:** Telegram（或你用的渠道）

### Session snippet (sanitized)

One session entry from `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` (only model-related fields):

```json
{
  "modelOverride": "claude-opus-4-6",
  "providerOverride": "nexus"
}
```

Another session may have only `modelOverride` without `providerOverride`, or runtime fields like:

```json
{
  "modelProvider": "yunyi-claude",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
}
```

Config has several providers with the same model id, e.g. in `models.providers`: `nexus-d`, `nexus-a`, `nexus-official`, `yunyi-claude`, each with a model `id: "claude-opus-4-6"` and distinct `name` (e.g. "Claude Opus 4.6 (nexus-d)").

### Additional context

Related to model state sync: #18603, #19087. This issue is specifically about the **UI selection highlight** when multiple providers share the same model id; the fix should ensure the “selected” state is determined by `provider/modelId`, not `modelId` alone.
```

---

### 期望的 session 字段示例（供参考）

若上游改为「用完整 key 存 override」，session 里可以类似这样（仅示意）：

```json
{
  "modelOverride": "nexus-d/claude-opus-4-6"
}
```

或保留分字段但保证 UI 比较时用两者一起：

```json
{
  "providerOverride": "nexus-d",
  "modelOverride": "claude-opus-4-6"
}
```

这样 UI 在判断「当前项是否选中」时，用 `providerOverride + "/" + modelOverride`（或解析后的 provider+model）与列表里的 `provider/modelId` 做严格比较，就不会出现多个 4.6 同时勾选。


### Steps to reproduce

1. In `openclaw.json`, configure multiple providers that each expose a model with the same `id` (e.g. `nexus-d`, `nexus-a`, `nexus-official`, `yunyi-claude`, each with `claude-opus-4-6`).
2. Open a chat session and open the model switcher (/model list or Control UI model selector).
3. Select one provider’s model (e.g. “Claude Opus 4.6 (nexus-a)”).
4. Scroll or switch to another provider’s section in the same list.
5. Observe: the other provider’s “Claude Opus 4.6” (e.g. nexus-d, nexus-official) also shows as selected/checked, even though only one was chosen.

### Expected behavior

Actual behavior
*
What happened instead, including user-visible errors.

Write

No reply is posted; gateway logs "reply target not found".
Field can not be empty

Operating system

macOS 15.4 / Ubuntu 24.04 / Windows 11

Install method
How OpenClaw was installed or launched.

### Actual behavior

In the /model list (or Control UI model selector), after I select one provider's model (e.g. "Claude Opus 4.6 (nexus-a)"), every other provider's entry with the same model id also shows as selected/checked. Multiple items appear checked at once. No errors; only the selection highlight is wrong.

### OpenClaw version

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35484. The merge logic in `resolvePreferredTokenLimit` now honours user-explicit `contextWindow`/`maxTokens` values unconditionally, so Ollama users can lower `num_ctx` to fit constrained hardware.

Good news: this is already acknowledged as a bug, and there’s a fix in flight.

- @Sid-Qin filed PR #35484 to make `resolvePreferredTokenLimit` always honor user-explicit `contextWindow` / `maxTokens`, which is exactly what you need for Ollama on constrained RAM.

**What you can do now (until a release includes the PR):**
1) Keep your proxy workaround (rewriting `options.num_ctx`) — that’s currently the most reliable way to force a smaller KV cache.
2) Use a value OpenClaw will actually accept: OpenClaw enforces a hard minimum context window of **16,000 tokens**, so set `num_ctx >= 16000` (your `MAX_CTX=16000` is the right idea).

**Once the fix is released:**
- Set the model’s `contextWindow` (and optionally `maxTokens`) in `models.providers.ollama.models[]` and verify in the Ollama logs that the request is using the expected `num_ctx`/KV sizing.

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Configuration Guide: openclaw.json, Models, Gateway, Channels, and Plugins](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-configuration)
- [Ollama configured, but OpenClaw still uses Anthropic (or model discovery keeps failing)](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting/solutions/ollama-configured-but-falls-back-to-anthropic/)

If you want, paste the exact `models.providers.ollama` block you’re using (redact secrets) and I’ll suggest the smallest config diff that will work cleanly once #35484 lands.

Thanks for the quick turnaround on PR #35484 — that was fast.

Confirmed the root cause: OpenClaw was sending KvSize=262144 to the Ollama 
runner regardless of any config, causing a 2.5GB model to request 36GB RAM. 
The proxy workaround (rewriting num_ctx in the request body) confirmed the 
fix works at the right layer.

Current workaround: HTTP proxy on 11435 rewriting num_ctx to 16000 before 
forwarding to Ollama on 11434. Will swap to the config-based fix once #35484 lands.

Setup for context: RTX 2060 6GB VRAM, 16GB RAM, Windows 11, qwen3:4b (Q4_K_M). 
This config is pretty common for local model users so hopefully the fix helps others too.

Triage linkage update (2026-03-05): this appears to be the same core context/KV escalation path tracked in #33163 (ARM Ollama runner hangs under oversized effective context).

For fix coordination, recommend treating:
- #33163 as the broad impact/canonical crash report
- #35436 as the concrete reproduction for explicit low-context override behavior

Current active PR in this path is #35484, but it is not merge-ready yet (failing check + unresolved P1 review feedback).

title: [Bug]: Ollama: agent bootstrap hardcodes KvSize=262144 (128K context), ignoring all config attempts to lower num_ctx — unusable on <32GB RAM

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

In the Control UI “Core Files” panel, the Refresh button only reloads the file list metadata. It never re-fetches the active file’s contents, so any edits made from the CLI (or another editor) are invisible. Saving from the UI after that silently overwrites the newer file on disk.

Workaround
Hard-refresh the entire Control UI (Shift+Reload) or restart the gateway so the cached file contents are cleared, then reopen the file.

Proposed fix
Force a re-fetch of the active file when the Refresh button is clicked:

// ui/src/ui/app-render.ts
onLoadFiles: async (agentId) => {
const active = state.agentFileActive;
await loadAgentFiles(state, agentId);
if (active && resolvedAgentId === agentId) {
await loadAgentFileContent(state, agentId, active, {
force: true,
preserveDraft: false,
});
}
};

Patch: control-ui-refresh-source.patch (attached)
Built bundle diff comes from running corepack pnpm --filter openclaw-control-ui build.



### Steps to reproduce

Open Control UI → Agents → Core Files.
Select MEMORY.md (or any file). The contents load once.
In a shell, echo "test" >> MEMORY.md.
Click Refresh in the Core Files card.


### Expected behavior

The text area should update to show the new line or at least provide a way to reload the file body.

### Actual behavior

The file contents stay frozen at the first version that was loaded. The only way to see the new text is to reload the entire Control UI (clearing all state).

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw 2026.3.2 

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Repro is deterministic via the steps above. Example shell command:

echo "agent-web refresh test" >> /opt/agent-workspace/agent_web/MEMORY.md

After clicking Refresh in Control UI, the textarea still shows the old content. Saving from the UI removes the new line.
(No screenshots because the behavior is just the text area staying unchanged.)
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Anyone using Control UI → Agents → Core Files while also editing files via SSH/CLI.
Severity: High (silent data loss risk).
Frequency: 100% whenever Refresh is clicked after an external edit.
Consequence: Latest persona/instructions are overwritten unless the operator reloads the entire Control UI first.

### Additional information

First observed on 2026.3.2 (fresh install); earlier versions unverified.
Workaround: hard-refresh the Control UI (Shift+Reload) before editing, or avoid the Refresh button and reopen the page

comments.body:
Fixed in #36299.

**Root cause:** `onLoadFiles` (the Refresh button handler) called `loadAgentFiles()` which only reloads the file *list* metadata. It never re-fetched the active file's *contents*, so external edits remained invisible until a full page reload.

**Fix:** `onLoadFiles` now awaits `loadAgentFiles` and then — when a file is already selected — calls `loadAgentFileContent` with `{ force: true, preserveDraft: false }` to always pull the latest content from the gateway. This is essentially the same fix you proposed in the issue description.

title: [Bug]: Control UI - Core Files refresh never shows external edits

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary


**Version:** OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (also reproduced on 2026.3.1)
**OS:** Windows 11, Node 24.14.0
**Install method:** npm global (`npm install -g openclaw`)

**Summary:**
The bundled Chrome extension (`assets/chrome-extension/background.js`) sends a `nonce` property in its WebSocket connect params, which the gateway rejects with:
```
invalid connect params: at root: unexpected property 'nonce'; at /client/id: must be equal to constant; at /client/id: must match a schema in anyOf
```
This makes the browser relay completely non-functional — the extension cannot connect to the gateway regardless of token or port configuration.


### Steps to reproduce


**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Install OpenClaw 2026.3.2 via npm
2. Run `openclaw gateway start`
3. Run `openclaw browser extension install` and load the unpacked extension in Chrome
4. Enter gateway port and token in the extension popup and click Save
5. Connection is rejected immediately

**Gateway log error:**
```
closed before connect — code=1008
reason=invalid connect params: at root: unexpected property 'nonce'
```

**Root cause (confirmed):**
In `assets/chrome-extension/background.js` (line ~341 and ~360), the extension constructs a connect payload that includes `nonce: nonce || undefined`. The gateway schema does not accept `nonce` as a valid property and rejects the handshake.

**Workaround:** None found. The extension code in the npm package itself contains the incompatible `nonce` field — manually reinstalling or copying extension files does not help.


### Expected behavior


**Expected behavior:** The extension should connect successfully after entering the correct port and gateway token.

**Additional context:**
- Gateway responds correctly to direct HTTP requests with the token (returns 200 OK)
- Token and port are confirmed correct in `openclaw.json`
- `openclaw doctor --fix` was run successfully as Administrator
- Issue persists across both 2026.3.1 and 2026.3.2

### Actual behavior

The bundled Chrome extension (`assets/chrome-extension/background.js`) sends a `nonce` property in its WebSocket connect params, which the gateway rejects with:
```
invalid connect params: at root: unexpected property 'nonce'; at /client/id: must be equal to constant; at /client/id: must match a schema in anyOf
```

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

 Windows 11, Node 24.14.0

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Fixed in #36290.

**Root cause:** `ensureGatewayHandshakeStarted()` extracted the `nonce` from the `connect.challenge` payload and sent it as a top-level field in the gateway connect params. The connect schema (`ConnectParamsSchema`) has `additionalProperties: false` and does not allow a top-level `nonce` — that field only belongs inside `device` (used for device-signature auth). The extension uses token-based auth only, so the challenge nonce is irrelevant.

**Fix:** Removed `nonce` from the connect params. The extension now sends only the fields the schema accepts.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: **Bug Report: Chrome Extension handshake rejected by gateway (nonce mismatch)**

### Operating system

Debian 13

### Install method

podman

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

_No response_

_No response_

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35411. The `podman build` command was missing `--build-arg` flags for `OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` and `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`. Now mirrors the same flags used in `docker-setup.sh`.

Looks like two things are at play here:

1) `setup-podman.sh` currently calls `podman build ...` **without** passing any `--build-arg OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES=...`, so the Dockerfile’s `ARG OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES` stays empty.
2) Because you’re running via `sudo`, your exported env vars may not even reach the script unless you use `sudo -E` (or `sudo env VAR=... ...`).

**Immediate workaround (until a release includes the fix):**
- Patch the build line in `setup-podman.sh` to include build args, then run it with env preserved, e.g.
  - `sudo -E OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES="ffmpeg ..." ./setup-podman.sh`
  - and ensure the `podman build` invocation includes:
    - `--build-arg "OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES=${OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES}"`

**Upstream status:**
- PR #35411 already adds the missing `--build-arg` flags (and also mirrors `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`). Once that’s released, `setup-podman.sh` should behave like `docker-setup.sh` here.

Recommended reading:
- [OpenClaw Installation Troubleshooting: Node/NPM, PATH, Windows (WSL2), and Docker](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-installation-troubleshooting)
- [Install OpenClaw on Linux - Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch Setup Guide](https://coclaw.com/getting-started/linux)

If you share the exact `podman build ...` line from your current `setup-podman.sh` (or confirm you’re on 2026.3.3 exactly), I can point to the precise one-line change.
Submitted a fix in PR #35818. setup-podman now forwards OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES to podman build via --build-arg so Dockerfile package installs are applied.

title: [Bug]: OPENCLAW_DOCKER_APT_PACKAGES is not obeyed in setup-podman.sh

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When switching models in an existing session, `contextTokens` can become "stuck" at a lower value.

Example:
- Model A context window: ~198k
- Switch to Model B context window: ~160k
- Switch back to Model A
- Session still reports/uses ~160k context window (in session file and runtime behavior)

This causes premature compaction and incorrect context accounting.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure two models with different context windows (for example 198k and 160k).
2. Start a session (OpenClaw TUI / gateway-client).
3. Use Model A (198k).
4. Switch to Model B (160k).
5. Switch back to Model A (198k).
6. Check `/status` and/or session store file (`contextTokens`).

### Expected behavior

- Context window should update based on the **currently selected provider/model** each time.
- After switching back to Model A, context should return to ~198k

### Actual behavior

- `contextTokens` remains at the lower value (~160k) and appears effectively hardcoded in session state.
- Switching back to higher-context model does not restore the larger context window in that session.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
n/a
```

### Impact and severity

- Early compaction / pruning
- Incorrect status reporting
- Reduced usable context in long-running sessions

### Additional information

This looks related to previous context-window cache/provider-resolution issues:
- #27394
- #27439
- #15632
- #27500
- #34511
- #9382

Could be a regression or another path where session state keeps stale `contextTokens` after model switch

comments.body:
I can reproduce this on `OpenClaw 2026.3.2` with an `npm global` install on `Ubuntu 24.04.4`, and I have a concrete provider/model-switch example that shows the stale value clearly.

Repro variant:
1. Configure one model with a larger context window (`infini/kimi-k2.5`, `contextWindow=262144`).
2. Configure another model with a smaller context window (`infini/deepseek-v3.2-thinking`, `contextWindow=131072`).
3. Reuse the same long-lived session (in my case: Telegram direct session).
4. Switch the session model to `infini/deepseek-v3.2-thinking`.
5. Run `/status`.

Observed:
- `/status` shows the active model correctly as `infini/deepseek-v3.2-thinking`
- but the context denominator still shows the previous Kimi-sized value (`262k`), e.g.

```text
🧠 Model: infini/deepseek-v3.2-thinking
📚 Context: 27k/262k (10%)
```

Session-store evidence from `agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` at the same time:

```json
{
  "modelProvider": "infini",
  "model": "deepseek-v3.2-thinking",
  "contextTokens": 262144,
  "totalTokens": 27457
}
```

Expected:
- once the active model is `infini/deepseek-v3.2-thinking`, the session context should be `131072`
- `/status` should therefore show `27k/131k`, not `27k/262k`

Additional evidence:
- the configured model metadata was already correct in both `openclaw.json` and runtime `models.json`
- manually changing that session entry's `contextTokens` from `262144` to `131072` and restarting `openclaw-gateway` makes `/status` show the correct denominator immediately

So this looks consistent with session model switching updating `model` / `modelProvider` but leaving a stale `sessionEntry.contextTokens` behind, which `/status` then prefers over the current model's `contextWindow`.

title: [Bug]: Session contextTokens gets stuck at lower model limit after model switch (e.g. 198k -> 160k -> stays 160k)

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Description
I noticed a breaking change/regression when upgrading from OpenClaw v3.1 to v3.2. Several built-in tools related to file reading and execution that were available by default in v3.1 appear to be missing or disabled in v3.2 without explicit configuration.
Steps to Reproduce
Install/OpenClaw v3.1.
Observe that built-in tools for reading files and executing commands are available out-of-the-box.
Upgrade to (or fresh install) OpenClaw v3.2.
Attempt to use the same file read/execute capabilities.
The system fails to recognize these tools or reports them as unavailable.
Expected Behavior
The core built-in tools for file reading and execution (e.g., fs.readFile, system.exec, or similar internal commands) should remain available by default in v3.2, ensuring backward compatibility with workflows created in v3.1.
Actual Behavior
These tools are not present in the default toolset in v3.2. It seems they might have been moved to an optional plugin/skill package or removed entirely for security reasons, but this change was not clearly documented as a migration step.
Environment
OS: macOS (Darwin)
OpenClaw Version: 3.2 (Working) vs 3.1 (Broken/Previous)
Node Version: [Insert your Node version here, e.g., v20.11.0]
Additional Context
Did this change intend to improve security by forcing users to explicitly enable dangerous tools?
If so, is there a specific configuration flag or a specific "skill" package we need to install to restore this functionality?
A migration guide or release note highlighting this removal would be very helpful for existing users.
Thank you for maintaining this amazing project!


### Steps to reproduce

Let OpenClaw search for skills on ClawHub and install them,

"Return to no valid reading and writing tools."

### Expected behavior

Call the read-write tool and complete the instruction

### Actual behavior

"Return to no relevant tools"

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Macos 26.1 (25B78)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

I'm using the Qwen 3.5 35B model running locally via llama-server. It has been working great for the past couple of days but frequently shows the error "Invalid diff: now finding less tool calls!" on version 2026.3.2. I tried turning off some of the tools I suspected were causing the issue, but the problem persists. It seems like this happens to a lot of local LLMs, not just Qwen 3.5.
"tools": {

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

I suspect the core issue is Proxy Environment Isolation. Even though I successfully installed Chrome or Brave via the terminal by manually specifying the proxy, OpenClaw launches Playwright in a "clean" sandbox that does not inherit these system settings. Consequently, the browser attempts a direct connection instead of routing through my corporate proxy server, causing the firewall to immediately sever the connection (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET).

### Steps to reproduce

  "browser": {
    "enabled": true,
    "remoteCdpTimeoutMs": 10000,
    "remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs": 10000,
    "color": "#FF4500",
    "headless": true,
    "noSandbox": true,
    "attachOnly": false,
    "executablePath": "/usr/bin/brave-browser",
    "profiles": {
      "openclaw": {
        "cdpPort": 18800,
        "color": "#FF4500"
      }
    }
  },


### Expected behavior

Work

### Actual behavior

Openclaw chat reply ERR_CONNECTION_RESET

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When OpenClaw's webchat heartbeat polling triggers an agent response, the system incorrectly sets the delivery context to the Feishu channel and attempts to deliver messages to `heartbeat` as a Feishu user ID, causing the Feishu API to return an invalid user ID error.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure multiple channels (webchat + feishu)
2. Bind main agent to feishu channel via bindings config
3. Webchat heartbeat polling sends a message with `from: "heartbeat"`, `provider: "webchat"`
4. Main agent responds to the heartbeat message
5. System incorrectly sets `deliveryContext.channel` to `"feishu"`
6. Message delivery fails and enters delivery-queue for retry loop

### Expected behavior

1. Webchat heartbeat messages should NOT trigger cross-channel delivery
2. The `heartbeat` placeholder should be recognized and skipped for delivery
3. `deliveryContext.channel` should follow the message origin channel, not be overwritten by agent bindings

### Actual behavior

Feishu API returns error: `Invalid ids: [heartbeat]`. The message delivery fails and enters delivery-queue, causing retry loops on every gateway restart. Error log shows:
```
code: 99992360
msg: The request you send is not a valid {user_id} or not exists
```

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.1

### Operating system

Windows 11

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Session state in sessions.json shows:

{
  "agent:main:main": {
    "origin": {
      "from": "heartbeat",
      "to": "heartbeat",
      "provider": "webchat"
    },
    "deliveryContext": {
      "channel": "feishu"  // Should be "webchat" or no delivery
    }
  }
}


Delivery queue entry:

{
  "to": "heartbeat",
  "accountId": "main",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:feishu:direct:heartbeat",
  "text": "..."
}
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Users with multi-channel setup (webchat + feishu)
Severity: Medium (causes API errors and retry loops)
Frequency: 100% repro when heartbeat triggers agent response
Consequence: Gateway logs fill with errors, delivery-queue accumulates failed entries

### Additional information

**Root Cause:** The `deliveryContext` inheritance logic incorrectly uses the agent's channel binding instead of the message origin channel.

**Workaround:** Disable or reduce heartbeat task frequency to avoid triggering this bug.

**Suggested Fix:**
1. Heartbeat messages (`from: "heartbeat"`) should be identified as system messages and not trigger message delivery
2. `deliveryContext` should be derived from message origin (`provider` field), not from agent's channel bindings
3. Add validation to prevent using placeholder IDs like "heartbeat" as real user IDs
title: Webchat Heartbeat Message Incorrectly Delivered to Feishu

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

In Control UI, tool toggles can be changed visually, but Save is disabled or changes do not persist. “Enable all/Disable all” and selective toggling are unreliable.

### Steps to reproduce

Steps to reproduce:

Open Dashboard (http://127.0.0.1:18789/)
Go to tool settings
Toggle off 1-2 tools (or click Enable all/Disable all)
Attempt to save
Expected:
Tool settings save and persist after refresh/restart.

Actual:
Save button is disabled or changes are not persisted.

What I already tried:

Removed restrictive tools profile (tools.profile: "messaging"), set tools: {}
openclaw config validate passes
openclaw gateway restart
Hard refresh (Ctrl+F5)
Incognito/private window
Confirmed config path is same for CLI and service (~/.openclaw/openclaw.json)
Diagnostics:

openclaw config get tools returns {}
Logs show repeated config.get from UI, but no successful save/write event when toggling tools
Earlier had a transient config parse error during manual edits, now fixed and config valid
Impact:
Cannot manage tool access from UI; must edit config file manually as workaround.

Workaround:
Edit ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json directly + restart gateway.

### Expected behavior

If the bug didn’t exist, expected behavior is:

- You toggle tools (or click **Enable all / Disable all**)
- **Save** becomes enabled immediately
- Clicking **Save** succeeds with confirmation
- Refreshing dashboard keeps the same state
- `openclaw config get tools` reflects exactly what you saved
- Restarting gateway/session still preserves those settings
- New sessions enforce the updated tool permissions right away

That’s the baseline “working” behavior you should use in the bug report’s **Expected** section.

### Actual behavior

- Tool switches can be toggled in UI, but **Save** stays disabled (or does nothing).
- **Enable all / Disable all** appears to change the UI state but doesn’t persist.
- After refresh/reopen, tool settings revert or remain unchanged.
- CLI still shows neutral config (`openclaw config get tools` → `{}`), i.e., UI edits are not being committed.
- Net effect: tool control from dashboard is unreliable; only manual config edits work.

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

Windows 10.0.19045 (x64), Node 24.14.0

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

_No response_
