body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

   ## Bug: TUI messages routed to Discord DM instead of TUI                                                             
                                                                                                                        
   ### Description                                                                                                      
   When sending messages via TUI (openclaw-tui), the bot responds in Discord DM instead of the TUI channel. This causes 
 a routing issue where:                                                                                                 
   - User sends message from TUI (`sender: openclaw-tui`)                                                               
   - Bot replies to Discord DM instead of TUI                                                                           
                                                                                                                        
   ### Environment                                                                                                      
   - OpenClaw version: [latest]                                                                                         
   - Channels: Discord (enabled) + TUI                                                                                  
   - OS: macOS                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                        
                                           
                                                                                                                        
                            
                                                                                                                        
                                                                              
                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                        
                                                                    
                                                                                                                        


### Steps to reproduce

   1. Have both Discord and TUI configured                                                                              
   2. Send a message via TUI                                                                                            
   3. Observe that the response appears in Discord DM, not TUI          

### Expected behavior

   When sending a message via TUI, the response should come back to TUI, not Discord DM.                                
                                                                                                                        

### Actual behavior

- Message source: `openclaw-tui` (TUI)                                                                               
   - Reply destination: Discord DM                                                                                      
                                                                

### OpenClaw version

OpenClaw version: [latest]  

### Operating system

macOS  Tahoe 26.3

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

 - This issue persists even when trying to use only TUI (without Discord)                                               
 - Discord DM conversations work normally when accessed directly                                                        
 - TUI seems to be routing through Discord channel incorrectly

comments.body:
Thank you for the detailed root cause analysis! This is exactly what I was
looking for.

The suggested fix makes sense - the message source channel should take
priority over agent bindings. I've confirmed this issue affects my setup as
well:

- OpenClaw version: latest
- Channels: Discord (enabled) + TUI
- OS: macOS

The workaround mentioned (Priority 1: preserve source channel) aligns with
the expected behavior - messages should always reply to their source
channel unless explicitly routed elsewhere.
```


qiuyuemartin-max ***@***.***>于2026年3月5日 周四13:58写道：

> *qiuyuemartin-max* left a comment (openclaw/openclaw#35254)
> <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/35254#issuecomment-4002435809>
> Root Cause Analysis & Fix Suggestion Problem Summary
>
> When sending messages via TUI (openclaw-tui), the bot incorrectly
> responds in Discord DM instead of the TUI channel. This is a message
> routing regression affecting multi-channel setups.
>
> *Observed behavior*:
>
>    - User sends message from TUI (sender: openclaw-tui)
>    - Bot replies to Discord DM instead of TUI
>
> ------------------------------
> Root Cause
>
> *Location*: Message delivery context routing logic (likely
> src/messaging/delivery.ts or channel dispatch)
>
> *Issue*: The deliveryContext.channel is being overwritten or incorrectly
> set based on agent configuration, ignoring the original message source
> channel.
>
> *Likely causes*:
>
>    1. Agent has Discord bindings configured
>    2. Routing logic prioritizes agent channel bindings over message source
>    3. deliveryContext is not properly preserved from inbound message
>
> ------------------------------
> Suggested Fix
>
> Preserve the original message source channel when determining reply
> destination:
> Conceptual Fix
>
> // When preparing reply delivery contextfunction getReplyDeliveryContext(inboundMessage, agentConfig) {
>   // Priority 1: Use the inbound message's channel (preserve source)
>   if (inboundMessage.deliveryContext?.channel) {
>     return {
>       channel: inboundMessage.deliveryContext.channel,
>       to: inboundMessage.deliveryContext.to,
>       accountId: inboundMessage.deliveryContext.accountId,
>     };
>   }
>
>   // Priority 2: Fall back to agent default bindings
>   return getDefaultDeliveryContext(agentConfig);}
>
> Key Principles
>
>    1. *Message source wins*: Always reply to the channel where the
>    message originated
>    2. *Agent bindings as fallback*: Only use when no source context exists
>    3. *Explicit overrides only*: Only change channel if explicitly
>    requested (e.g., cross-channel routing)
>
> ------------------------------
> Verification Steps Test Setup
>
> {
>   channels: {
>     discord: { enabled: true },
>     // TUI is always available
>   },
>   agents: {
>     list: [
>       {
>         id: "main",
>         bindings: [
>           { channel: "discord", target: "user:123" }
>         ]
>       }
>     ]
>   }}
>
> Before Fix
>
> openclaw-tui> hello# ❌ Response appears in Discord DM
>
> After Fix
>
> openclaw-tui> hello# ✅ Response appears in TUI
>
> ------------------------------
> Impact
>
> *Severity*: High
> *Type*: Regression (worked before, now fails)
>
> *Affected users*:
>
>    - All users with multi-channel setups (TUI + Discord/Telegram/etc.)
>    - Users relying on TUI for development/testing
>
> *Workaround*: Remove Discord bindings from agent config (not acceptable)
>
> *Benefits of fix*:
>
>    - ✅ Restores correct message routing behavior
>    - ✅ TUI works reliably in multi-channel environments
>    - ✅ Message source channel is always respected
>
> ------------------------------
> Additional Context
>
> *Similar issues*:
>
>    - #35300 <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/35300>: Webchat
>    heartbeat incorrectly routed to Feishu (same root cause)
>    - Both involve deliveryContext.channel being overwritten by agent
>    bindings
>
> *Expected behavior*:
>
>    - Messages should ALWAYS reply to their source channel unless
>    explicitly routed elsewhere
>    - Agent bindings should not override inbound message context
>
> ------------------------------
>
> *Environment*:
>
>    - OpenClaw: latest
>    - OS: macOS
>    - Channels: Discord (enabled) + TUI
>
> ------------------------------
> Implementation Priority
>
> *Estimated effort*: < 15 lines
> *Risk*: Low (restores expected behavior)
> *User impact*: High (blocks TUI usage in multi-channel setups)
> ------------------------------
>
> *Analysis based on root-cause methodology from #35024
> <https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/35024>. AI-assisted
> contribution (OpenClaw Agent C).*

### Summary

Google Chat inbound webhooks reach /googlechat but are rejected with 401 Unauthorized before pairing, even when JWT aud exactly matches configured app-url audience.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure channels.googlechat with audienceType=app-url and audience=<public>/googlechat.
2. Verify Google Chat app config uses the same HTTP endpoint URL and app status is LIVE.
3. Send DM from allowed business user to the bot in Google Chat.
4. Check ngrok request inspector + openclaw pairing list + openclaw channels status --probe.

### Expected behavior

Inbound webhook should authenticate successfully and proceed to DM pairing/handling (or generate pairing code when unpaired sender messages).

### Actual behavior

Webhook is delivered to /googlechat (confirmed in ngrok), but OpenClaw returns HTTP 401 Unauthorized. No pairing code appears (openclaw pairing list is empty). Channel status remains: running, dm:pairing, probe failed.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Windows 10 (10.0.19045)

### Install method

npm global (openclaw CLI)

```shell
Receipts: ngrok request inspector shows POST /googlechat from Google-gsuiteaddons with JWT aud=https://272b-68-187-109-50.ngrok-free.app/googlechat; gateway response is 401 Unauthorized. openclaw pairing list returns no pending googlechat pairing requests. channels status --probe shows running, dm:pairing, probe failed.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Google Chat DM users for this deployment.
Severity: High (blocks inbound messaging end-to-end).

Consequence: Bot cannot receive user DMs; pairing never triggers; channel unusable for inbound communication.

### Additional information

Tried audienceType app-url and project-number; both rejected inbound for this environment (app-url token aud matched exactly). Multiple channel and gateway restarts did not resolve. Plugin is loaded and webhook receives traffic, so failure appears to be in auth validation path before pairing.

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

When using `@mem0/openclaw-mem0` in `open-source` mode, every `recall` and `capture` call 
fails at runtime with a native binding resolution error. The plugin registers and initializes 
successfully, but is completely non-functional.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install `@mem0/openclaw-mem0` v0.1.2
2. Configure in `openclaw.json` with `mode: open-source`, Ollama embedder, Qdrant vector store
3. Start a conversation
4. Run `openclaw logs | grep mem0` — observe `recall failed` and `capture failed` on every turn

### Expected behavior

Recall and capture should work. If `sqlite3` history is unavailable, the plugin should 
gracefully degrade and continue operating with the configured vector store (Qdrant/Chroma).

### Actual behavior

The binding lookup is resolving paths relative to OpenClaw's own `jiti` module directory 
rather than the plugin's installation directory. The plugin itself does not depend on 
`node_sqlite3` — OpenClaw's Jiti-based plugin loader is incorrectly anchoring the native 
binding search path.

Setting a custom `historyDbPath` in plugin config does **not** resolve the issue. 
The problem is in the binding path resolution, not the db file location.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS (Apple Silicon)

### Install method

npm globe

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
openclaw-mem0: recall failed: Error: Could not locate the bindings file. Tried:
→ /Users/xiang/.nvm/versions/node/v22.18.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules/jiti/build/node_sqlite3.node
→ .../jiti/build/Release/node_sqlite3.node
→ .../jiti/compiled/22.18.0/darwin/arm64/node_sqlite3.node
... (13 paths total, all in openclaw/node_modules/jiti/)
```

### Impact and severity

`autoRecall` and `autoCapture` are completely broken in OSS mode on this platform. 
The plugin silently fails on every conversation turn, making persistent memory 
non-functional despite successful initialization.

### Additional information

The Jiti plugin loader should either correctly resolve native binding paths relative to the 
plugin's own `node_modules`, or OpenClaw should ensure `node_sqlite3` bindings are bundled 
and resolvable for plugins that depend on them. Alternatively, making sqlite history opt-in 
in the mem0 plugin (so Qdrant-backed memory can operate independently) would also unblock users.

comments.body:
I've investigated this issue. The problem is in how Jiti resolves native module bindings.

**Root Cause:**
In `src/plugins/loader.ts`, Jiti is created with `import.meta.url` as the base:
```ts
jitiLoader = createJiti(import.meta.url, { ... });
```

This causes Jiti to search for native bindings (like `node_sqlite3`) relative to OpenClaw's installation directory instead of the plugin's directory.

**Suggested Fix:**
The plugin loader should either:
1. Create a separate Jiti instance for each plugin with its own base path
2. Or use `module.parent`/dirname resolution to find the plugin's node_modules

**Potential Solution:**
Modify `src/plugins/loader.ts` to pass the plugin's directory as the base for Jiti:
```ts
// Instead of:
jitiLoader = createJiti(import.meta.url, { ...});

// Use plugin-specific base:
jitiLoader = createJiti(pluginEntryPoint, { ...});
```

Would you like me to implement this fix?

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: Plugin native bindings (node_sqlite3) fail to load under Jiti loader, breaking mem0 OSS mode recall/capture entirely

Triage summary (as of 2026-03-05): this report appears to be an umbrella of multiple regressions rather than a single defect.

Impact:
- High user impact (core workflow blocked: read/write/exec unavailable, plus auth/UI failures).
- Multiple independent confirmations in this thread.

Likely split by root cause:

1) Missing tools/read-write-exec regression (onboarding/tools profile path)
- Related issues: #32991, #33225, #33419, #33871, #34629, #34810, #35350
- Related PRs: #34958, #34957, #34861, #34827, #34695, #33231, #33323, #32969, #32878
- Current best candidate: #34958 (CLEAN, checks passing)

2) 401 auth/header regression
- Related issues: #34830, #34656
- Related PRs: #34842, #34917, #34766, #34740
- Current best candidate: #34842 (CLEAN, checks passing)

3) Control UI tool-toggle persistence instability (may explain some "still broken after toggling" reports)
- Related issue: #35297
- Related PR: #35338 (CLEAN, checks passing)

Triage recommendation:
- Track #35077 as umbrella only, with fixes landing in the child issues above.
- If maintainers prefer strict dedupe, close this issue as duplicate after linking canonical child issues.
- For currently impacted users (tool-missing path), temporary workaround remains:
  `openclaw config set tools.profile coding && openclaw gateway restart`

What you’re seeing matches a concrete regression/behavior change in 2026.3.2 installs: the default config can come up with a restrictive tools profile ("messaging"), which makes the agent report **“no tools”** and blocks `exec` / file read/write.

### Fast path to get unblocked
1) Switch to a tools profile that includes filesystem/exec, then restart the gateway:

```bash
openclaw config set tools.profile coding
openclaw gateway restart
```

(If you still can’t run what you need, try `tools.profile=full` as a temporary workaround.)

2) In Control UI, make sure you’re actually connected with a valid gateway token (use the tokenized dashboard URL or set the token in UI settings). The “401 missing auth headers” symptom is usually just “UI is talking to the gateway without auth”.

**Why this works:** tool availability is policy-driven; if the active profile denies `exec`/files, the model literally cannot call them, and workflows that depend on tools will look “totally broken”.

If you paste your (redacted) `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` `tools` + `gateway.auth` blocks, it’s easy to confirm you’re on the intended profile/auth mode.

Recommended reading:
- [New User Checklist: Secure Setup, Backups, and First Week Wins](https://coclaw.com/guides/new-user-checklist)
- [OpenClaw Config Generator | CoClaw](https://coclaw.com/openclaw-config-generator)

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

The channel health monitor uses a fixed 30-minute staleEventThresholdMs to detect dead sockets, then force-restarts the provider. This threshold works for high-traffic channels (Slack/Discord) but iMessage can be legitimately quiet for hours. Result: 59 unnecessary provider restarts in 2 days, causing delivery gaps during active conversations. The restart cascade compounds — each restart resets the "last event" timer, so if no message arrives quickly after restart, the next 5-minute health check can flag it stale again almost immediately.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure OpenClaw with an iMessage channel on macOS.
2. Leave the gateway running for any period with no inbound/outbound iMessage activity (30+ minutes).
3. Check gateway.log — the iMessage provider will be restarted every ~30 minutes.
4. Send a message during or immediately after a restart — delivery may be delayed.

### Expected behavior

The health monitor should not restart a provider that is functioning correctly just because the channel has been quiet. iMessage can go hours between messages without any socket problem. A per-channel configurable staleEventThresholdMs or provider-level keepalive pings would prevent false positives.

### Actual behavior

The iMessage provider is killed and restarted every ~30 minutes continuously, 24/7, regardless of actual socket health. During active conversations, these restarts cause transient delivery failures — outbound messages queue behind the restart cycle. Observed 59 stale-socket restarts in a 48-hour period. Restarts cascade: each restart resets the last-event timer, so subsequent 5-minute health checks can trigger another restart almost immediately.

### OpenClaw version

v2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 15.4 (Apple Silicon M4)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Sample from gateway.log showing continuous 30-minute restarts:


2026-03-04T22:04:10.274Z [health-monitor] [imessage:default] health-monitor: restarting (reason: stale-socket)
2026-03-04T22:29:10.287Z [health-monitor] [imessage:default] health-monitor: restarting (reason: stale-socket)
2026-03-04T22:34:10.294Z [health-monitor] [imessage:default] health-monitor: restarting (reason: stale-socket)


Source: DEFAULT_STALE_EVENT_THRESHOLD_MS = 30 * 6e4 in channel-health-monitor.ts
No per-channel config path exists — deps.timing?.staleEventThresholdMs is checked but never populated from openclaw.json.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All iMessage channel users on always-on gateway deployments
Severity: High — blocks message delivery during active conversations
Frequency: 100% repro — happens every 30 minutes on any idle iMessage channel
Consequence: Missed/delayed message delivery, restart cascades during conversations, noisy logs (59 restarts in 48 hours obscure real issues)

### Additional information

Proposed fix: (A) Make staleEventThresholdMs configurable per channel in openclaw.json, (B) Add iMessage-specific keepalive pings so idle sockets don't appear dead, or (C) both. This issue compounds with auto-compaction delivery blocking — when compaction runs during a stale-socket restart window, the user can wait 7+ minutes for a response that was already generated.

comments.body:
We hit this exact same pattern on Telegram with a 9-agent setup — 270+ false stale-socket restarts per day across our bots. Filed #33147 for the Telegram side.

Two fixes are in flight that should help here too:

- **PR #33209** by @Sid-Qin adds `hasActiveResponses` to the health snapshot so the monitor skips channels with in-flight responses
- **@byungsker** identified the root cause: when `lastEventAt` is null (channel never received a message), `eventAge = now - 0` always exceeds the threshold, so idle channels are permanently flagged as stale

For iMessage specifically, your suggestion of a per-channel configurable `staleEventThresholdMs` makes a lot of sense — Telegram and iMessage have fundamentally different traffic patterns than Slack/Discord. A 30-minute threshold is way too aggressive for low-traffic channels.

As a temporary workaround, we added a recovery cron that detects restart loops and backs off, but the real fix needs to be in the health monitor logic itself.

Submitted a fix in PR #35499. Added `gateway.channelStaleEventThresholdMinutes` config option so users can tune how long a channel may be idle before being considered stale. Default remains 30 minutes; iMessage users can set e.g. 240 (4 hours) to prevent unnecessary restarts.

title: [Bug]: iMessage channel health monitor restarts provider every 30 minutes during normal idle periods

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

When gateway.auth.mode is set to "token", agent tool calls (e.g., sessions_spawn, cron, sessions_list) fail with a 10-second timeout and "device-required" error. The web UI (Control UI) works fine because it uses a different authentication path that respects dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth, but the agent tool layer uses a separate code path that doesn't properly resolve gateway credentials from environment variables.

Environment
• OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2
• OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Linux x86_64)
• Gateway auth mode: token
• Environment variables set: OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD
-OR-
• Environment variables set: OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure gateway with auth.mode: "token" and set OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var
2. Start gateway: openclaw gateway start
3. Send a message to an agent via Telegram (or any non-webchat channel)
4. Have the agent invoke a tool that calls the gateway, e.g.:

sessions_spawn({
runtime: "subagent",
task: "test"
})

### Expected behavior

The agent should successfully authenticate to the gateway's tool WebSocket using the OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN environment variable (or config), and the tool call should complete and allow spawning of subagents

### Actual behavior

Error: gateway timeout after 10000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Source: local loopback
Config: /home/openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Bind: loopback

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Linux x86_64)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
JSON

{
"cause": "device-required",
"handshake": "failed",
"durationMs": 10,
"lastFrameType": "req",
"lastFrameMethod": "connect",
...
}
Root Cause Analysis

The code path for agent tool calls (callGatewayTool):

1. callGatewayTool(method, opts, params) is invoked
2. Calls resolveGatewayOptions$1(opts) to get gateway config
3. resolveGatewayOptions$1 only checks opts?.gatewayToken — no env/config fallback
4. Returns { token: undefined, ... }
5. GatewayClient is created without a token
6. sendConnect() sends auth payload without token
7. Gateway rejects with device-required (because mode: token requires auth)

The code path for CLI/web UI (callGatewayWithScopes):

1. Calls resolveGatewayCredentials(context)
2. Which calls resolveGatewayCredentialsWithEnv(context, process.env)
3. Properly reads OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN from env via readGatewayTokenEnv(env)
4. Returns { token: "...", ... }
5. Connection succeeds

Key finding: dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth only applies to Control UI connections (isControlUi === true), not to agent tool WebSocket connections.

Relevant Code Locations

> Rachael:
Here's the bug report ready to copy-paste:

───

Bug Report: Agent tool calls fail with "device-required" when gateway.auth.mode="token"

Summary

When gateway.auth.mode is set to "token", agent tool calls (e.g., sessions_spawn, cron, sessions_list) fail with a 10-second timeout and "device-required" error. The web UI (Control UI) works fine because it uses a different authentication path that respects dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth, but the agent tool layer uses a separate code path that doesn't properly resolve gateway credentials from environment variables.

Environment

• OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2
• OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Linux x86_64)
• Gateway auth mode: token
• Environment variables set: OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD

Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure gateway with auth.mode: "token" and set OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN env var
2. Start gateway: openclaw gateway start
3. Send a message to an agent via Telegram (or any non-webchat channel)
4. Have the agent invoke a tool that calls the gateway, e.g.:

sessions_spawn({
runtime: "subagent",
task: "test"
})

Expected Behavior

The agent should successfully authenticate to the gateway's tool WebSocket using the OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN environment variable (or config), and the tool call should complete.

Actual Behavior

Error: gateway timeout after 10000ms
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Source: local loopback
Config: /home/openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Bind: loopback

Gateway logs show:

{
"cause": "device-required",
"handshake": "failed",
"durationMs": 10,
"lastFrameType": "req",
"lastFrameMethod": "connect",
...
}

Root Cause Analysis

The code path for agent tool calls (callGatewayTool):

1. callGatewayTool(method, opts, params) is invoked
2. Calls resolveGatewayOptions$1(opts) to get gateway config
3. resolveGatewayOptions$1 only checks opts?.gatewayToken — no env/config fallback
4. Returns { token: undefined, ... }
5. GatewayClient is created without a token
6. sendConnect() sends auth payload without token
7. Gateway rejects with device-required (because mode: token requires auth)

The code path for CLI/web UI (callGatewayWithScopes):

1. Calls resolveGatewayCredentials(context)
2. Which calls resolveGatewayCredentialsWithEnv(context, process.env)
3. Properly reads OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN from env via readGatewayTokenEnv(env)
4. Returns { token: "...", ... }
5. Connection succeeds

Key finding: dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth only applies to Control UI connections (isControlUi === true), not to agent tool WebSocket connections.

Relevant Code Locations

File: dist/plugin-sdk/reply-DFFRlayb.js (and similar bundled files)

// Bug: resolveGatewayOptions$1 doesn't resolve credentials from env/config
function resolveGatewayOptions$1(opts) {
const cfg = loadConfig();
const explicitToken = trimToUndefined$1(opts?.gatewayToken);
const token = validatedOverride ? resolveGatewayOverrideToken({...}) : explicitToken;
// Missing: fallback to readGatewayTokenEnv(process.env) or resolveGatewayCredentials()
return { url: validatedOverride?.url, token, timeoutMs };
}

// Working: CLI path properly resolves credentials
async function resolveGatewayCredentials(context) {
return resolveGatewayCredentialsWithEnv(context, process.env);
}

async function resolveGatewayCredentialsWithEnv(context, env) {
const envToken = readGatewayTokenEnv(env); // Reads OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN
const envPassword = readGatewayPassword
```

### Impact and severity

• Severity: High — breaks core functionality (sub-agents, cron, session management) for non-webchat channels
• Affected tools: sessions_spawn, sessions_list, sessions_history, cron, gateway, nodes, etc.
• Workaround available: Yes (auth mode "none"), but with security trade-off

### Additional information

Trying to spawn subagents from telegram sessions

comments.body: Submitted a fix in PR #35040. fix(agents): honor gateway token env/config in tool calls.

We run 9 agents on Telegram (each with their own bot token) and spawn subagents regularly — can confirm this works on our setup with `gateway.auth.mode` at the default setting.

Great root cause analysis on the `resolveGatewayOptions$1` vs `resolveGatewayCredentials` code path divergence. The fact that the CLI/web UI properly resolves env vars but the agent tool layer doesn't is a clear oversight in the auth resolution chain.

For anyone hitting this before the fix lands: if you're comfortable with it on a local/trusted network, the workaround is to avoid `auth.mode: "token"` for now. Not ideal from a security standpoint, but it unblocks subagent functionality.

Good to see PR #35040 already submitted — this is a critical path for multi-agent setups.

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Bash script commands don't honor "Always allow" runtime allowlist and keep requiring approval on every invocation, unlike binary commands which correctly persist the allowlist.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Execute a bash script command: bash scripts/save_crystal.sh
2. Approval popup appears; click "Always allow"
3. Immediately run the same command again: bash scripts/save_crystal.sh
4. Expected: Command runs without approval (like binary commands do)
5. Actual: Approval popup appears again requesting approval

### Expected behavior

Bash script commands should behave identically to binary commands — once "Always allow" is selected, the command should be added to the runtime allowlist and never prompt again.

### Actual behavior

Bash script commands remain ungated by "Always allow" and request approval every time, regardless of prior approval selections. Binary commands (ls, find, docker, grep, cp, tar, curl, python3) correctly honor the allowlist after first approval.

### OpenClaw version

2026.2.25

### Operating system

Mac OS tahoe 26.2

### Install method

nom global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Any user running bash scripts through the approval gate
Severity: High — blocks automated backup workflows entirely
Frequency: 100% reproducible, happens every single invocation
Consequence: Users cannot whitelist bash scripts, forcing manual approval on every run even after explicitly selecting "Always allow"

### Additional information

Binary commands (ls, find, docker, grep, cp, tar, curl, python3) correctly honor "Always allow" after first approval. Only bash script invocations fail to persist. Environment: macOS, OpenClaw 2026.3.2, local gateway.

comments.body: I'd like to work on this! 🙋

## 🎉 Fix Available: Bash script allowlist now works correctly

### Summary
I've identified and fixed the root cause of this issue. Bash script commands like `bash scripts/save_crystal.sh` now correctly honor "Always allow" decisions.

### Root Cause
In `exec-approvals-allowlist.ts` (around line 214-215), when a shell wrapper command (`bash`) is followed by a script path (not `-c <inline_command>`), the function returns early **without adding the command to the allowlist**.

```typescript
// Bug (before):
const inlineCommand = extractShellWrapperInlineCommand(params.segment.argv);
if (!inlineCommand) return;  // ← Returns without adding to allowlist
```

### Solution
When `extractShellWrapperInlineCommand` returns `null`, add the complete command path to the allowlist:

```typescript
// Fixed:
const inlineCommand = extractShellWrapperInlineCommand(params.segment.argv);
if (!inlineCommand) {
    // Bug fix for #35024: When bash is followed by a script path (not -c),
    // add the entire command to allowlist instead of returning early
    params.out.add(candidatePath);
    return;
}
```

### Testing Results
All tests passed (6/6, 100%):

| Test Category | Test Case | Status |
|--------------|-----------|--------|
| Functionality | Bash script direct execution | ✅ PASS |
| Functionality | Bash `-c` inline command | ✅ PASS |
| Regression | Binary commands (ls, etc.) | ✅ PASS |
| Regression | Python scripts | ✅ PASS |
| Boundary | Relative path execution | ✅ PASS |
| Code Review | Fix code verification | ✅ PASS |

**Test Environment**: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic, Node.js v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.3.2

### Risk Assessment
**Overall Risk**: 🟢 **Extremely Low**

- **Functionality Risk**: 🟢 Only fills a missing logic branch
- **Security Risk**: 🟢 Allowlist mechanism unchanged
- **Performance Risk**: 🟢 Single O(1) operation

Complete fix documentation, test reports, and implementation details available upon request.

### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary


Subject: Incident Report: Recovery of OpenClaw Environment v2026.3.2 (7-Hour Service Outage)
Total Downtime: ~7 Hours Core Issue: Cascading failures triggered by a forced version update, API model deprecation, and mismatched local security signatures. System Environment: Mac Mini, OpenClaw Desktop App, xAI Grok API.
The Problem: The gateway refused to start because Port 18789 was "already in use," yet no visible app was running.


### Steps to reproduce

Update to newest version on mac os 

### Expected behavior

Be able to update without an issue 

### Actual behavior

Subject: Incident Report: Recovery of OpenClaw Environment v2026.3.2 (7-Hour Service Outage)
Total Downtime: ~7 Hours Core Issue: Cascading failures triggered by a forced version update, API model deprecation, and mismatched local security signatures. System Environment: Mac Mini, OpenClaw Desktop App, xAI Grok API.

Phase 1: The "Ghost" Process and Port Lock
The Problem: The gateway refused to start because Port 18789 was "already in use," yet no visible app was running.
* Step 1: Ran ps aux | grep openclaw to find hidden background processes.
* Step 2: Identified a "zombie" process (PID 90557) that had hung during the update.
* Step 3: Executed killall -9 node and openclaw gateway run --force to hard-reset the port.
* Result: The port was cleared, but a new error immediately surfaced regarding the AI model itself.

Phase 2: The "Grok-Beta" Deprecation
The Problem: The engine crashed with a 404 error because grok-beta was officially retired by xAI on 2025-09-15.
* Step 4: Diagnosed that the existing config was pointing to a dead endpoint.
* Step 5: Manually reconfigured the primary agent model using: openclaw config set agents.defaults.model.primary "openai/grok-3".
* Result: The "Brain" was updated to Grok-3, but the Desktop App still refused to connect.

Phase 3: The Version Mismatch & Security Guardrails
The Problem: The Desktop App (v2026.2.23) was incompatible with the CLI Engine (v2026.3.2). This triggered a device signature invalid error.
* Step 6: Attempted openclaw update, which revealed the core was ahead of the UI.
* Step 7: Performed a manual "Nuclear Swap": deleted the old .app and ran the curl installer: curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash.
* Step 8: Encountered new 2026 security guardrails blocking plaintext ws:// connections to openclaw.local.
* Step 9: Forced the gateway back to local loopback mode: openclaw config set gateway.mode local openclaw config set gateway.bind loopback.
* Result: Security blocks were cleared, but a "Token Mismatch" appeared.

Phase 4: Identity Purge & The Final Handshake
The Problem: The app was "ClawJacked" by its own old security tokens. It showed 2 active sessions (main:main) but couldn't talk to either.
* Step 10: Ran openclaw devices clear --yes --all to wipe the corrupted pairing table.
* Step 11: Manually extracted the secret gateway token from openclaw.json using grep.
* Step 12: Pasted the token into the App's "Control UI" settings to align the "Body" and "Brain".
* Step 13: Performed the final pairing handshake: openclaw devices approve --latest.
* Result: SUCCESS. Logs confirmed [ws] webchat connected.

Phase 5: Stabilization & Persistence
* Step 14: Verified the Telegram provider - successfully initialized.
* Step 15: Daemonized the process so it survives restarts: openclaw gateway start.
* Step 16: Created a custom .command desktop shortcut to automate future "force runs".

Summary of Resolution: The system is now fully operational on v2026.3.2 using Grok-3. The fix required a total of 16 technical interventions over 7 hours, moving from process management to API reconfiguration, and finally to cryptographic identity reset.

### OpenClaw version

v2026.3.2 

### Operating system

mac OS Tahoe 26.3

### Install method

mac app

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Affected: App, web app, telegram
Severity: SEVERE 
Frequency: Always 
Consequence: Completely unusable in any way shape or form. Failed onboarding, failed gateway error, zombie processes. Multiple hour troubleshooting and fixes

body:
### Bug type

Crash (process/app exits or hangs)

### Summary

A plugin tool handler that returns `undefined`/`void` produces a malformed content block `{type: "text"}` (no `text` property), which crashes the context truncation pipeline on every subsequent message and locks the session into an unrecoverable crash loop.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install or enable a plugin whose tool handler returns `undefined` for at least one action (e.g. sentinel plugin's `remove` action has no `return` statement).
2. Invoke that tool action in an agent session — for example, call `sentinel_control` with `action: "remove"`.
3. The tool result is persisted to the session JSONL as `content: [{"type": "text"}]` (missing `text` key).
4. Send any follow-up message to the session.

### Expected behavior

The session continues normally. If a tool handler returns `undefined`, the framework should either default the content to an empty string or reject the malformed block before persistence.

### Actual behavior

Every subsequent message triggers:

```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')
    at estimateMessageChars (reply-DhtejUNZ.js:76200:76)
```

The crash repeats on every incoming message because the malformed content block is already persisted in the session JSONL. The agent becomes completely unresponsive. In the observed incident, this lasted ~17 minutes until the session was manually reset.

Full crash path: `enforceToolResultContextBudgetInPlace` → `truncateToolResultToChars` → `estimateMessageChars` → `isTextBlock` returns `true` for `{type: "text"}` → `block.text.length` throws on `undefined`.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Session transcript timeline showing the corrupted tool result:

| Time (UTC) | Action | Result |
|---|---|---|
| 19:10:49 | list | Valid (`[]`) |
| 19:10:55 | add | Valid error (wrong strategy) |
| 19:11:18 | add | Valid error (host not allowed) |
| 19:11:42 | add | Valid error (host not allowed) |
| 19:12:52 | add | Success — created `timeapi-test-2361` |
| 19:13:06 | status | Success |
| 19:13:09 | get | Success |
| 19:13:12 | list | Success |
| 19:13:17 | remove | **Corrupted** — `{"type":"text"}` with no `text` |

The corrupted tool result message as persisted in the session JSONL:


{
  "role": "toolResult",
  "toolCallId": "call_iuCGHR5hjNegUaPHMjNIgeRL|fc_0f8c6651728332d20169a8844d498481929d39e79247a406d5",
  "toolName": "sentinel_control",
  "content": [{ "type": "text" }],
  "isError": false
}


Every other `sentinel_control` action (`list`, `add`, `status`, `get`) returned well-formed `{"type": "text", "text": "..."}` blocks. Only `remove` produced the malformed response.
```

### Impact and severity

- **Affected users/systems/channels:** Any session using a plugin whose tool handler returns `void`/`undefined` for any action. The sentinel plugin's `remove` action is a known trigger, but any plugin with a missing `return` statement can cause this.
- **Severity:** Blocks workflow. The session becomes permanently unresponsive until manually reset. No data loss, but all in-flight conversation context is effectively lost.
- **Frequency:** Edge case per plugin action, but deterministic once triggered — every session that hits the codepath will crash, and every subsequent message in that session will crash again.
- **Consequence:** Complete session lockout. In the observed incident, the agent was unresponsive for ~17 minutes. Users have no self-service recovery path other than resetting the session.

### Additional information

There are two independent bugs:

1. **`isTextBlock` type guard** (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/tool-result-char-estimator.ts`): Only checks `block.type === "text"` without verifying `block.text` is a string. This lets malformed blocks pass the guard, then `block.text.length` throws.

2. **No content block validation at persistence** (`src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.ts`): Malformed content blocks are persisted to session JSONL without sanitization. Once persisted, the crash triggers on every session load — there is no recovery without manual session reset or JSONL editing.

The plugin under development also needed a fix to properly return a string, but the core framework should be resilient to any plugin returning `undefined`.

comments.body:
# Root Cause Analysis: Issue #34979

⚠️ **Verification Method**: Code review + user report analysis  
❌ **Not reproduced**: Unable to test (requires specific plugin installation)  
✅ **Code location verified**: Found exact crash point in OpenClaw dist files

---

## Problem Summary

A plugin tool handler that returns `undefined`/`void` produces a malformed content block `{type: "text"}` (missing `text` property). This crashes the context truncation pipeline on every subsequent message, locking the session into an unrecoverable crash loop.

**User impact**: Agent becomes completely unresponsive for ~17 minutes until manual session reset.

---

## Code Location Verified

**File**: `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/pi-embedded-CtM2Mrrj.js`  
**Function**: `estimateMessageChars$1(message)`  
**Line**: 87334 (in minified dist)

### Vulnerable Code

```javascript
function estimateMessageChars$1(message) {
  // ... [lines 87325-87332]
  if (message.role === "assistant") {
    let chars = 0;
    for (const b of message.content) {
      if (b.type === "text") chars += b.text.length;  // ← Line 87334: CRASH HERE
      if (b.type === "thinking") chars += b.thinking.length;
      // ...
    }
    return chars;
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Problem**: When `b = {type: "text"}` (missing `text` property), `b.text` is `undefined`, causing:
```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')
```

---

## Why This Matches User Report

### User's Error Message
```
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')
    at estimateMessageChars (reply-DhtejUNZ.js:76200:76)
```

### Code Analysis Match
✅ **Function name matches**: `estimateMessageChars`  
✅ **Error type matches**: `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`  
✅ **Root cause matches**: No validation for `b.text` existence before accessing `.length`

### User's Root Cause Analysis
> "Full crash path: `enforceToolResultContextBudgetInPlace` → `truncateToolResultToChars` → `estimateMessageChars` → `isTextBlock` returns `true` for `{type: "text"}` → `block.text.length` throws on `undefined`."

✅ **Confirmed**: The code does exactly what user described — it checks `b.type === "text"` but doesn't verify `b.text` exists.

---

## How Malformed Block Gets Created

**Upstream issue**: Plugin tool handler returns `undefined`:

```javascript
// sentinel plugin example (from user report)
export async function sentinel_control(params) {
  if (params.action === "remove") {
    await removeSentinel(params.id);
    // ❌ No return statement → returns undefined
  }
}
```

**Framework issue**: OpenClaw persists this as `content: [{"type": "text"}]` without validating the `text` property exists.

---

## Suggested Fix

### Option A: Defensive check in `estimateMessageChars` (Quick fix)

```javascript
if (b.type === "text") {
  chars += (b.text?.length ?? 0);  // ✅ Safe access
}
```

**Pros**: Prevents crash immediately  
**Cons**: Doesn't fix the root cause (malformed blocks still get created)

### Option B: Validate tool results before persistence (Proper fix)

```javascript
// In tool result handler (before saving to JSONL)
function normalizeToolResult(result) {
  if (result === undefined || result === null) {
    return { type: "text", text: "" };  // ✅ Default to empty string
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Pros**: Prevents malformed blocks from being created  
**Cons**: Requires more changes

### Recommended: Both

1. **Immediate**: Add defensive check in `estimateMessageChars` (prevents crash)
2. **Long-term**: Validate tool results before persistence (prevents root cause)

---

## Verification Steps (If Reproducible)

### Setup
```bash
# Install sentinel plugin or create a test plugin
# with a tool handler that returns undefined
```

### Reproduce
```bash
# 1. Call the tool that returns undefined
# 2. Check session JSONL for {"type":"text"} with no text property
# 3. Send any follow-up message
# Expected: TypeError crash
```

### Verify Fix (Option A)
```javascript
// Apply defensive check
if (b.type === "text") chars += (b.text?.length ?? 0);

# 4. Restart gateway
# 5. Send follow-up message
# Expected: No crash, session continues
```

---

## Impact

**Severity**: High  
**Type**: Session-permanent crash loop

**Affected users**:
- Any plugin with tool handlers that return `undefined`
- Sentinel plugin confirmed affected
- Potentially other plugins with similar issues

**Workaround**: Manual session reset (delete JSONL or edit to remove malformed block)

**Benefits of fix**:
- ✅ Prevents crash loop
- ✅ Makes framework more resilient to plugin bugs
- ✅ Improves error handling for undefined tool results

---

## Evidence Summary

| Criterion | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| User report detail | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent (error, stack, timeline) |
| Code location found | ✅ Line 87334 in pi-embedded-CtM2Mrrj.js |
| Logic matches error | ✅ `b.text.length` with undefined throws exact error |
| Root cause clear | ✅ Missing null/undefined check |
| Fix straightforward | ✅ Add optional chaining or default value |

---

**Environment**:
- OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
- OS: macOS
- Install: npm global

---

**Verification Limitations**:
- ⚠️ Not tested in live environment (requires plugin installation)
- ✅ Code logic confirmed in dist files
- ✅ Matches user report exactly (error message, stack trace, root cause)

---

*Analysis based on code review and detailed user report. Code location verified in OpenClaw dist files. User's root cause analysis aligns perfectly with actual code logic.*

title: [Bug]: Malformed tool result content block crashes context truncation in a session-permanent loop

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Issue 1: UI webchat replies bleeding into Telegram
Before the update, UI and Telegram were isolated — messages sent from the Control UI stayed in the UI only. After 2026.3.2, any conversation in the UI also sends replies to Telegram, even when Telegram hasn't been touched in that session.

Issue 2: Duplicate messages being sent
Replies are being sent twice — once as the inline assistant response, once via the message tool (or some internal double-send). This happens intermittently across both UI and Telegram sessions.

Both issues started immediately after updating to 2026.3.2. Suspect the streaming: "partial" change is affecting reply routing/deduplication logic.

Config: Single agent (main), Telegram dmPolicy: allowlist, streaming: partial

### Steps to reproduce

Issue 1 — UI replies bleeding into Telegram:
1. Configure a single agent (main) with Telegram channel, dmPolicy: allowlist, streaming: partial
2. Open the Control UI (web chat) in a browser
3. Send any message in the UI and receive a reply
4. Check the Telegram bot — the same reply appears in Telegram without any interaction there

Issue 2 — Duplicate messages:
1. Configure a single agent (main) with Telegram channel, streaming: partial
2. Send a message via Telegram (or the UI)
3. Observe the response — it arrives twice: once as the inline assistant response, once as a separate message (appears to be sent via the message tool internally)
4. Reproducible intermittently; occurs across both UI and Telegram sessions

### Expected behavior

1. Messages sent from the Control UI should only appear in the UI session — Telegram should not receive any replies unless a message was sent via Telegram directly.

2. Each message should receive exactly one reply. No duplicate messages should be sent regardless of channel or streaming mode.

### Actual behavior

1. Any reply generated in the Control UI is also sent to Telegram, even when Telegram was never used in that session. UI and Telegram sessions are no longer isolated.

2. Replies are sent twice — once as the inline assistant response and once as a duplicate message (likely via internal message tool). This happens intermittently on both UI and Telegram channels.

Both issues began immediately after updating to 2026.3.2.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Linux x86_64)

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Discord VC join succeeds, but spoken prompts frequently produce no bot voice reply. Behavior is intermittent and can fail repeatedly even after leave/join and restarts.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure Discord channel + OpenClaw Discord voice enabled.
2. Run /vc join (bot appears in VC).
3. Speak a short phrase ("can you hear me").
4. Observe: sometimes reply in ~5-10s, often no reply.
5. Retry /vc leave -> /vc join; issue persists intermittently.
6. Issue reproduced on 2026.3.1 and after update to 2026.3.2.

### Expected behavior

After /vc join, each spoken prompt should reliably trigger a voice reply within a few seconds.

### Actual behavior

Intermittent no-reply in VC despite successful join and visible presence in member list. During failures, logs often show monitor/listener slowness and periodic discord health-monitor restarts (reason: stuck), but not always a direct voice error line at the exact no-reply timestamp.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.1 and 2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (Linux 6.17.0-14-generic)

### Install method

npm global install (openclaw update.run)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Representative logs observed during failures:
- `health-monitor: restarting (reason: stuck)` for [discord:default]
- `Slow listener detected: InteractionEventListener took 1-2s`
- earlier sessions also showed `discord voice: TTS failed: ... edge: disabled; ... elevenlabs 401` before re-enabling edge voice path.
No deterministic direct voice error appears on every no-reply timestamp.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Discord voice users in group channel workflows.
Severity: High (core voice interaction unreliable).
Frequency: Intermittent but frequent (multiple consecutive no-reply runs).
Consequence: Voice assistant appears online/joined but does not respond, causing repeated retries and workflow disruption.

### Additional information

Troubleshooting already attempted:
- /vc leave + /vc join cycles
- gateway restart
- full Discord provider re-init (disable channel -> restart -> enable -> restart)
- debounce/queue tuning (aggressive settings reduced reliability)
- update from 2026.3.1 to 2026.3.2
- rollback of config and TTS prefs to pre-update baseline

Related but separate issue (fixed): text-chat MP3 attachments caused by TTS auto-mode override in ~/.openclaw/settings/tts.json.

labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

After upgrading to OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2), sessions running on nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 show severe tool instability: tool calls intermittently fail with “Tool not found”, time out, or produce malformed tool-call behavior. Switching the same session to OpenAI GPT‑5.2 immediately restores reliable tool execution (read, web_fetch, exec, etc.).

This appears to be a regression specific to Kimi 2.5 tool-calling compatibility (or OpenClaw’s adapter for it) rather than the tools themselves.

### Steps to reproduce

Reproduction Steps (high-level)
Run OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2).
Use a Discord direct chat session with model set to nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5.
Attempt basic tool calls:
read a local file (e.g. workspace markdown)
web_fetch a URL
exec simple shell command (echo, whoami)
Observe intermittent failures (“Tool not found”, timeouts, inconsistent behavior).
Switch the same agent/session model to GPT‑5.2.
Re-run the same tool calls; observe stable success.

### Expected behavior

Tool calls should behave consistently regardless of model choice, assuming the same OpenClaw runtime + permissions.

### Actual behavior

Symptoms / Observed Behavior
When model is nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5:
Tool calls intermittently fail with errors like:
“Tool not found”
timeouts on tool calls (e.g., cross-session send)
inconsistent tool invocation behavior (some tools work once, then fail again)
Failures observed with:
read
exec
web_fetch
browser (attempted)
messaging/session tools sometimes timing out depending on agent session state

When switching model to GPT‑5.2:
Tool calls become stable and consistent.
Verified successful:
read (local workspace files)
web_fetch (external docs fetch)
exec (shell commands)
extended workflows (Firecrawl install/auth, CLI tests, etc.)

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

macOS Monterey 12.5.x

### Install method

npm

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Tool not found
timeout
exec denied
allowlist miss
```

### Impact and severity

Agents become unreliable / unusable for automation because tool calls fail unpredictably.
Significant time lost debugging gateway/tool configuration, but issue resolves by changing model.

### Additional information

Notes / Hypothesis
This looks like a tool schema / tool-call formatting regression affecting Kimi 2.5 in OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (or the specific provider adapter for nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5). The exact same tools + permissions worked immediately after model swap.

Environment
OpenClaw version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
Host OS: Darwin 21.6.0 (x64) (macOS)
Node: v22.22.0
Channel/surface where observed: Discord direct chat (provider: discord)
Models compared:
Problematic: nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5
Stable: OpenAI GPT‑5.2 (openai/gpt-5.2)

Workaround
Switch tool-using agents from nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 to a model with stable tool calling (e.g. openai/gpt-5.2).

Logs / Additional Info
If maintainers want, I can provide:
session transcript paths from ~/.openclaw/agents/.../sessions/*.jsonl
exact error strings as they appeared in Discord
exact tool call payloads during failure vs success

comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report. This looks actionable, but the current evidence mixes multiple failure classes (Tool not found, allowlist miss / exec denied, and timeout), which may come from different layers.

To isolate the Kimi/NVIDIA tool-calling regression, could you share a minimal reproducible package for one failing session?

Please provide
Exact model/provider ref used in the failing run
Example: provider=nvidia, model=nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5.

One session transcript JSONL (redacted) covering:

one failing read/web_fetch/exec tool turn
one succeeding turn (if any) in the same session
Path pattern is usually: ~/.openclaw/agents/.../sessions/*.jsonl
Raw request payload snippet for the failing turn (especially tools, tool_choice, and assistant tool-call block), with secrets removed.

Runtime config snippets (redacted) for:

tools.* policy (especially allowlist / exec policy)
agents.defaults + model override for this session
any provider extraParams overrides
Whether the same prompt + same tool policy succeeds on openai/gpt-5.2 in the same workspace/session constraints.

Why this is needed
Tool not found usually indicates tool-name/schema/call-shape mismatch.
allowlist miss / exec denied is policy-layer denial.
timeout may be provider latency or tool-loop behavior.
A minimal failing JSONL + payload will let us reproduce and determine whether this is:

NVIDIA/Kimi adapter compatibility,
tool-name normalization mismatch,
or policy/timeout interaction.
If helpful, we can also provide a redaction template for safe sharing.
Submitted a fix in PR #35044. Normalizes provider-prefixed tool names across both streamed partial and message payloads to prevent intermittent "tool not found" dispatch misses.

title: [Bug]: Regression: tools intermittently fail (“Tool not found” / timeouts) when using nvidia/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5 on OpenClaw 2026.3.2; switching to GPT‑5.2 restores tool stability

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

The wrapExternalContent function (and by extension wrapWebContent and buildSafeExternalPrompt) wraps untrusted external content with special boundary markers (e.g., <<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>) to clearly separate it from trusted instructions. To prevent an attacker from spoofing these markers using visually similar Unicode characters, the content is first passed through foldMarkerText, which maps certain Unicode homoglyphs to their ASCII equivalents (e.g., fullwidth < → <).

However, the mapping table ANGLE_BRACKET_MAP currently covers only a subset of characters that can serve as substitutes for < and >. Many other Unicode characters that closely resemble angle brackets are missing, including guillemets, double angle brackets, and ornamental variants. An attacker could inject a marker like «EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT»; because it is not normalized, the subsequent regex (/<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT...>>>/gi) fails to detect it, leaving the spoofed marker untouched in the content. If the underlying LLM treats these Unicode characters as equivalent to < and > (e.g., through tokenization or Unicode normalization), the security boundary can be bypassed, potentially allowing the attacker to inject malicious instructions.

This is a follow-up for #30948 and 30951.

### Steps to reproduce

Because this issue is reported publicly and I consider it a proactive hardening measure rather than an actively exploited vulnerability, I deliberately refrain from publishing exact exploit steps or proof‑of‑concept code. The goal is to raise the bar for potential adversaries while giving maintainers and advanced users enough information to understand the rationale behind the change.

### Expected behavior

The list should include these characters.

### Actual behavior

The list does not include these characters.

### OpenClaw version

Latest

### Operating system

All

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
title: [Security]: Potential security risk allowing the attacker to inject malicious instructions in src/security/external-contents.ts

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

 With compaction.mode: "safeguard", OpenClaw calls the LLM API every ~30 minutes on all active sessions — including isolated cron sessions that contain no real conversation messages. The safeguard then correctly cancels the compaction, but the API call has already been made and billed.

From gateway.err.log, this pattern repeats continuously across every active session:
[compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize. Each cancellation is preceded by a full LLM completion request (~33k prompt tokens, ~45 completion tokens based on OpenRouter activity logs). The check that determines there are "no real messages" happens after the API call, not before it.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure any agent with compaction.mode: "safeguard" and a remote LLM provider 
2. Set up one or more isolated cron jobs (e.g. daily heartbeat agents) 
3. Leave the system running idle between scheduled jobs
4. Observe: LLM API calls fire every ~30 minutes on all sessions, including sessions with zero conversation content
5. Observe gateway.err.log: each call results in the safeguard cancellation message

### Expected behavior

Before initiating a compaction request to the LLM provider, the system should check locally whether the session contains real conversation messages. If no real messages exist, compaction should abort immediately with no API call made.

### Actual behavior

A full LLM completion request is sent to the remote provider (~33k prompt tokens) regardless of session content. The response is received and billed. Only after the round-trip does the safeguard evaluate the result and cancel and then it logs "cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize." The API cost is already incurred.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

macOS 26.3 (25D125)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
gateway.err.log — compaction firing and cancelling every 30 minutes on sessions with no content:

2026-03-04T09:12:03.260Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T09:12:20.814Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize. 
2026-03-04T09:42:03.262Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize. 
2026-03-04T09:42:31.061Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T10:07:43.134Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T10:12:03.250Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T10:42:03.270Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T11:12:03.265Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T11:42:03.256Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T12:12:03.307Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T12:42:03.284Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T13:12:03.314Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T13:42:03.292Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T14:12:03.296Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.
2026-03-04T14:42:03.345Z [compaction-safeguard] Compaction safeguard: cancelling compaction with no real conversation messages to summarize.

30-minute cadence, continuous, system otherwise idle.

---
gateway.err.log — compaction timeout on a session that had no content worth compacting, wasting 10 minutes of execution time:
2026-03-04T11:22:03.276Z [agent/embedded] embedded run timeout: runId=[RUN-ID] sessionId=[SESSION-ID] timeoutMs=600000
2026-03-04T11:22:03.285Z [agent/embedded] using current snapshot: timed out during compaction runId=[RUN-ID] sessionId=[SESSION-ID]

---
OpenRouter activity log — single representative call (one of 396 on this date):

  {
    "model": "stepfun/step-3.5-flash",
    "tokens_prompt": 33593,
    "tokens_completion": 45,
    "native_tokens_reasoning": 42,
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "usage": 0.0036987,
    "app": { "title": "OpenClaw" }
  }

33k prompt tokens in — 45 tokens out — then discarded. This call pattern repeated 396 times on a single day with the system idle between two scheduled cron jobs.
```

### Impact and severity

Severity: Moderate — does not break functionality but causes continuous unnecessary API spend with no user-visible benefit
Frequency: Always — fires every ~30 minutes on every active session, 24/7, regardless of activity
Affected users: Any user running OpenClaw with:
 - A remote/paid LLM provider as primary model (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)
 - One or more long-running or idle sessions (cron agents, persistent Slack/Telegram sessions, background agents)
 - compaction.mode: "safeguard" (the default — users who have never touched this setting are affected without knowing it)

Practical consequence:
 - ~48 LLM API calls per day per instance generated with zero productive output
 - Each call sends the full session system prompt + context (~33k tokens) to the provider and discards the response
 - At Step-3.5-Flash pricing this produced $1.26/day in invisible background spend — roughly $38/month — on a system doing nothing
 - Users who switch to a paid model without realizing the compaction issue exists will see immediate unexplained API cost
 - Users relying on Hindsight or another external memory system have no workflow reason for in-context compaction at all, they are paying for a system designed to help them that actively works against their cost model
 - The community workaround ("adjust compaction settings") does not exist in practice, "off" is not a valid schema value, leaving affected users with no recourse short of patching the binary

Risk: Low data risk, high cost risk at scale. A team running multiple agents (Odie + Piper + subagents) multiplies the call count proportionally.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Confirming this is a significant cost issue for multi-agent deployments using safeguard mode with expensive models.

**Our setup:** 3 agents (soon 4), all running Claude Opus 4.6 with `compaction: "safeguard"`, 24/7 on Discord. Each agent has 5-8 active sessions.

**Estimated waste:** ~48 unnecessary LLM calls per agent per day × 3 agents = **144 wasted Opus calls daily**. At Opus pricing, this adds up fast — especially since these are idle sessions with no new user messages.

We specifically chose safeguard mode because our conversations are long, tool-intensive, and context-sensitive. Switching to default mode would lose the benefits (chunked summarization, tool failure preservation) just to avoid this bug.

**Suggested fix:** Skip the compaction check entirely when `lastMessageAt` hasn't changed since the last check. A simple timestamp comparison would eliminate all idle-session waste with zero risk to active sessions.

title: [Bug]:  safeguard compaction makes LLM API call before checking for real messages — 48 unnecessary calls/day on   idle sessions

body: ### Bug type Regression (worked before, now fails) ### Summary The Control UI dashboard displays "Version 2026.2.24" after updating to 2026.3.2. The old version string is hardcoded in the pre-built frontend bundle shipped with the npm package. **Environment:** macOS ARM64 (Lume VM), Node.js 25.6.1, npm global install. **Evidence:** - `openclaw --version` reports 2026.3.2 - `package.json` shows version 2026.3.2 - `dist/build-info.json` shows version 2026.3.2 - `dist/control-ui/assets/index-Qb3PJV7U.js` contains the string "2026.2.24" - Dashboard badge reads "Version 2026.2.24" and "Health OK" **Root cause:** The Control UI frontend assets appear to have been compiled before the version was bumped in the 2026.3.2 release pipeline. **Impact:** Cosmetic only. Gateway functionality is correct at 2026.3.2. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Install or update OpenClaw to 2026.3.2 via `npm install -g openclaw@latest` 2. Restart the gateway (or let the LaunchAgent restart it) 3. Confirm the CLI reports the new version: `openclaw gateway status` shows "OpenClaw 2026.3.2" 4. Confirm the package is correct: `cat /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/build-info.json` shows version 2026.3.2 5. Open the Control UI dashboard at `http://127.0.0.1:18789/?token=` 6. Observe the version badge in the top-right corner displays "Version 2026.2.24" instead of "2026.3.2" 7. Hard-refresh the browser and clear all cached data for 127.0.0.1. The old version persists because the string is in the compiled JS, not the cache. 8. Verify: `grep "2026.2.24" /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/control-ui/assets/index-Qb3PJV7U.js` returns a match **Expected:** Dashboard badge shows "Version 2026.3.2" **Actual:** Dashboard badge shows "Version 2026.2.24" ### Expected behavior The dashboard version badge should display "Version 2026.3.2", matching the CLI, `package.json`, and `build-info.json`. All version indicators across the CLI and Control UI should agree after an update and gateway restart. ### Actual behavior The Control UI dashboard displays "Version 2026.2.24" in the top-right badge despite the gateway running 2026.3.2. The Health indicator shows "Health OK" and the gateway functions correctly. No error messages are shown. The incorrect version persists after gateway restarts, browser hard-refreshes, and clearing all browser cache and website data for 127.0.0.1. The old version string "2026.2.24" is present in the compiled frontend bundle at `dist/control-ui/assets/index-Qb3PJV7U.js` shipped inside the 2026.3.2 npm package, confirming this is a build pipeline issue rather than a caching or runtime problem. ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 ### Operating system macOS ARM64 (Lume VM) ### Install method Node.js 25.6.1, npm global install. ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell $ openclaw gateway status 🦞 OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2) Service: LaunchAgent (loaded) Runtime: running (pid 13528, state active) RPC probe: ok For: cat /opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/build-info.json { "version": "2026.3.2", "commit": "85377a28175695c224f6589eb5c1460841ecd65c", "builtAt": "2026-03-03T04:36:41.924Z" } But Dashboard UI shows older version despite clearing cache ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information

comments.body:
Investigated this — the version string is hardcoded in the pre-built frontend bundle at `dist/control-ui/assets/`. The server-side `build-info.json` and `package.json` both show the correct 2026.3.2 version, so the issue is in the frontend build pipeline.

The control-ui assets are compiled at publish time, so this can't be fixed via PR — it needs a rebuild of the frontend bundle with the correct version injected before the next npm publish.

Likely fix: ensure the build step for control-ui reads version from `package.json` (or `build-info.json`) rather than using a stale value. Happy to help if there's a build script I can look at.

Submitted a fix in PR #35742. Added `state.serverVersion` (from the bootstrap `/api/info` call) as a fallback in the version badge resolution chain, so the correct version is displayed immediately on page load instead of waiting for WebSocket hello.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

title: [Bug]: ontrol UI version badge shows 2026.2.24 in 2026.3.2 release

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

openclaw acp client connects and creates a session, but every interactive input returns only [end_turn] with no assistant output.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run:
openclaw gateway status
Confirm gateway is healthy (running + RPC probe OK).

2. Start ACP client:
openclaw acp client -v --server openclaw --server-verbose

3. At the prompt, enter:
/config
/context
hello

4. Observe output.


### Expected behavior

ACP client should display command output and/or assistant response text in the terminal.

### Actual behavior

Each input returns only [end_turn], with no visible response content.

Example:

> /config
[end_turn]
> hello
[end_turn]


### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04 (Linux 6.8.0-100-generic x64)

### Install method

npm global (systemd gateway service)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Relevant log evidence:

[acp-client] spawning: openclaw acp --verbose
[acp-client] initializing
[acp] ready
[acp-client] creating session
[commands] ...
OpenClaw ACP client
Session: <uuid>
Type a prompt, or "exit" to quit.

[end_turn]
[end_turn]
[end_turn]
Gateway remains healthy during repro:

runtime active
RPC probe OK
listening on 127.0.0.1:18789
Additional corroboration:

openclaw acp (without client) expects JSON protocol input; plain text fails JSON parse (expected), so issue appears specific to openclaw acp client interactive rendering/response path.
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Users relying on terminal ACP client interaction
Severity: High (blocks ACP terminal workflow)
Frequency: 100% reproducible in this environment
Consequence: Cannot use ACP client for normal interactive chat; must fall back to Web UI/standard chat path

### Additional information

Workaround: use Web UI / normal OpenClaw chat instead of ACP terminal client.
Appears to be a regression/bug in ACP interactive response handling for this version (2026.3.2).

comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed bug report with repro steps. This sounds like a regression in the ACP client response rendering pipeline.

Some things that may help diagnose:
1. Does the gateway show any response content in verbose logs when the client only shows `[end_turn]`?
2. Is this specific to the `openclaw acp client` interactive mode, or does it also happen with `openclaw acp` using JSON input?
3. Any difference if you pipe input vs interactive (e.g., `echo '{"role":"user","content":"hello"}' | openclaw acp`)?

This could be related to how the client parses/renders the response stream vs the raw ACP protocol. The `[end_turn]` marker suggests the turn boundaries are being detected, but the actual content isn't being displayed.

Thanks — I ran deeper checks and captured concrete evidence.

1) Gateway logs when ACP client shows only [end_turn]
Yes, gateway/client logs show successful setup and turn boundaries, but no visible assistant content in interactive mode.

Startup + session creation is healthy
[acp-client] spawning: openclaw acp --verbose
[acp-client] initializing
[acp] ready
[acp-client] creating session
[commands] /help /commands /context /whoami /id /subagents /config /debug /usage /stop /restart /dock-telegram /dock-discord /dock-slack /activation /send /reset /new /think /verbose /reasoning /elevated /model /queue /bash /compact
OpenClaw ACP client
Session: f337bb93-6c33-4fef-9da8-7170c9da8d74
Type a prompt, or "exit" to quit.
After user inputs, only turn markers appear
[end_turn]
[end_turn]
[end_turn]
[end_turn]
No command output or assistant content appears in terminal.

2) Is this specific to openclaw acp client interactive mode?
From testing, yes.

openclaw acp client -v --server openclaw --server-verbose:

Connects and creates session

Interactive prompts accepted

Responses display only [end_turn]

Raw openclaw acp with plain text:

Expected JSON parse errors (because raw bridge expects JSON protocol input), e.g.:

Failed to parse JSON message: /whoami SyntaxError: Unexpected token '/'
Failed to parse JSON message: hey SyntaxError: Unexpected token 'h'
So plain-text failure in raw mode seems expected; the bug is interactive rendering/content in acp client.

3) Health / infra sanity checks (not likely infra failure)
openclaw status / openclaw gateway status show healthy runtime:

OpenClaw: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
OS: Linux 6.8.0-100-generic, Node 22.22.0
Gateway: loopback 127.0.0.1:18789
Runtime active, RPC probe OK
Listening on 127.0.0.1:18789
Relevant lines:

Runtime: running (pid ..., state active, sub running, last exit 0, reason 0)
RPC probe: ok
Listening: 127.0.0.1:18789
4) Additional possibly relevant observation
During one restart window, logs showed:

received SIGTERM; shutting down
shutdown timed out; exiting without full cleanup
After restart, ACP client behavior remained the same ([end_turn] only), so this may be unrelated, but including for completeness.

5) Exact deterministic repro used
openclaw gateway restart
openclaw acp client -v --server openclaw --server-verbose
Then at prompt:

/commands
/config
/context
hello
Observed: only [end_turn] each time.

6) Pending check requested (pipe JSON into raw ACP)
I can run:

echo '{"role":"user","content":"hello"}' | openclaw acp -v
If this is not the expected stdin envelope for 2026.3.2 raw ACP mode, please share the minimal valid JSON message format and I’ll retest exactly with that schema.

Current hypothesis matches yours: turn boundary detection works, but interactive client response content is dropped/not rendered in openclaw acp client.
