body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Around 4:00 AM ET the OpenClaw agent suddenly stopped executing any action involving filesystem or system commands.

Before that moment the agent could:
- create files on my PC and NAS
- run commands
- deploy code to remote SSH hosts
- execute scripts
- perform API actions (Gmail etc.)

Suddenly it started returning manual instructions instead of executing actions.

For example instead of creating files or deploying code, the agent now outputs full code snippets and tells me to run them manually in my terminal.

The agent also explicitly stated that it does not have access to execution tools anymore (exec/write).

### Steps to reproduce

1. Start OpenClaw normally
2. Ask the agent to perform a filesystem action

Example prompts:

"Create a file called hello.txt on my desktop"

or

"Deploy this simple app to my remote SSH host"

3. Observe the response

### Expected behavior

The agent should execute filesystem and system actions automatically using its available tools.

For example:
- create hello.txt on the desktop
- write files to disk
- execute shell commands
- deploy files to SSH hosts

This behavior was working normally before 4:00 AM ET.

### Actual behavior

The agent refuses to execute filesystem operations.

Instead it returns instructions such as:
- "Run this in your terminal"
- "Create this file manually"

Example message returned:

"Gateway running unreachable, but agent has no exec/read/write tools, only message and sessions. How to enable file system tool?"

This indicates that the agent lost access to the following tools:
- exec
- read
- write

It still appears to have:
- message
- sessions

So the agent runs conversationally but cannot perform actions anymore.

### OpenClaw version

Latest version installed via official PowerShell installer (March 2026)

### Operating system

Windows 11 (dedicated machine, freshly reformatted last week)

### Install method

Official OpenClaw PowerShell one-line installer

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Example response from the agent:

"Gateway running unreachable, but agent has no exec/read/write tools, only message and sessions."

Conversation excerpt:

KIM.AI — 4:05 AM
"Gab, je suis honnête avec toi — dans ma session actuelle, je n'ai pas accès aux outils d'exécution (exec/write) sur ta machine. C'est pour ça que rien ne se crée pour vrai depuis tantôt."

Instead of executing commands, the agent provided PowerShell code for me to run manually.

I am currently restoring the logs and will attach them shortly.
```

### Impact and severity

Severity: High (agent cannot execute tasks)

Impact:
The agent can no longer perform any filesystem or command execution tasks.

This completely prevents workflows such as:
- file creation
- code deployment
- automation
- scripting
- API integrations

The agent is essentially reduced to a chat-only assistant.

### Additional information

Troubleshooting attempted:

- restarted OpenClaw
- changed API key and model
- reinstalled with existing workspace
- reinstalled with fresh workspace
- reinstalled using the official PowerShell installer

Even after a clean install the problem persists.

Additional note:
Another user on the OpenClaw Discord reported the same issue but on Ubuntu, suggesting it may not be Windows-specific.

Also an unusual behavior occurred around 4:30 AM where the bot started telling me to go to sleep even though I had not mentioned being tired. This may or may not be related.

comments.body:
This is a serious issue. A few diagnostic questions:

1. What does `openclaw gateway status` show now?
2. Check the gateway logs: `openclaw gateway logs --lines 100` - any errors around 4 AM?
3. In the agent's response, it mentioned "Gateway running unreachable" - is the gateway actually running and reachable now?

This could be caused by:
- Tool configuration being lost/reset
- Gateway in a degraded state
- Agent runtime issue (wrong agent selected, e.g., `agent=subagent` vs `agent=main`)
- Workspace configuration issue

Can you also check your config for the agent runtime setting? Look for `agent=` in your config files.

From the snippet you pasted — **“Gateway running unreachable, … only message and sessions”** — this usually means your client is *not actually connected to a reachable Gateway*. When that happens, OpenClaw can only do “chat + session routing”, and the filesystem/system tools (`read`/`write`/`exec`) won’t be available.

### Try this (2 quick checks)
1) **On the machine that should run tools**, run:
```bash
openclaw gateway status
openclaw status --deep
```
If you see anything like *probe fails / unreachable / wrong URL/profile*, fix that first (this is the root cause of missing tools).

2) If the Gateway looks “running” but still unreachable, the most common fix is a **service/profile mismatch**. Reinstall the service metadata from the profile/state-dir you actually use:
```bash
openclaw gateway install --force
```
Then re-run `openclaw gateway status` and confirm the Control UI works at the printed dashboard URL.

### If it’s still broken
Reply with:
- `openclaw gateway status --json`
- `openclaw gateway logs --lines 200` (especially around ~04:00 ET)
- Whether you’re using profiles / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` (a mismatch can make one terminal “see” a different gateway than the one your app is talking to).

Recommended CoClaw pages:
- [Gateway: service says running but the CLI/UI can't connect (probe fails)](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting/solutions/gateway-service-running-but-probe-fails/)
- [OpenClaw Operability: logs, evidence, and a simple task board](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-operability-and-observability/)

> If you see anything like _probe fails / unreachable / wrong URL/profile_, fix that first (this is the root cause of missing tools).
> 
> 2. If the Gateway looks “running” but still unreachable, the most common fix is a **service/profile mismatch**. Reinstall the service metadata from the profile/state-dir you actually use:
> 
> openclaw gateway install --force
> Then re-run `openclaw gateway status` and confirm the Control UI works at the printed dashboard URL.
> 
> ### If it’s still broken
> Reply with:
> 
> * `openclaw gateway status --json`
> * `openclaw gateway logs --lines 200` (especially around ~04:00 ET)
> * Whether you’re using profiles / `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` (a mismatch can make one terminal “see” a different gateway than the one your app is talking to).
> 
> Recommended CoClaw pages:
> 
> * [Gateway: service says running but the CLI/UI can't connect (probe fails)](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting/solutions/gateway-service-running-but-probe-fails/)
> * [OpenClaw Operability: logs, evidence, and a simple task board](https://coclaw.com/guides/openclaw-operability-and-observability/)

why this is got me too... im confuse

## Fix Found  - Thanks everyone for the help.

This post from about 20 hours ago pointed me in the right direction:  
https://x.com/chefamysins/status/2028960264591196490

It appears the latest update changed the **tools profile** for some users.
You can check which profile you currently have by running:

openclaw config get tools

If it returns:

**{
  "profile": "messaging"
}**

then that is likely the cause of the issue.

The messaging profile does not include filesystem tools, which means the agent cannot use read, write, exec, etc.
Fix: Go to your OpenClaw root folder. Example on Windows (C:\Users\YOUR_USERNAME\.openclaw)

Open the file:
openclaw.json

Find this line:
"profile": "messaging"

Change it to:
"profile": "coding"

Save the file and restart OpenClaw.

After restarting, filesystem tools (read, write, exec, etc.) should work again.

Thanks for the detailed report! This looks like a regression from our recent Telegram refactor in v3.2.

We've identified the likely cause - the streaming "partial" mode refactor may be sending messages twice in some cases.

A workaround for now: try setting `streaming: "full"` instead of "partial" to see if that avoids the duplicates.

We'll investigate the refactor commits and get back to you with a fix.

### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

The Cloudflare AI Gateway with the Authentication feature enabled rejects requests despite having the cf-aig-authorization header set with the correct token.

### Steps to reproduce

According to the docs I need to add the following configuration block under the cloudflare-ai-gateway provider section:

"headers": {
  "cf-aig-authorization": "Bearer: CLOUDFLARE_TOKEN"
},

https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/cloudflare-ai-gateway

I have added this to the configuration. When I enable Authentication in the gateway I get this error:

openclaw-gateway-1  | 2026-03-04T17:25:06.596+00:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=36944c82-7780-4b3a-9ce9-0800aba7b6c9 isError=true error=HTTP 401: [{"code":2009,"message":"Unauthorized"}]

If I disable Authentication, it works fine. I tested my token with curl and it works.

### Expected behavior

Requests should be accepted by the gateway with Authentication enabled.

### Actual behavior

Requests are denied with an authorization error.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.3

### Operating system

Ubuntu Server 24.04 ARM

### Install method

docker

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
openclaw-gateway-1  | 2026-03-04T17:25:06.596+00:00 [agent/embedded] embedded run agent end: runId=36944c82-7780-4b3a-9ce9-0800aba7b6c9 isError=true error=HTTP 401: [{"code":2009,"message":"Unauthorized"}]
```

Affected: Gateway authentication
Severity: Prevents requiring authentication to use the gateway
Frequency: Every time.
Consequence: Leave gateway in an unsecured state.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report. This appears to be a missing feature, not a regression - custom headers aren't currently passed through to Cloudflare AI Gateway requests in the provider config.

We're tracking this as a feature gap. The workaround is to disable Authentication on the Cloudflare gateway side for now.

Let me add this to our backlog to support custom headers in provider configs.

title: [Bug]: Cloudflare AI Gateway Authentication Broken

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

When `commands.allowFrom` is configured, Discord native slash commands from unauthorized users result in a silent timeout instead of a clear "not authorized" response.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Configure commands.allowFrom to restrict command access to specific users
2. Have an unauthorized user invoke a Discord native slash command (e.g., /status)
3. Observe that the command shows "The application did not respond" after few minutes instead of an authorization error message

### Expected behavior

Unauthorized users should receive a clear error message like "You are not authorized to use this command" immediately, similar to how DM policy violations are handled in `native-command.ts`.

### Actual behavior

- The slash command is deferred (ACK sent)
- `commands.allowFrom` is evaluated later in the auto-reply pipeline
- Unauthorized users are silently rejected with { shouldContinue: false }
- No response body is sent back to Discord
- Users see "The application did not respond" timeout error after few minutes
- No indication that the command was rejected due to authorization

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

**Code review by Codex (GPT-5.3-Codex high):**

The issue stems from the order of operations:

1. `src/discord/monitor/native-command.ts:1345-1414 `- Discord slash command handler checks DM policy and guild access, but **does not check `commands.allowFrom`**
2. `src/auto-reply/command-auth.ts:264-367` - `commands.allowFrom` is evaluated later, setting `isAuthorizedSender=false` for unauthorized users
3. `src/auto-reply/reply/command-gates.ts:7-17` - Command handlers return { shouldContinue: false } without a response message
4. `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts:404-407` - reply: undefined is returned, so Discord receives no follow-up

The deferred ACK is sent, but no completion message follows, resulting in a timeout.
title: [Bug]: commands.allowFrom causes silent timeout for unauthorized Discord slash command users

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary


When the gateway restarts (for any reason — crash, config change, watchdog, OS reboot), any task that was in progress is silently dropped. The agent comes back up but sits idle — no continuation, no update to the user. Events from before the restart are not replayed. A new trigger is required every time to wake the agent.
Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
Platform: Linux VPS

### Steps to reproduce

Start a long-running or multi-step agent task (e.g. a cron job, a multi-tool workflow, or any task that takes more than ~30 seconds)
While the task is in progress, trigger a gateway restart — via config.patch, health-monitor, watchdog, manual restart, or OS reboot
Wait for the gateway to come back up fully
Observe that the agent is online but completely silent — no continuation of the task, no status update, no error message
The task only resumes if the user manually sends a new message

### Expected behavior

After a gateway restart, the agent should detect that a task was in progress and resume it automatically
At minimum, the agent should send a message to the user indicating the restart and the interrupted task
Any events or messages generated before the restart that were not yet delivered should be flushed or re-queued on restart
No manual input from the user should be required

### Actual behavior

Gateway restarts cleanly and reports healthy
Agent is online and responsive but takes no action
In-flight task is silently dropped — no error, no notification, no partial result
Messages generated by the agent before the restart that were not yet delivered are lost entirely
The agent waits indefinitely for a new user message before doing anything
channels status shows everything connected — no indication anything was lost

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

ubuntu

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

What We've Tried (None Stable)

Approach | Result
-- | --
BOOT.md with resume instructions | Agent reads it but takes no action without a trigger message
gateway:start hook calling openclaw agent --message | Unstable — fires before gateway is fully ready, or fires multiple times in a restart storm causing duplicate wake events
@reboot cron with openclaw agent --message | Only works on OS reboot, misses crash/config restarts
Simulating user input via CLI on boot | Partially works but race conditions cause duplicate triggers or missed wakes
Heartbeat cron every 15 min | Agent eventually wakes but with up to 15 min gap and no task context

The closest working solution is simulating user input on gateway:start, but it is not stable due to:

No deduplication across restart storms (each restart sends a new wake, even if a previous one is still processing)
No readiness guarantee (message fires before the agent channel is fully ready)
No task context passed with the wake event (agent knows to resume but not what to resume)


Root Cause (as understood)
1. Events are not replayed after reconnect
After a gateway restart, clients detect a sequence gap but events from before the disconnect are not replayed. Any messages or tool results generated during the interrupted turn are permanently lost.
2. No persistent turn state
There is no mechanism to persist in-flight turn state (accumulated messages, tool call position, task context) to disk before a restart. When the process exits, all turn state is gone.
3. No built-in post-restart trigger
The gateway has no native mechanism to automatically send a wake event or resume signal to agents after coming back up. BOOT.md is read passively — the agent only acts on it if something triggers a turn first.
4. gateway:start hook fires too early
The gateway:start hook fires before all channels and agents are fully initialized. Using it to send a wake message is a race condition — the agent channel may not be ready to receive it yet.

Impact

Any task longer than the restart window is silently lost
Users receive no notification that a task was interrupted
Long-running cron jobs (email cleanup, multi-step workflows) are especially affected
The only recovery is manual user input — not acceptable for automated/unattended setups
Restart storms (multiple rapid restarts) compound the problem — each restart loses more state


Suggested Fix
1. Persist turn state before shutdown
Before processing SIGUSR1 or exiting, flush any active turn's accumulated messages and state to disk (e.g. .openclaw/turn-state/). On next startup, restore and continue.
2. Add a gateway:ready hook (fires after all channels initialized)
A hook that fires only once all agents and channels are confirmed ready — not at process start. This would allow reliable post-restart wake messages without race conditions.
3. Built-in resume event injection
On startup, if .openclaw/turn-state/ contains unfinished turns, automatically inject a system message into the relevant channel:
[System] Gateway restarted. Task "{task}" was in progress. Resuming.
4. Event replay or at-least-once delivery for turn messages
Messages generated during a turn should be buffered until confirmed delivered. If the gateway restarts before delivery, re-queue them on next startup.

Workaround (Current, Unstable)
Using gateway:start hook to simulate user input:
json{
  "hooks": {
    "gateway:start": {
      "command": "sleep 12 && openclaw agent --message 'Gateway restarted. Resume any in-progress tasks.' --agent main"
    }
  }
}
This partially works but has race conditions and causes duplicate wake events during restart storms. A cross-PID cooldown and single-flight lock (flock) are needed on top of this to make it marginally stable — which is significant complexity for something that should be built in.

title: [Bug]: Agent does not resume in-flight tasks or send updates after gateway restart (v2026.3.2)

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

WhatsApp No active listener while status=connected — mid-session desync (v2026.3.2) 

I NEED HELP, context: 


Summary
WhatsApp sends fail with No active WhatsApp Web listener while channels status --probe reports linked/running/connected. The listener disappears from the internal map mid-session without any close/disconnect/logout event, and the gateway never self-recovers.
Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
Platform: Linux VPS

What Happens

Gateway starts, WhatsApp connects normally — sends work fine.
~20–30 minutes later, outbound sends start failing:

   Forum notifier error: failed to send forum new-post message to group target. (attempts=3)
   No active WhatsApp Web listener (account: default)

At the same time, channels status --probe still reports:

   linked / running / connected

The gateway never auto-recovers. Manual gateway restart temporarily fixes it, but the desync returns.


Root Cause (from local RCA)
Two separate bugs working together:
Bug 1 — Status and listener map are fully decoupled
status.connected is published via statusSink / emitStatus independently from the active listener map used by requireActiveWebListener() for sends.
It is possible to have connected=true with an empty listener map indefinitely, with no reconciliation between the two systems.
Bug 2 — Watchdog silently exits when lastMessageAt is null
The watchdog that should detect and recover a dead listener has this condition:
jsif (!lastMessageAt) return;
When lastMessageAt is null (which it is at the time of failure), the watchdog exits early and never checks whether the listener map is empty. So even if the listener disappears, the watchdog takes no action.
What triggers this
A gateway restart storm (rapid successive restarts from config.patch, health-monitor, or watchdog overlap) causes a reconnect cycle where the listener is removed from the map without emitting a close event — leaving the status stale as connected.

Evidence from Logs
15:20 → OK — notified 1 new post (send succeeded)
15:40:05 → No active WhatsApp Web listener (attempt 1)
15:40:19 → No active WhatsApp Web listener (attempt 2)
15:40:32 → No active WhatsApp Web listener (attempt 3)
In the same window:

channels.status = connected=true, lastDisconnect=null
reconnectAttempts=0
No web reconnect: connection closed, scheduling retry, or logged out events in logs

The listener disappears silently — no event, no log, no recovery.

Affected Code (identified in dist/)

dist/channel-web-sl83aqDv.js ~line 2008
dist/web-CSq0l9pG.js ~line 2054
dist/active-listener-*.js — const listeners = new Map() (all variants)

Key observations:

Listener stored via setActiveWebListener(accountId, listener) into a module-level Map — not GC'd
requireActiveWebListener() reads from this Map for sends
emitStatus reads from a separate status object — no cross-check with the Map
listenerFactory() init now has try/catch (hotfixed locally) but desync persists mid-session


Expected Behavior

If listener map is empty, status.connected must reflect false
Watchdog must detect empty listener map regardless of lastMessageAt
Auto-recovery (reconnect) must trigger when map is empty, with a cooldown to prevent loops


Suggested Fix
1. Watchdog — check listener map, not just lastMessageAt
jsconst hasListener = !!getActiveWebListener(accountId);
if (!hasListener) {
  const now = Date.now();
  if (now - reconnectTriggeredAt > 30000) {
    reconnectTriggeredAt = now;
    reconnectLogger.warn({ accountId }, 'watchdog: map empty, forcing reconnect');
    // continue to reconnect logic
  }
  return;
}
if (!lastMessageAt) return;
2. Derive connected from both status AND listener map
jssetActiveWebListener(accountId, listener);
status.connected = !!getActiveWebListener(accountId);
emitStatus({ ...status, connected: status.connected && !!getActiveWebListener(accountId) });
3. Diagnostic log on listener map removal
In active-listener*.js, inside setActiveWebListener when called with null:
jsif (value == null && listeners.has(accountId)) {
  console.warn('[active-listener] removed from map', accountId, new Error().stack);
}
This will expose why the listener is being removed without a close event.

Workaround
Full gateway restart temporarily restores the listener. Not acceptable long-term for production setups.

Impact

Any outbound WhatsApp send silently fails after a reconnect cycle
Notifiers, alerts, and any channel-bound automation are blocked until manual intervention
Status reporting gives false confidence — connected=true masks a broken send path
ShareContent

### Steps to reproduce

Run OpenClaw gateway on Linux with WhatsApp Web channel linked and active
Trigger a gateway restart storm — e.g. apply a config.patch, or let health-monitor + watchdog overlap and cause 2–3 rapid successive restarts within 60 seconds
Wait for gateway to come back up — channels status --probe reports linked/running/connected
Wait 20–30 minutes (or trigger any outbound WhatsApp send, e.g. a notifier job)
Observe send failures with No active WhatsApp Web listener despite status showing connected

### Expected behavior

After any restart or reconnect cycle, outbound WhatsApp sends should work normally
If the listener is unavailable, channels status --probe should reflect that — connected=false
The gateway should self-recover automatically without manual intervention

### Actual behavior

Outbound sends fail with No active WhatsApp Web listener (account: default) after 3 attempts
channels status --probe continues to report linked/running/connected — false positive
The gateway never self-recovers — manual full restart is required every time
The notifier backlog grows as failed messages are retried every 10 minutes, generating repeated warnings

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

Working on a fix for this. Will have a PR up shortly.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

> Working on a fix for this. Will have a PR up shortly.
> 
> — [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

Thanks man, waiting. <3

Fix tracked in #34783. Root cause was two bugs: (1) a stale `monitorWebChannel` instance's `closeListener` would unconditionally wipe the shared listener map even when a newer instance had already registered its listener, leaving `status=connected` with an empty map; and (2) the watchdog skipped all recovery logic when `lastMessageAt` was null, so the desync went undetected indefinitely. Both are fixed, plus `setActiveWebListener` now logs a warning+stack whenever a listener is removed from the map.

Root Cause & Fix: WhatsApp No active listener desync — bundler module duplication (v2026.3.2)

Summary

After deeper investigation following the initial report in #34741, the true root cause is not the watchdog or the closeListener identity guard alone — it is a bundler module duplication issue that creates 4 separate listeners Maps in memory, causing getActiveWebListener() to always return null on the wrong chunk.

What Was Actually Happening

The bundler (Vite/Rollup) creates 4 separate compiled chunks that each import their own copy of active-listener.ts:

Chunk | Imports from -- | -- channel-web-sl83aqDv.js | active-listener-DqWnxP4n.js web-CSq0l9pG.js | active-listener-12pjy02v.js outbound--SQSL_Cf.js | active-listener-*.js (separate) subagent-registry-*.js | active-listener-*.js (separate)

Each of these active-listener-*.js files has its own isolated listeners Map:

js
// active-listener-DqWnxP4n.js
const listeners = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();

// active-listener-12pjy02v.js  
const listeners = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();

// ... and so on for all 4

These are 4 separate Map instances in memory, not the same one.

The failure chain

  1. health-monitor restarts the WhatsApp channel
  2. On restart, the runtime switches internal path (e.g. channel-web-sl83aqDv.js → web-CSq0l9pG.js)
  3. The new path registers the listener in its own Map (e.g. active-listener-12pjy02v.js)
  4. The send path (e.g. outbound--SQSL_Cf.js) calls getActiveWebListener() from a different Map (e.g. active-listener-DqWnxP4n.js)
  5. That Map is empty → throws No active WhatsApp Web listener
  6. channels status --probe still reports connected: true because status is tracked separately

This explains why:

  • The error only appears after ~30 minutes (first health-monitor check cycle)
  • A full gateway restart temporarily fixes it (resets which path is active)
  • The error returns reliably after the next health-monitor restart

Previous Fixes Were Incomplete

The fixes in PR #34783 (closeListener identity guard, watchdog map check, registration ordering) are correct and necessary but only fix the logic within a single chunk. They do not solve the cross-chunk Map isolation problem — because each chunk patches its own Map, not the one that the send path reads from.

The Actual Fix: Shared Singleton via globalThis

Since the chunks are ESM and cannot share module state through normal imports, the fix is a globalThis singleton that all chunks read from:

1. Create dist/active-listener-singleton.mjs:

js
if (!globalThis.__activeWebListeners) {
  globalThis.__activeWebListeners = new Map();
}
export default globalThis.__activeWebListeners;

2. In all 4 active-listener-*.js files, replace:

js
// BEFORE
const listeners = /* @__PURE__ */ new Map();
js
// AFTER
import singleton from './active-listener-singleton.mjs';
const listeners = singleton;

This ensures that regardless of which chunk sets the listener and which chunk reads it, they all access the same Map in process memory.

Validation

After applying the fix:

  • Listener set by health-monitor in channel-web-sl83aqDv.js ✅ accessible from outbound--SQSL_Cf.js
  • Send succeeds without No active WhatsApp Web listener after health-monitor restart cycles ✅
  • Status correctly reflects actual listener state ✅

Proper Long-Term Fix (Upstream)

The correct fix at the source level is to configure the bundler to deduplicate active-listener.ts into a single shared chunk, so it is only instantiated once at runtime. The globalThis singleton is a reliable workaround but will be overwritten on every OpenClaw update until the bundler config is fixed.

A re-patch script is needed for anyone applying this manually until the upstream fix lands.

Environment

  • Version: 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
  • Platform: Linux VPS
  • Trigger: health-monitor restart with stale-socket detection (~every 30 min)

### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

### Environment
- **Gateway:** WSL2 Ubuntu, OpenClaw v2026.3.2
- **Node:** Windows 10 headless node host, OpenClaw v2026.3.2
- **Node status:** Connected, paired, capabilities: `browser`, `system`
- **Commands:** `browser.proxy`, `system.run`, `system.which`
- **Exec approvals (both sides):** `security=full`, `ask=off`, `askFallback=full`
- **Workspace:** `~/.openclaw/workspace` exists and is accessible

### Gateway exec config
json
{
  "tools.exec.host": "node",
  "tools.exec.security": "full",
  "tools.exec.ask": "off",
  "tools.exec.node": "MY-WINDOWS-NODE"
}

What Works (CLI) ✅


openclaw nodes run --node MY-WINDOWS-NODE -- powershell -Command "echo hello"
# → returns "hello"

openclaw nodes status
# → node connected with capabilities

openclaw approvals get --node MY-WINDOWS-NODE
# → security=full, ask=off

What's Broken (Agent Tools) ❌

`**nodes**`
• Call: `{"action":"run","node":"MY-WINDOWS-NODE","command":["powershell","-Command","Get-Date"]}`
• Error: `INVALID_REQUEST: rawCommand does not match command`

**`exec` (node)**
• Call: `{"command":"powershell...","host":"node"}`
• Error: `SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval requires an existing canonical cwd`

**`exec` (gateway)**
• Call: `{"command":"pwd"}` (no host)
• Error: `approval requires an existing canonical cwd`

**Impact:** Even `pwd` fails with the cwd error — the `exec` tool is completely non-functional for this agent.

Additional Issues Found During v2026.3.2 Upgrade

1. **Bare shell builtins fail on Windows node**
  • Commands like `echo`, `dir` fail with `"stable executable path"`
  • Must use `powershell -Command "..."` or `cmd.exe /c "..."`
  • May be intentional but undocumented
2. **WSL→Windows node connection now requires env var**
  • `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_INSECURE_PRIVATE_WS=1` required for WSL→Windows node connection
  • New security check blocks plaintext `ws://` to non-localhost
3. **mem0 plugin broken**
  • `node_sqlite3.node` bindings not found after update
  • jiti path resolution issue



### Steps to reproduce

1. Install/update OpenClaw to v2026.3.2 on both WSL gateway and Windows node
2. Configure Windows node as headless host (openclaw node run --host <gateway-ip> --port 18790)
3. Verify node connects: openclaw nodes status → shows connected ✅
4. Try agent tool execution:
  • nodes tool: {"action":"run","node":"MY-WINDOWS-NODE","command":["powershell","-Command","Get-Date"]}
  • exec tool: {"command":"powershell -Command \"Get-Date\"","host":"node"}
  • exec tool (gateway): {"command":"pwd"}

### Expected behavior

* nodes tool should execute PowerShell command on Windows node and return output
* exec tool with host: "node" should route to Windows node and execute
* exec tool on gateway should execute local WSL commands

### Actual behavior

**nodes**
* Call: {"action":"run","node":"MY-WINDOWS-NODE","command":["powershell","-Command","Get-Date"]}
* Error: INVALID_REQUEST: rawCommand does not match command

exec (node)
* Call: {"command":"powershell...","host":"node"}
* Error: SYSTEM_RUN_DENIED: approval requires an existing canonical cwd

exec (gateway)
* Call: {"command":"pwd"} (no host)
* Error: approval requires an existing canonical cwd

### OpenClaw version

v2026.3.2

### Operating system

* WSL2 Ubuntu (gateway) * Windows 10 (node host)

### Install method

npm install -g openclaw (both sides)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```

### Impact and severity

Severity

**🔴 Critical** — Agent cannot execute ANY commands. Completely blocks all exec-dependent workflows including:

• Browser automation
• File operations
• System commands
• Node-based operations

comments.body:
This is a critical regression! The exec tool being completely broken is a severe impact.

**Debugging suggestions:**

1. **For "canonical cwd" error** - This seems like a session state issue where the working directory isn't being initialized properly. Check if there's a regression in how sessions initialize the cwd.

2. **For "rawCommand does not match command"** - This might be related to command sanitization/parsing. Compare how the command is processed in the nodes tool vs CLI.

3. **Check for recent changes** - Look at commits around v2026.3.2 that touched:
   -  or similar
   - Session initialization code
   - Command approval/validation logic

4. **Quick diagnostic** - Try adding explicit cwd in config:
   ```json
   {
     "tools.exec": {
       "cwd": "/tmp"
     }
   }
   ```

Would love to help debug this further - any relevant error logs from the gateway would help!

This is a critical regression that completely breaks the exec tool. Thanks for the detailed report!

**Analysis of the three errors:**

1. **"rawCommand does not match command"** - This suggests the command validation is now stricter. Likely a security improvement in v2026.3.2 that's comparing rawCommand vs command and failing.

2. **"approval requires an existing canonical cwd"** - This is the main blocker. The exec approval system now requires a valid current working directory. This might have been introduced with the sandboxing changes.

**Suggested fixes to try:**

1. **For the cwd issue** - Try setting an explicit working directory:
   

2. **Check the approval config** - The error might be from missing or incorrect approval:
   

3. **Debug the rawCommand mismatch** - Check if there's a new validation in the nodes tool that's comparing the raw command string.

**Additional context for others hitting this:**
- WSL→Windows node now needs  (security improvement)
- Bare shell builtins on Windows need explicit shell: 

Is this on a clean install or after upgrade? If upgrade, check if any config keys changed.

body: ### Bug type Regression (worked before, now fails) ### Summary After updating API keys in models.json and restarting the gateway, both the dashboard and Telegram bot start throwing HTTP 401 authentication_error: invalid api key every time I send any message. Dashboard: Page loads normally after refresh, but sending any message (even "hi") immediately shows red error bubble: HTTP 401 authentication_error: invalid api key (request_id: xxx) Telegram bot: Same 401 error on every message sent to the bot. Refreshing the dashboard temporarily makes it look connected, but the next message fails again with 401. curl http://127.0.0.1:18789/ returns the HTML page normally → gateway is running and responding to basic HTTP. Logs (gateway.log) show normal websocket connect/disconnect, no 401 or auth error logged. This started after modifying models.json (updated MiniMax key + changed baseUrl to domestic endpoint) and running openclaw gateway install --force to sync token. ### Steps to reproduce Install OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (fresh or updated) Configure MiniMax (Coding Plan, domestic endpoint https://api.minimaxi.com/anthropic), Moonshot, etc. via models.json or onboarding. Run openclaw gateway restart or openclaw gateway install --force Open dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:18789/ Send any message in the chat (e.g. "hi") → Immediate red error: HTTP 401 authentication_error: invalid api key (request_id: ...) Same behavior in Telegram: send message to bot → bot replies with the same 401 error bubble. Refresh dashboard page → looks connected briefly, but next message fails again with 401. ### Expected behavior Sending messages in dashboard or Telegram should succeed without 401 errors after gateway restart and key update. ### Actual behavior Every message send (dashboard or Telegram) triggers HTTP 401 authentication_error: invalid api key, despite: curl to gateway succeeding No 401 in gateway.log Token synced via gateway install --force Cleared browser localStorage / IndexedDB / cookies ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 (85377a2) ### Operating system macOS (MacBook Air) ### Install method _No response_ ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

comments.body:
Working on a fix for this. Will have a PR up shortly.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

Fix tracked in #34740. Root cause: `mergeWithExistingProviderSecrets` only preserved `apiKey` and `baseUrl` from `models.json` during regeneration — endpoint-protocol fields (`api`, `authHeader`, `headers`) were overwritten by the built-in catalog on every gateway restart, which dropped `authHeader: true` and switched back to `openai-completions` when users had manually set `anthropic-messages` for the domestic MiniMax endpoint.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

comments.body:
看你描述的现象（`cron status` 里 `nextWakeAtMs=null` 且任务从不触发），通常只有两类原因：

1) **Cron 调度器被禁用**（job 还会被保存，但不会自动跑）
2) **没有任何“启用且 nextRunAtMs 有效”的 job**（例如 job 被 disable、或 job 记录里 nextRunAtMs 缺失/无效）

你这边可以先用下面两步把原因钉死：

### 1) 先确认 cron 是否真的 enabled
在 Gateway 主机上执行：
```bash
openclaw cron status
openclaw config get cron.enabled
```
- 如果 `enabled=false`：把配置里加上/改成 `cron.enabled: true`（或移除 `cron.enabled: false`），然后重启 gateway。

### 2) 再确认是否存在可调度的 job
```bash
openclaw cron list --include-disabled --json
```
重点看：
- 是否至少有 1 个 `enabled: true`
- 该 job 的 `state.nextRunAtMs` 是否是一个未来时间戳（不是 `null`/缺失/NaN）

如果 list 里 job 都是 `enabled=false` 或 `nextRunAtMs` 缺失，最直接的修复方式是：
- 先用 `openclaw cron edit <jobId> --enable` 重新启用（或重新创建该 job），然后再看 `openclaw cron status` 是否出现一个未来的 `nextWakeAtMs`。

如果你愿意贴一下下面两段输出（不用全量日志），我可以更精确地告诉你是哪一种：
- `openclaw cron status --json`
- `openclaw cron list --include-disabled --json`

推荐延伸阅读：
- [Cron: jobs don't fire and nextRunAtMs silently advances](https://coclaw.com/troubleshooting/solutions/cron-jobs-not-firing-next-run-advances/)
- [OpenClaw Config Generator](https://coclaw.com/openclaw-config-generator/)

### Bug type

### Summary

Gateway correctly identifies control-ui connections but inbound metadata defaults channel to Slack.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open web dashboard (control-ui/webchat client)
2. Send a message to the agent
3. Check agent inbound metadata - sender_id will be correct but channel will be "slack"
4. Agent attempts to route response via message tool
5. Response goes to Slack instead of web dashboard

### Expected behavior

Control-UI messages should have channel: "web" or channel: "control-ui" in inbound metadata, matching the actual client source. Response is routed back to the web dashboard.

### Actual behavior

Inbound metadata contains channel: "slack" even though sender_id is "openclaw-control-ui" and gateway logs show client=openclaw-control-ui. Response is routed to Slack instead of web dashboard.

2026.3.2

Linux 6.17.0-14-generic (Proxmox VM)

npm global

**Gateway logs show correct client identification:**

webchat connected conn=85a3746f-c877-4556-8cfc-f2852847113c remote=192.168.1.5 client=openclaw-control-ui webchat vdev


**Inbound metadata received by agent (WRONG):**

{
  "sender_id": "openclaw-control-ui",
  "sender": "openclaw-control-ui",
  "channel": "slack"  // ← BUG: should be "web" or "control-ui"
}


**Impact:** Agent message tool routes based on the `channel` field, not `sender_id`, so responses go to Slack instead of web dashboard.
```

## Confirming: Signal also affected

Same regression observed with Signal channel instead of Slack.

**Environment:**
- Version: 2026.2.24
- OS: Ubuntu (ai-server)
- Install: npm global
- Config: Signal channel enabled with single `allowFrom` entry

**Evidence:**

Session metadata from `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`:

```json
"agent:main:main": {
  "deliveryContext": {
    "channel": "signal",
    "to": "+15717666717"
  },
  "origin": {
    "provider": "signal",
    "surface": "webchat"
  }
}
```

**Symptoms:**
- Control UI messages correctly identified as `surface: "webchat"`
- But `deliveryContext.channel` set to `"signal"`
- Responses route to Signal phone number instead of webchat
- TTS fires for webchat messages (inheriting Signal channel config)
- Audio filenames appear as text in webchat UI

**Gateway logs confirm:**
- Inbound: `messageChannel=signal` for webchat-originated messages
- All messages share session key `agent:main:main`

Same root cause as Slack variant — control UI lacks independent channel identity.

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Title: LINE webhook route intermittently returns 404 until gateway restart (2026.3.2)

Summary: On macOS LaunchAgent gateway (loopback:18789), LINE channel shows running/configured, but GET /line/webhook intermittently returns 404. LINE webhooks via ngrok then get 404 and no sessions are created. openclaw gateway restart immediately restores /line/webhook to 200.
---

### Steps to reproduce

[ ] openclaw gateway restart
[ ] sleep 2 && curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18789/line/webhook

### Expected behavior

/line/webhook should remain reachable while LINE channel is running.

### Actual behavior

Body (copy/paste):
Version: OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2)
OS: macOS (LaunchAgent service: ai.openclaw.gateway.plist)
Gateway: bind loopback, port 18789
Channel: LINE plugin (line, accountId=default), running/configured=true
Symptom: /line/webhook intermittently returns 404:
curl -i http://127.0.0.1:18789/line/webhook → 404 Not Found
At the same time, openclaw channels status --json shows line running/configured true (no lastError)
openclaw channels logs --channel line --lines 200 shows repeated [default] starting LINE provider (Steve hair) with no clear channel exited / auto-restart attempt
When this happens, LINE webhooks via ngrok receive POST /line/webhook 404
Workaround: openclaw gateway restart immediately restores GET /line/webhook → 200 OK (and LINE works again)
Expected: /line/webhook should remain reachable while LINE channel is running.

### OpenClaw version

ersion: OpenClaw 2026.3.2 (85377a2)

### Operating system

macOS

### Install method

_No response_

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Working on this in #34646.

Root cause: `createGatewayPluginRequestHandler` pins the plugin registry at gateway startup. After any plugin config change, `loadOpenClawPlugins` is called with a different cache key → `setActivePluginRegistry` replaces the active registry with a fresh object. The LINE channel re-registers `/line/webhook` on the new registry, but the HTTP handler still reads routes from the original stale registry — returning 404 until a full gateway restart.

Fix: pass `requireActivePluginRegistry` as a live callback instead of capturing the registry at handler-creation time, so routes are always resolved against the current active registry.

— [Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) · [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)

title: [Bug]: Title: LINE webhook route intermittently returns 404 until gateway restart (2026.3.2)

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Agent 在执行需要重启 Gateway 的操作后存在三个问题，影响用户体验和系统稳定性。

### Steps to reproduce

问题 1: Gateway 更新后重启仍是旧版本
1. 通过对话让 Agent 执行 `npm update -g openclaw`
2. Agent 执行 `systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway.service`
3. 检查版本发现仍是旧版本

问题 2: Agent 不主动通知重启完成
1. Agent 执行需要重启 Gateway 的操作
2. Gateway 重启成功
3. Agent 没有主动告知用户"重启完成"

问题 3: 浏览器标签不关闭导致内存泄漏
1. Agent 使用 CDP 模式打开浏览器访问网页
2. 完成任务后 Agent 没有关闭标签页
3. 随着时间推移，打开的标签越来越多，最终导致虚拟机内存耗尽崩溃

### Expected behavior

问题 1: 更新后应确保 Gateway 运行的是新版本代码，可能需要在重启前执行 daemon-reload 或清除 Node.js 缓存。

问题 2: Agent 应在执行完重启操作后，主动向用户发送确认消息（如"Gateway 已重启完成"）。

问题 3: Agent 在使用浏览器完成任务后应主动关闭不再需要的标签页或浏览器实例，避免内存泄漏。

### Actual behavior

问题 1: 更新后重启仍是旧版本，systemd 服务文件虽然指向 node_modules，但 Node.js 可能缓存了旧模块，且没有执行 daemon-reload。

问题 2: Agent 重启 Gateway 后直接结束操作，没有向用户确认重启结果。

问题 3: 浏览器标签持续累积，最终导致虚拟机内存耗尽卡死崩溃。

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04 (Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x64)

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

**问题 1 影响**:
- Affected: 所有通过 Agent 更新 OpenClaw 的用户
- Severity: 中等（更新后功能未生效）
- Frequency: 每次更新都会发生
- Consequence: 用户以为更新了，实际仍在用旧版本

**问题 2 影响**:
- Affected: 所有执行需要重启操作的对话
- Severity: 低（不影响功能）
- Frequency: 每次重启都会发生
- Consequence: 用户不知道操作是否完成，需要主动询问

**问题 3 影响**:
- Affected: 长期运行的 Agent 实例（特别是虚拟机环境）
- Severity: 高（系统崩溃）
- Frequency: 持续累积，最终必现
- Consequence: 虚拟机内存耗尽崩溃，需要重启

### Additional information

_No response_
title: [Bug]: Gateway更新后仍是旧版本 + Agent不通知重启完成 + 浏览器标签不关闭导致内存泄漏

### Bug type

### Summary

   # OpenClaw TUI Bug Report

## Issue: TUI messages not reaching agent / replies going to wrong channel

comments.body: Hi, I am looking into this issue, will update here with my findings

Thanks for the detailed report! This sounds like a regression in how the TUI connects to sessions.

**Debugging suggestions:**

1. **Check the TUI connection** - Run with verbose logging:
```
openclaw tui --deliver --verbose
```

2. **Check gateway logs for TUI connection:**
```
openclaw gateway logs --follow | grep -i tui
```

3. **Verify the session key** - The TUI might be connecting to a different session than WhatsApp. Check what session keys are active.

4. **Test with explicit session:**
```
openclaw tui --session agent:main:main --deliver
```

5. **Check the `--deliver` flag behavior** - There might have been a change in how message routing works when this flag is used.

6. **Compare with working version** - You mentioned this worked before. Can you identify which version worked?

**Possible causes:**
- Session affinity changed - TUI and WhatsApp might be using different sessions now
- The `--deliver` flag behavior changed
- Message routing logic regression for the TUI channel

Would you like help debugging further?

## Fixed

**Root cause**: The main session special handling added in commit 7f2708a8c prevented internal channels (TUI/WebChat) from updating `lastChannel`/`lastTo`, causing replies to use stale external routes.

**Fix**: Modified `resolveLastChannelRaw`/`resolveLastToRaw` to allow internal channels to update routing fields for main sessions, preventing replies from leaking to stale external channels.

**PR**: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/34603

**Verification**: All 55 related tests pass.

**How to test**:
```bash
openclaw tui --deliver
# Send a message in TUI
# Verify the reply appears in TUI, not WhatsApp
```

This sounds like a regression with the TUI channel delivery. A few debugging suggestions:

1. **Check the TUI channel registration** - The error `unknown channel id: terminal` suggests the TUI channel isn't being registered properly. Check if `channels.tui` or similar config exists.

2. **Verify --deliver flag** - The `--deliver` flag should enable bidirectional messaging. Try:
   `openclaw tui --deliver --url ws://127.0.0.1:18789 --token <token>`

3. **Check session binding** - When using `--deliver`, the session should be bound to the TUI channel. You can verify with `openclaw sessions list` to see what channel each session is bound to.

4. **Logs debugging** - Run gateway with debug logging to see what happens when TUI connects:
   `openclaw gateway logs --level debug`
   
   Then watch for messages like `tui connected` or `channel bound`.

5. **Quick test** - Try sending via the Control UI webchat instead of TUI to see if it works there.

This is a regression since it worked before. Could you share what changed between when it worked and now (update, config changes, etc)?

body: ### Bug type Regression (worked before, now fails) ### Summary Environment - OpenClaw Version: 2026.3.2 - Model: minimax-cn/MiniMax-M2.5 - Deployment: Docker (openclaw-docker-cn-im) - Client: openclaw-tui Description When using openclaw tui to interact with the agent, responses generated by the model are sometimes not displayed in the TUI interface, even though the model successfully generates a reply. Steps to Reproduce 1. Start OpenClaw container: docker run -d ... openclaw-gateway 2. Connect via TUI: openclaw tui 3. Send a message that triggers thinking (e.g., a complex prompt) 4. Observe that the response is not displayed in TUI Root Cause The Gateway relies on the presence of tags in the model's output to determine whether to send stream=assistant events to the TUI client. When the model outputs text without the tag (which happens when thinking is enabled and the model generates thinking content), the Gateway skips sending the stream=assistant event entirely. Evidence From session log (8a5909b9-8993-486a-b92c-3b659775e1af.jsonl): Working case (first message "你好"): {"type":"text","text":"你好！有什么我可以帮你的吗？"} - Gateway sent: seq=9 stream=assistant text=你好！有什么我可以帮你的吗？ - TUI: ✅ Displayed correctly Broken case (second message with roleplay prompt): {"type":"text","text":"记住了～不过我要诚实告诉你：我是个AI助手..."} - Gateway sent: NO stream=assistant event - TUI: ❌ Not displayed Suggested Fix The Gateway should handle responses without tags by: 1. Treating all text content (regardless of tag) as valid assistant output 2. Or ensuring the model always outputs proper tags ### Steps to reproduce just talk to an agent, then response will not show. ### Expected behavior ### Actual behavior ### OpenClaw version 2026.3.2 ### Operating system macOS 26.3 ### Install method docker ### Logs, screenshots, and evidence ```shell ``` ### Impact and severity _No response_ ### Additional information _No response_

body:
### Bug type

Regression (worked before, now fails)

### Summary

Yeah, that's exactly what the logs show. Here's the smoking gun from
 yesterday:

 ```
   21:30:17 — config overwrite detected (openclaw.json updated)
   21:30:17 — hot reload applied
   21:30:46 — npm update completed: 2026.3.1 → 2026.3.2
   21:31:04 — SIGTERM received → "received SIGTERM; shutting down"
 ```

 The updater runs npm i -g openclaw@latest, then sends SIGTERM to the running
 gateway to hand off to the new binary. That part is intentional. The bug is
 what comes after — it should call launchctl bootstrap to reload the
 LaunchAgent with the new version, but it's not doing that. So the gateway
 dies and stays dead.

 The plist even has the new version baked in (OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION =
 2026.3.2), which means the install step ran... but the bootstrap step either
 failed silently or never happened.

 Worth posting in the OpenClaw Discord — this is a pretty clear reproducible
 bug: auto-update + launchd = gateway orphaned after every upgrade.

### Steps to reproduce

let the autoupdater run

### Expected behavior

updater just works

### Actual behavior

updater corrupted daemon - gateway did not restart. 

### OpenClaw version

???

### Operating system

macOS

### Install method

cannot remember

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
Yeah, that's exactly what the logs show. Here's the smoking gun from
 yesterday:

 
   21:30:17 — config overwrite detected (openclaw.json updated)
   21:30:17 — hot reload applied
   21:30:46 — npm update completed: 2026.3.1 → 2026.3.2
   21:31:04 — SIGTERM received → "received SIGTERM; shutting down"
 

 The updater runs npm i -g openclaw@latest, then sends SIGTERM to the running
 gateway to hand off to the new binary. That part is intentional. The bug is
 what comes after — it should call launchctl bootstrap to reload the
 LaunchAgent with the new version, but it's not doing that. So the gateway
 dies and stays dead.

 The plist even has the new version baked in (OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION =
 2026.3.2), which means the install step ran... but the bootstrap step either
 failed silently or never happened.

 Worth posting in the OpenClaw Discord — this is a pretty clear reproducible
 bug: auto-update + launchd = gateway orphaned after every upgrade.
```

comments.body: Hi, I am looking into this issue, will update here with my findings

I reviewed current `main` and wanted to share what I found.

  What I could verify:

  - The updater path does include a macOS launchd bootstrap fallback:
    - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:96` (`kickstart` -> `bootstrap` -> `kickstart`)
    - `src/infra/restart.ts:342` (same fallback pattern)
  - Auto-update invokes `openclaw update --yes --channel ... --json`:
    - `src/infra/update-startup.ts:236`

  So the specific claim “bootstrap is never called” does not appear to match current code.

  That said, the symptom “gateway can end up down after update” still looks plausible. The restart script currently suppresses launchctl errors (`2>/dev/null`, trailing `|| true`), and JSON-mode update
  flow is quiet, so restart failures can be hard to see:
  - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:98`
  - `src/cli/update-cli/update-command.ts:519`

  Could you share a bit more detail so maintainers can reproduce quickly?

  1. Exact OpenClaw version before and after update (for example `2026.3.1 -> 2026.3.2`)
  2. Install method (`npm -g`, `pnpm -g`, git checkout, mac app)
  3. Whether this happened via auto-update or manual `openclaw update`
  4. Output of:
     - `openclaw gateway status --deep`
     - `launchctl print gui/$UID/ai.openclaw.gateway`
     - `tail -n 200 ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`
  5. Whether a profile is in use (`OPENCLAW_PROFILE`) and, if so, expected label (`ai.openclaw.<profile>`)

  If you’re blocked meanwhile, this may recover the service:

  ```bash
  openclaw gateway install --force
  openclaw gateway restart
  openclaw gateway status --deep

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Web search key presence audit is incomplete: hasWebSearchKey only check Perplexity and Brave, missing Gemini, Grok, and Kimi keys

### Steps to reproduce

1. `Configure only one of these keys: tools.web.search.gemini.apiKey, tools.web.search.grok.apiKey, or tools.web.search.kimi.apiKey.`
2. `Run the audit path that uses hasWebSearchKey (security audit extras).`
3. `Observe that the key is not recognized as present.`

### Expected behavior

`hasWebSearchKey` should return true when any configured web search provider key is present (Brave, Perplexity, Gemini, Grok, or Kimi).

### Actual behavior

`hasWebSearchKey` only accounts for Brave + Perplexity, so Gemini/Grok/Kimi-only configurations are treated as if no web search key exists.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Debian 12

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

`src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts` shows this is the case

### Impact and severity

Affected: users relying on Gemini/Grok/Kimi for web search + maintainers depending on audit signals
Severity: Medium (audit false negatives)
Frequency: 100% for configs using only missing providers
Consequence: incorrect audit output

### Additional information

I do personally find it quite odd how the options outside of Perplexity and Brave exist, given that both of them handle/produce structured JSON data, while these others are grounded summaries. I believe they should be treated as skills or MCPs instead of web search, but that is a call for the maintainers to make, not me

comments.body:
I've submitted a PR that fixes this issue: #34560

The fix adds the missing checks for Gemini, Grok, and Kimi API keys to the `hasWebSearchKey` function in `src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts`.

Please review when you have a chance!
Submitted a fix in PR #34587. Added web search key paths to the doctor audit collector so Brave/Perplexity/Gemini/Grok/Kimi credentials are included in the secret audit surface.
#34515's generic scanning approach (Object.entries for any apiKey) is more future-proof than the hardcoded lists IMO

# Root Cause Analysis: Issue #34509

⚠️ **Verification Method**: Code review verification  
✅ **Code location confirmed**: Found exact function in OpenClaw dist files  
❌ **Not reproduced**: Unable to test (would require configuring Gemini/Grok/Kimi API keys)

---

## Problem Summary

The `hasWebSearchKey()` function only checks for Brave and Perplexity API keys, missing Gemini, Grok, and Kimi providers. This causes security audit false negatives for users who configure only these newer providers.

**Impact**: Audit incorrectly reports "no web search key" when Gemini/Grok/Kimi keys are configured.

---

## Code Location Verified

**File**: `/usr/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/audit-Cu_xW7y4.js`  
**Function**: `hasWebSearchKey(cfg, env)`

### Current Implementation (Incomplete)

```javascript
function hasWebSearchKey(cfg, env) {
	const search = cfg.tools?.web?.search;
	return Boolean(
		search?.apiKey || 
		search?.perplexity?.apiKey || 
		env.BRAVE_API_KEY || 
		env.PERPLEXITY_API_KEY || 
		env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY
	);
}
```

**Missing checks**:
- ❌ `search?.gemini?.apiKey`
- ❌ `search?.grok?.apiKey`
- ❌ `search?.kimi?.apiKey`
- ❌ Corresponding env vars (if they exist)

---

## Why This Causes the Bug

### User's Configuration (Example)
```json5
{
  tools: {
    web: {
      search: {
        gemini: {
          apiKey: "..."  // ← Configured
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Current Behavior
```javascript
hasWebSearchKey(cfg, env)
// → Checks: search?.apiKey || search?.perplexity?.apiKey
// → Result: false ❌ (Gemini key is ignored)
```

### Expected Behavior
```javascript
hasWebSearchKey(cfg, env)
// → Should also check: search?.gemini?.apiKey
// → Result: true ✅
```

---

## Suggested Fix

Add missing provider checks to `hasWebSearchKey()`:

```javascript
function hasWebSearchKey(cfg, env) {
	const search = cfg.tools?.web?.search;
	return Boolean(
		search?.apiKey || 
		search?.perplexity?.apiKey || 
		search?.gemini?.apiKey ||      // ✅ Add Gemini
		search?.grok?.apiKey ||         // ✅ Add Grok
		search?.kimi?.apiKey ||         // ✅ Add Kimi
		env.BRAVE_API_KEY || 
		env.PERPLEXITY_API_KEY || 
		env.OPENROUTER_API_KEY
		// Add env vars if they exist (GEMINI_API_KEY, etc.)
	);
}
```

**Change required**: +3 lines (or more if env vars exist)

---

## Verification Steps (If Reproducible)

### Setup
```json5
// Configure ONLY Gemini web search
{
  tools: {
    web: {
      search: {
        gemini: { apiKey: "test-key-123" }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Before Fix
```bash
# Run security audit
openclaw doctor

# Expected: Audit shows "no web search key" ❌
```

### After Fix
```bash
# Apply the fix
# Restart gateway
openclaw doctor

# Expected: Audit correctly detects Gemini key ✅
```

---

## Impact

**Severity**: Medium  
**Type**: Audit false negative

**Affected users**:
- Anyone using Gemini/Grok/Kimi as their web search provider
- Maintainers relying on audit signals for security checks

**Frequency**: 100% for configs using only missing providers

**Workaround**: Also configure Brave or Perplexity key (undesirable)

**Benefits of fix**:
- ✅ Accurate audit reporting
- ✅ Proper detection of all web search providers
- ✅ Consistent behavior across all supported providers

---

## Additional Context

**User's observation**:
> "I do personally find it quite odd how the options outside of Perplexity and Brave exist, given that both of them handle/produce structured JSON data, while these others are grounded summaries."

This suggests Gemini/Grok/Kimi may have different capabilities than Brave/Perplexity. However, since they're already implemented as `tools.web.search.*` providers in the config schema, the audit should recognize them.

**Related functions**:
- `isWebSearchEnabled(cfg, env)` depends on `hasWebSearchKey()`
- Security audit extras call `hasWebSearchKey()` for key presence checks

---

## Evidence Summary

| Criterion | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| User report detail | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Excellent (exact location + impact) |
| Code location found | ✅ audit-Cu_xW7y4.js, hasWebSearchKey() |
| Logic matches bug | ✅ Missing Gemini/Grok/Kimi checks |
| Fix straightforward | ✅ Add 3 lines to boolean check |

---

**Environment**:
- OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
- OS: Debian 12
- Install: npm global

---

**Verification Limitations**:
- ⚠️ Not tested in live environment (requires Gemini/Grok/Kimi API keys)
- ✅ Code logic confirmed in dist files
- ✅ User report matches code exactly (missing provider checks)

---

*Analysis based on code review. User identified exact source location. Code in dist confirms missing provider checks in hasWebSearchKey().*

Submitted a fix in PR #35726. Added Gemini (`search.gemini.apiKey` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`), Grok (`search.grok.apiKey` / `XAI_API_KEY`), and Kimi (`search.kimi.apiKey` / `KIMI_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`) to the `hasWebSearchKey` audit check.

@Sid-Qin Thanks for the quick fix in PR #35726! I verified the same issue in the code and your solution looks correct.

@kesku's point about generic scanning is valid — a future-proof approach would be to automatically detect any `*.apiKey` fields instead of maintaining hardcoded lists. But the explicit approach in #35726 works well for now and is easy to review. 👍

### Bug type

### Steps to reproduce


  1. Install OpenClaw 2026.3.2
  2. Configure MiniMax API key (sk-cp-01...)
  3. Set model to minimax/MiniMax-M2.5
  4. Run `openclaw agent --agent main --message "hello"`
  5. Get timeout error

  ### Environment

  - OpenClaw: 2026.3.2
  - macOS: Darwin 25.3.0 (arm64)
  - Node: v25.6.1
  - Model: minimax/MiniMax-M2.5

  1. `authHeader: true` - gives 401 error
  2. `authHeader: false` - times out
  3. Using `minimax` provider - times out
  4. Using `minimax-portal` provider - times out

  ### Additional information

  - Direct API call with curl works fine:

  curl -s https://api.minimaxi.com/anthropic/v1/messages \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer sk-cp-01..." \
    -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M2.5","max_tokens":10,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"hi"}]}'
  # Returns valid response

  - Related issues: #28458 (MiniMax 401 error - similar problem)

### Expected behavior

 MiniMax should respond without timeout

### Actual behavior

2026.3.2

comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report! This is a regression in 2026.3.2.

Since curl works but OpenClaw doesn't, this is likely a difference in how the request is being made. A few things to check:

1. **Check the actual endpoint being used** - OpenClaw might be hitting a different URL than curl. Check your config for `baseUrl` or any custom endpoint settings.

2. **Check for proxy/network issues** - OpenClaw might be using a different network configuration than curl. Try setting `HTTP_PROXY` and `HTTPS_PROXY` env vars.

3. **Check the model name** - The issue shows `minimax/MiniMax-M2.5` but the API call uses `MiniMax-M2.5`. Try:
   `minimax/MiniMax-Text-01`
   or
   `minimax/M2.5`

4. **Check if there were any changes to the MiniMax provider** between 2026.3.1 and 2026.3.2 - look at the changelog or git diff.

5. **Enable debug logging** - Set `logging.level: debug` to see the actual request being made.

Would you like me to help dig into the provider code to find what changed?

Thanks for the detailed report! This is a regression affecting multiple MiniMax users.

Since curl works but OpenClaw doesn't, this is likely an issue with how OpenClaw constructs the request. Here are some debugging steps:

**1. Check the endpoint URL:**
MiniMax changed their API endpoints recently. Verify what URL OpenClaw is actually calling:
- Add debug logging to see the full request URL
- Compare with the curl command that works

**2. Check request headers:**
The difference might be in headers. Try checking if:
- Content-Type header is correct
- User-Agent might be blocked
- Any required headers are missing

**3. Check for regional issues:**
MiniMax has different API portals for different regions. You mentioned trying `minimax-portal` - did you also try `minimax-pro` or `minimax-flash`?

**4. Timeout settings:**
The timeout might be too aggressive. Check if `timeoutMs` is set appropriately for MiniMax (they can be slower than other providers).

**5. Related issues:**
- #28458 (MiniMax 401 error) - similar problem was reported before
- Check if there's a recent change in how the MiniMax provider handles authentication

Would you like me to help investigate the MiniMax provider code to find what might have changed in 2026.3.2?

@openclaw bot I've investigated and fixed this issue. Here's a summary:

## Root Cause

The MiniMax provider was hardcoded to use the global endpoint `api.minimax.io/anthropic`. Your API key (`sk-cp-01...`) is for the CN region and requires the CN endpoint `api.minimaxi.com`.

When requests went to the wrong endpoint:
- `authHeader: true` → 401 error (CN key not valid for global endpoint)
- `authHeader: false` → timeout (endpoint waiting for auth)

## Fix

Added support for `MINIMAX_API_HOST` environment variable to the main MiniMax provider (previously only VLM endpoint supported it).

## Workaround (until fix is released)

Set the environment variable before running OpenClaw:

\`\`\`bash
export MINIMAX_API_HOST=https://api.minimaxi.com
openclaw agent --agent main --message "hello"
\`\`\`

## PR

Fix submitted: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/34524

To verify once merged, set the env var and run your command again.

labels.name: regression
labels.description: Behavior that previously worked and now fails

comments.body: Addressed in #34508

I suspect the core issue is Proxy Environment Isolation. Even though I successfully installed Chrome or Brave via the terminal by manually specifying the proxy, OpenClaw launches Playwright in a "clean" sandbox that does not inherit these system settings. Consequently, the browser attempts a direct connection instead of routing through my corporate proxy server, causing the firewall to immediately sever the connection (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET).
No error message appears in the log, but Openclaw tells me it cannot connect (ERR_CONNECTION_RESET).

[tools] browser failed: Error: page.goto: net::ERR_CONNECTION_RESET at https://www.google.com/
Call log:
  - navigating to "https://www.google.com/", waiting until "load"

body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Summary

Two related issues in message processing: (1) Agent sometimes doesn't respond until user sends another message, (2) Agent's reply gets interrupted and doesn't continue until user sends another message.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Set up OpenClaw with QQBot channel
2. Have a conversation with the agent
3. Observe that sometimes the agent doesn't respond immediately
4. Send another message to trigger the pending response
5. Also observe that multi-part replies sometimes get interrupted

### Expected behavior

Agent should respond immediately to each user message, and multi-part replies should complete without interruption.

### Actual behavior

(1) Agent doesn't respond to user message - no reply is sent until user sends another message. (2) Agent starts replying but stops mid-way - subsequent parts of the reply are not sent until user sends another message.

### OpenClaw version

2026.3.2

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04

### Install method

npm global

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell

```

### Impact and severity

Affected: All users using messaging channels (QQBot, Telegram, etc.)
Severity: High (blocks normal conversation flow)
Frequency: Intermittent but frequent
Consequence: Users must send multiple messages to get a single response, degrading user experience significantly

Root cause analysis:
1. In gateway.ts, if handleMessageFn throws an uncaught exception, messageProcessorRunning stays true, blocking subsequent messages
2. In reply-DhtejUNZ.js, after timeout, aborted=true causes subsequent enqueue calls to silently drop messages
3. Dispatcher's pending counter has race conditions that may trigger premature onIdle or incomplete state cleanup

### Additional information

Related to issue #34156 (XML tags leakage). Both issues affect message processing reliability.

Analysis report saved at: /home/tester501/.openclaw/workspace/shared/artifacts/message-processing-analysis.md

Key code locations:
- gateway.ts: startMessageProcessor function (lines ~300-350)
- reply-DhtejUNZ.js: createBlockReplyPipeline, createReplyDispatcher
title: [Bug]: Message processing stalls and reply interruption - messages not processed until next user message
